/Users/alex/Documents/acepe/packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/logic/inbound-request-handler.ts (lines 1-260)

<details>
<summary>260 lines</summary>

```
1. /**
2.  * Inbound Request Handler - Handles JSON-RPC requests FROM the ACP subprocess.
3.  *
4.  * The ACP subprocess can send requests TO the client (not just responses).
5.  * This path still matters for legacy/provider-specific JSON-RPC requests and
6.  * for reply routing, but cc-sdk permission flows now prefer session updates.
7.  *
8.  * This handler:
9.  * 1. Listens to `acp-inbound-request` events from the ACP event bridge stream
10.  * 2. Parses the JSON-RPC request
11.  * 3. Dispatches to the appropriate handler (e.g., permission store)
12.  * 4. Sends the JSON-RPC response back via the Tauri command
13.  */
14. 
15. import { okAsync, type Result, type ResultAsync } from "neverthrow";
16. import { ACP_INBOUND_METHODS } from "../constants/acp-methods.js";
17. import type { AcpError } from "../errors/index.js";
18. import { ProtocolError } from "../errors/index.js";
19. 
20. import {
21. 	ErrorResponseParamsSchema,
22. 	type JsonRpcRequest,
23. 	parseInboundRequest,
24. } from "../schemas/inbound-request.schema.js";
25. import { api } from "../store/api.js";
26. import type { PermissionRequest } from "../types/permission.js";
27. import type { QuestionRequest } from "../types/question.js";
28. import { createLogger } from "../utils/logger.js";
29. import { openAcpEventSource } from "./acp-event-bridge.js";
30. import {
31. 	type NormalizedInboundQuestionRequest,
32. 	normalizeInboundInteractionRequest,
33. 	toPermissionRequest,
34. 	toQuestionRequest,
35. } from "./inbound-request-normalization.js";
36. 
37. const logger = createLogger({
38. 	id: "inbound-request-handler",
39. 	name: "Inbound Request Handler",
40. });
41. 
42. /**
43.  * Callback type for permission request events.
44.  */
45. export type OnPermissionRequest = (permission: PermissionRequest) => void;
46. 
47. /**
48.  * Callback type for question request events (AskUserQuestion tool).
49.  */
50. export type OnQuestionRequest = (question: QuestionRequest) => void;
51. 
52. /**
53.  * Handler for inbound JSON-RPC requests from the ACP subprocess.
54.  */
55. export class InboundRequestHandler {
56. 	private unlistenFn: (() => void) | null = null;
57. 	private onPermissionRequest: OnPermissionRequest | null = null;
58. 	private onQuestionRequest: OnQuestionRequest | null = null;
59. 
60. 	/**
61. 	 * Start listening for inbound requests.
62. 	 *
63. 	 * @param onPermissionRequest - Callback for permission requests
64. 	 * @param onQuestionRequest - Callback for question requests (AskUserQuestion)
65. 	 */
66. 	start(
67. 		onPermissionRequest: OnPermissionRequest,
68. 		onQuestionRequest?: OnQuestionRequest
69. 	): ResultAsync<void, AcpError> {
70. 		logger.info("InboundRequestHandler.start() called");
71. 
72. 		if (this.unlistenFn !== null) {
73. 			logger.debug("Inbound request handler already running, skipping duplicate start");
74. 			this.onPermissionRequest = onPermissionRequest;
75. 			this.onQuestionRequest = onQuestionRequest !== undefined ? onQuestionRequest : null;
76. 			return okAsync(undefined);
77. 		}
78. 
79. 		this.onPermissionRequest = onPermissionRequest;
80. 		this.onQuestionRequest = onQuestionRequest !== undefined ? onQuestionRequest : null;
81. 
82. 		return openAcpEventSource((envelope) => {
83. 			if (envelope.eventName !== "acp-inbound-request") {
84. 				return;
85. 			}
86. 			this.handleEvent(envelope.payload);
87. 		})
88. 			.map((unlisten) => {
89. 				this.unlistenFn = unlisten;
90. 				logger.info("Inbound request handler started successfully");
91. 			})
92. 			.mapErr(
93. 				(error) => new ProtocolError(`Failed to listen for inbound requests: ${error}`, error)
94. 			);
95. 	}
96. 
97. 	/**
98. 	 * Stop listening for inbound requests.
99. 	 */
100. 	stop(): void {
101. 		if (this.unlistenFn) {
102. 			this.unlistenFn();
103. 			this.unlistenFn = null;
104. 			logger.info("Inbound request handler stopped");
105. 		}
106. 	}
107. 
108. 	/**
109. 	 * Handle a raw event payload.
110. 	 */
111. 	private handleEvent(payload: unknown): void {
112. 		logger.debug("handleEvent entry", {
113. 			payloadType: typeof payload,
114. 			payloadKeys: payload && typeof payload === "object" ? Object.keys(payload) : [],
115. 		});
116. 
117. 		const parseResult = this.parseRequest(payload);
118. 
119. 		if (parseResult.isErr()) {
120. 			logger.error("Failed to parse inbound request:", parseResult.error);
121. 			return;
122. 		}
123. 
124. 		const request = parseResult.value;
125. 		logger.debug("Received inbound request", { method: request.method, id: request.id });
126. 
127. 		switch (request.method) {
128. 			case ACP_INBOUND_METHODS.REQUEST_PERMISSION:
129. 				this.handlePermissionRequest(request);
130. 				break;
131. 			default:
132. 				logger.warn("Unknown inbound request method", { method: request.method });
133. 				// Send error response for unknown methods
134. 				this.sendErrorResponse(request, -32601, "Method not found");
135. 		}
136. 	}
137. 
138. 	/**
139. 	 * Parse a raw payload into a JSON-RPC request.
140. 	 */
141. 	private parseRequest(payload: unknown): Result<JsonRpcRequest, AcpError> {
142. 		return parseInboundRequest(payload);
143. 	}
144. 
145. 	/**
146. 	 * Handle a client/requestPermission request.
147. 	 *
148. 	 * This method first normalizes the transport-shaped request into the
149. 	 * canonical inbound interaction seam, then routes it as a permission or
150. 	 * question request.
151. 	 */
152. 	private handlePermissionRequest(request: JsonRpcRequest): void {
153. 		const normalizedResult = normalizeInboundInteractionRequest(request);
154. 		if (normalizedResult.isErr()) {
155. 			logger.error("Invalid requestPermission params", { error: normalizedResult.error });
156. 			this.sendErrorResponse(request, -32602, "Invalid params");
157. 			return;
158. 		}
159. 
160. 		const normalizedRequest = normalizedResult.value;
161. 		if (normalizedRequest.kind === "question" && this.onQuestionRequest) {
162. 			this.handleQuestionRequest(normalizedRequest);
163. 			return;
164. 		}
165. 
166. 		const permission = toPermissionRequest(normalizedRequest);
167. 
168. 		logger.debug("Created permission request from inbound request", {
169. 			permissionId: permission.id,
170. 			jsonRpcRequestId: normalizedRequest.jsonRpcRequestId,
171. 		});
172. 
173. 		// Dispatch to the registered callback
174. 		if (this.onPermissionRequest) {
175. 			this.onPermissionRequest(permission);
176. 		}
177. 	}
178. 
179. 	/**
180. 	 * Handle an AskUserQuestion request that was sent via requestPermission.
181. 	 *
182. 	 * The ACP agent's AskUserQuestion tool uses the requestPermission mechanism
183. 	 * with _meta.askUserQuestion containing the question data.
184. 	 */
185. 	private handleQuestionRequest(request: NormalizedInboundQuestionRequest): void {
186. 		const question: QuestionRequest = toQuestionRequest(request);
187. 
188. 		logger.debug("Created question request from inbound request (AskUserQuestion)", {
189. 			questionId: question.id,
190. 			jsonRpcRequestId: request.jsonRpcRequestId,
191. 			questionCount: question.questions.length,
192. 		});
193. 
194. 		// Dispatch to the question callback
195. 		if (this.onQuestionRequest) {
196. 			this.onQuestionRequest(question);
197. 		}
198. 	}
199. 
200. 	/**
201. 	 * Send an error response for a failed request.
202. 	 */
203. 	private sendErrorResponse(request: JsonRpcRequest, code: number, message: string): void {
204. 		// For errors, we need to find the sessionId from the params
205. 		const paramsResult = ErrorResponseParamsSchema.safeParse(request.params);
206. 		const sessionId = paramsResult.success ? paramsResult.data.sessionId : undefined;
207. 
208. 		if (!sessionId) {
209. 			logger.error("Cannot send error response: no sessionId", { requestId: request.id });
210. 			return;
211. 		}
212. 
213. 		const errorResult = {
214. 			error: { code, message },
215. 		};
216. 
217. 		api.respondInboundRequest(sessionId, request.id, errorResult).match(
218. 			() => logger.debug("Sent error response", { requestId: request.id }),
219. 			(err) => logger.error("Failed to send error response", { error: err })
220. 		);
221. 	}
222. }
223. 
224. /**
225.  * Helper function to respond to a permission request.
226.  *
227.  * @param sessionId - The session ID
228.  * @param jsonRpcRequestId - The JSON-RPC request ID
229.  * @param allowed - Whether the permission was granted
230.  * @param optionId - The option ID that was selected (e.g., "allow", "allow_always", "reject")
231.  */
232. export function respondToPermission(
233. 	sessionId: string,
234. 	jsonRpcRequestId: number,
235. 	allowed: boolean,
236. 	optionId: string = allowed ? "allow" : "reject"
237. ): ResultAsync<void, AcpError> {
238. 	const result = {
239. 		outcome: {
240. 			outcome: allowed ? "selected" : "cancelled",
241. 			optionId,
242. 		},
243. 	};
244. 
245. 	logger.debug("Sending permission response", {
246. 		sessionId,
247. 		jsonRpcRequestId,
248. 		allowed,
249. 		optionId,
250. 	});
251. 
252. 	return api.respondInboundRequest(sessionId, jsonRpcRequestId, result).mapErr((error) => {
253. 		logger.error("Failed to send permission response", { error });
254. 		return new ProtocolError(`Failed to send permission response: ${error.message}`, error);
255. 	});
256. }
257. 
258. /**
259.  * Helper function to respond to a question request (AskUserQuestion tool).
260.  *
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 2m 31s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The frontend contract is stricter than the backend router: TS requires `options`, `toolCall.toolCallId`, and `rawInput`, while Rust is willing to forward thinner payloads. If Copilot emits a sparse permission request, the UI would reject it as invalid params.

---

<sub>⏱️ 2m 41s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Inspecting permission request transformations**

I need to check the `forwarded_permission_request.rs`. It seems like the backend is transforming requests into a format that's easier for the frontend to handle. I'm wondering if it generates a synthetic canonical ID when the `toolCallId` is missing. It's worth looking into to clarify how these pieces fit together. Let's see what insights I can uncover by viewing the code.*

<sub>⏱️ 2m 42s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

There’s a backend forwarding layer here too, which may be where Copilot gets reshaped before it ever reaches the Svelte side. I’m checking whether that forwarder handles Copilot’s sparse cases or just passes them through.

1. use super::SyntheticToolCallContext;
2. use crate::acp::parsers::kind::is_web_search_id;
3. use crate::acp::permission_tracker::WebSearchDedup;
4. use crate::acp::provider::AgentProvider;
5. use serde_json::Value;
6. 
7. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
8. pub(crate) struct ForwardedPermissionRequest {
9.     value: Value,
10. }
11. 
12. impl ForwardedPermissionRequest {
13.     pub fn new(value: Value) -> Self {
14.         Self { value }
15.     }
16. 
17.     pub fn value(&self) -> &Value {
18.         &self.value
19.     }
20. 
21.     pub fn into_value(self) -> Value {
22.         self.value
23.     }
24. 
25.     pub fn session_id(&self) -> Option<String> {
26.         self.value
27.             .pointer("/params/sessionId")
28.             .and_then(|value| value.as_str())
29.             .map(str::to_string)
30.     }
31. 
32.     pub fn inject_parsed_arguments(&mut self, parsed_arguments: Option<&Value>) {
33.         let Some(parsed_arguments) = parsed_arguments else {
34.             return;
35.         };
36. 
37.         if let Some(tool_call) = self.tool_call_object_mut() {
38.             tool_call.insert("parsedArguments".to_string(), parsed_arguments.clone());
39.         }
40.     }
41. 
42.     pub fn normalize_session_id(&mut self, session_id: Option<&str>) -> bool {
43.         let Some(session_id) = session_id else {
44.             return false;
45.         };
46. 
47.         let Some(params) = self.params_object_mut() else {
48.             return false;
49.         };
50. 
51.         let changed = params
52.             .get("sessionId")
53.             .and_then(|value| value.as_str())
54.             .is_some_and(|current| current != session_id);
55. 
56.         params.insert(
57.             "sessionId".to_string(),
58.             Value::String(session_id.to_string()),
59.         );
60.         changed
61.     }
62. 
63.     pub fn remap_tool_call_id(&mut self, tool_call_id: &str) -> bool {
64.         let Some(tool_call) = self.tool_call_object_mut() else {
65.             return false;
66.         };
67. 
68.         tool_call.insert(
69.             "toolCallId".to_string(),
70.             Value::String(tool_call_id.to_string()),
71.         );
72.         true
73.     }
74. 
75.     fn params_object_mut(&mut self) -> Option<&mut serde_json::Map<String, Value>> {
76.         self.value
77.             .pointer_mut("/params")
78.             .and_then(|value| value.as_object_mut())
79.     }
80. 
81.     fn tool_call_object_mut(&mut self) -> Option<&mut serde_json::Map<String, Value>> {
82.         self.value
83.             .pointer_mut("/params/toolCall")
84.             .and_then(|value| value.as_object_mut())
85.     }
86. }
87. 
88. pub(crate) fn remap_forwarded_web_search_tool_call_id(
89.     forwarded: &mut ForwardedPermissionRequest,
90.     provider: Option<&dyn AgentProvider>,
91.     parsed_arguments: &Option<Value>,
92.     synthetic_tool_call: &mut Option<Box<SyntheticToolCallContext>>,
93.     web_search_dedup: &mut WebSearchDedup,
94. ) -> Option<String> {
95.     let synthetic_ctx = synthetic_tool_call.as_mut()?;
96.     if !is_web_search_id(&synthetic_ctx.tool_call_data.id) {
97.         return None;
98.     }
99. 
100.     let query =
101.         provider?.extract_synthetic_permission_query(parsed_arguments, forwarded.value())?;
102.     let session_id = forwarded.session_id()?;
103.     let canonical_id = web_search_dedup.take(&session_id, &query)?;
104. 
105.     synthetic_ctx.tool_call_data.id = canonical_id.clone();
106.     forwarded.remap_tool_call_id(&canonical_id);
107.     Some(canonical_id)
108. }
109.
```

</details>

80. 			await handler.start(permissionCallback);
81. 			handler.stop();
82.

84. 		});
85. 	});
86. 
87. 	describe("parseRequest", () => {
88. 		it("should parse valid JSON-RPC request", async () => {
89. 			await handler.start(permissionCallback);
90. 
91. 			const validRequest = {
92. 				id: 123,
93. 				jsonrpc: "2.0",
94. 				method: ACP_INBOUND_METHODS.REQUEST_PERMISSION,
95. 				params: {
96. 					sessionId: "test-session",
97. 					options: [
98. 						{ kind: "allow", name: "Allow", optionId: "allow" },
99. 						{ kind: "allow_always", name: "Always Allow", optionId: "allow_always" },
100. 						{ kind: "reject", name: "Reject", optionId: "reject" },
101. 					],
102. 					toolCall: {
103. 						toolCallId: "tool-123",
104. 						rawInput: { command: "bun test" },
105. 						title: "Run tests",
106. 					},
107. 				},
108. 			};
109. 
110. 			eventCallback?.({ payload: validRequest });
111. 
112. 			expect(permissionCallback).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
113. 			const permission: PermissionRequest = permissionCallback.mock.calls[0][0];
114. 			expect(permission.id).toBe(buildAcpPermissionId("test-session", "tool-123", 123));
115. 			expect(permission.sessionId).toBe("test-session");
116. 			expect(permission.jsonRpcRequestId).toBe(123);
117. 			expect(permission.permission).toBe("Run tests");
118. 			expect(permission.always).toEqual(["allow_always"]);
119. 			expect(permission.tool).toEqual({
120. 				messageID: "",
121. 				callID: "tool-123",
122. 			});
123. 		});
124.

126. 			await handler.start(permissionCallback);
127. 
128. 			const invalidRequest = {
129. 				jsonrpc: "2.0",
130. 				method: ACP_INBOUND_METHODS.REQUEST_PERMISSION,
131. 				params: {},
132. 			};

135.

137. 		});

140. 			await handler.start(permissionCallback);
141.

143. 				id: 123,

146. 			};

151. 		});

I wonder if I can use a view of the absolute path from the preview. Let’s check it out!**Investigating app processes**

I’m looking into likely copilot subprocesses. I think I may need a broader search for the `com.acepe.app` process. It seems using bash with grep on that specific process could help. I’m also considering whether the driver session might start with a local IP. If the app process isn’t running a debug build, there may not be a bridge available. <sub>⏱️ 5m 33s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The exact UI string isn’t hardcoded in Acepe, which suggests it’s coming from the agent side and getting rendered as normal assistant output after the permission path fails. I’m tracing the backend request/response bridge for where that failure would surface.

1. import type { ResultAsync } from "neverthrow";
2. 
3. import { type AppError } from "../errors/app-error.js";
4. import { api } from "../store/api.js";
5. import type { PlanApprovalInteraction } from "../types/interaction.js";
6. import type { InteractionReplyRequest } from "../types/interaction-reply-request.js";
7. import type { PermissionReply, PermissionRequest } from "../types/permission.js";
8. import type { QuestionAnswer, QuestionRequest } from "../types/question.js";
9. import {
10. 	createLegacyInteractionReplyHandler,
11. 	type InteractionReplyHandler,
12. } from "../types/reply-handler.js";
13. 
14. interface InteractionReplyTarget {
15. 	sessionId: string;
16. 	id?: string;
17. 	jsonRpcRequestId?: number;
18. 	replyHandler?: InteractionReplyHandler;
19. }
20. 
21. type PlanApprovalReplyTarget =
22. 	| PlanApprovalInteraction
23. 	| {
24. 			sessionId: string;
25. 			jsonRpcRequestId: number;
26. 			replyHandler?: InteractionReplyHandler;
27. 			id?: string;
28. 	  };
29. 
30. function resolveReplyHandler(target: InteractionReplyTarget): InteractionReplyHandler {
31. 	return (
32. 		target.replyHandler ??
33. 		createLegacyInteractionReplyHandler(target.id ?? target.sessionId, target.jsonRpcRequestId)
34. 	);
35. }
36. 
37. function createInteractionReplyRequest(
38. 	target: InteractionReplyTarget,
39. 	payload: InteractionReplyRequest["payload"]
40. ): InteractionReplyRequest {
41. 	const base = {
42. 		sessionId: target.sessionId,
43. 		interactionId: target.id,
44. 		replyHandler: resolveReplyHandler(target),
45. 	};
46. 
47. 	switch (payload.kind) {
48. 		case "permission":
49. 			return {
50. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
51. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
52. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
53. 				payload: {
54. 					kind: "permission",
55. 					reply: payload.reply,
56. 					optionId: payload.optionId,
57. 				},
58. 			};
59. 		case "question":
60. 			return {
61. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
62. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
63. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
64. 				payload: {
65. 					kind: "question",
66. 					answers: payload.answers,
67. 					answerMap: payload.answerMap,
68. 				},
69. 			};
70. 		case "question_cancel":
71. 			return {
72. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
73. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
74. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
75. 				payload: {
76. 					kind: "question_cancel",
77. 				},
78. 			};
79. 		case "plan_approval":
80. 			return {
81. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
82. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
83. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
84. 				payload: {
85. 					kind: "plan_approval",
86. 					approved: payload.approved,
87. 				},
88. 			};
89. 	}
90. }
91. 
92. export function replyToPermissionRequest(
93. 	permission: PermissionRequest,
94. 	reply: PermissionReply,
95. 	optionId: string
96. ): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
97. 	return api.replyInteraction(
98. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(permission, {
99. 			kind: "permission",
100. 			reply,
101. 			optionId,
102. 		})
103. 	);
104. }
105. 
106. export function replyToQuestionRequest(
107. 	question: QuestionRequest,
108. 	answers: QuestionAnswer[],
109. 	answerMap: Record<string, string | string[]>
110. ): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
111. 	return api.replyInteraction(
112. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(question, {
113. 			kind: "question",
114. 			answers,
115. 			answerMap,
116. 		})
117. 	);
118. }
119. 
120. export function cancelQuestionRequest(question: QuestionRequest): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
121. 	return api.replyInteraction(
122. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(question, {
123. 			kind: "question_cancel",
124. 		})
125. 	);
126. }
127. 
128. export function replyToPlanApprovalRequest(
129. 	targetOrSessionId: PlanApprovalReplyTarget | string,
130. 	jsonRpcRequestIdOrApproved: number | boolean,
131. 	approved: boolean
132. ): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
133. 	const target: PlanApprovalReplyTarget =
134. 		typeof targetOrSessionId === "string"
135. 			? {
136. 					sessionId: targetOrSessionId,
137. 					jsonRpcRequestId: jsonRpcRequestIdOrApproved as number,
138. 				}
139. 			: targetOrSessionId;
140. 	const resolvedApproved =
141. 		typeof targetOrSessionId === "string"
142. 			? approved
143. 			: (jsonRpcRequestIdOrApproved as boolean);
144. 
145. 	return api.replyInteraction(
146. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(target, {
147. 			kind: "plan_approval",
148. 			approved: resolvedApproved,
149. 		})
150. 	);
151. }
152.
```

360.         .await
361.         .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::Timeout {
362.             operation: "acp_reply_permission: reject cancel".to_string(),
363.         })?
364.         .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
365.         update_permission_projection(&app, &session_id, &permission_id, &reply, false).await;
366.         log_permission_reply_event(
367.             &session_id,
368.             &permission_id,
369.             &reply,
370.             "session_cancelled",
371.             Some(true),
372.         );
373.         Ok(())
374.     } else {
375.         // "once" and "always" - send explicit permission reply
376.         let result = timeout(
377.             SESSION_CLIENT_OPERATION_TIMEOUT,
378.             client_guard.reply_permission(permission_id.clone(), reply.clone()),
379.         )
380.         .await
381.         .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::Timeout {
382.             operation: "acp_reply_permission: operation".to_string(),
383.         })?
384.         .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
385. 
386.         if result {
387.             tracing::info!(permission_id = %permission_id, reply = %reply, "Permission reply sent");
388.             update_permission_projection(&app, &session_id, &permission_id, &reply, true).await;
389.             log_permission_reply_event(&session_id, &permission_id, &reply, "sent", Some(true));
390.             Ok(())
391.         } else {
392.             log_permission_reply_event(
393.                 &session_id,
394.                 &permission_id,
395.                 &reply,
396.                 "rejected_by_client",
397.                 Some(false),
398.             );
399.             Err(SerializableAcpError::ProtocolError {
400.                 message: format!("Permission reply '{}' was not accepted", reply),
401.             })
402.         }
403.     }
404. }
405. 
406. /// Frontend-format question reply entry.
407. ///
408. /// The frontend sends answers as `Array<{ questionIndex: number; answers: string[] }>`.
409. /// This struct deserializes each entry from that format.
410. #[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
411. #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
412. struct QuestionReplyEntry {
413.     #[allow(dead_code)]
414.     question_index: usize,
415.     answers: Vec<String>,
416. }
417. 
418. /// Reply to a question request
419. ///
420. /// For OpenCode HTTP mode: sends reply to POST /question/{id}/reply endpoint
421. /// For ACP mode: questions are replied via JSON-RPC (respondInboundRequest)
422. #[tauri::command]
423. #[specta::specta]
424. pub async fn acp_reply_question(
425.     app: AppHandle,
426.     session_id: String,
427.     question_id: String,
428.     answers: Value,
429. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
430.     tracing::info!(
431.         session_id = %session_id,
432.         question_id = %question_id,
433.         answers = %answers,
434.         "acp_reply_question called"
435.     );
436. 
437.     // Frontend sends Array<{ questionIndex: number; answers: string[] }>.
438.     // Extract just the answers arrays in order for the HTTP client.
439.     let entries: Vec<QuestionReplyEntry> =
440.         serde_json::from_value(answers).map_err(|e| SerializableAcpError::SerializationError {
441.             message: e.to_string(),
442.         })?;
443.     let parsed_answers: Vec<Vec<String>> = entries.into_iter().map(|e| e.answers).collect();
444. 
445.     let session_registry = app.state::<SessionRegistry>();
446.     let client_mutex = session_registry
447.         .get(&session_id)
448.         .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
449. 
450.     let mut client_guard = lock_session_client(&client_mutex, "acp_reply_question").await?;
451. 
452.     let result = timeout(
453.         SESSION_CLIENT_OPERATION_TIMEOUT,
454.         client_guard.reply_question(question_id.clone(), parsed_answers.clone()),
455.     )
456.     .await
457.     .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::Timeout {
458.         operation: "acp_reply_question".to_string(),
459.     })?
460.     .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
461. 
462.     if result {
463.         tracing::info!(question_id = %question_id, "Question reply sent");
464.         update_question_projection(&app, &session_id, &question_id, &parsed_answers).await;
465.         Ok(())
466.     } else {
467.         Err(SerializableAcpError::ProtocolError {
468.             message: "Question reply was not accepted".to_string(),
469.         })
470.     }
```

360.                                             }
361.                                             for update in event.updates {
362.                                                 log_emitted_event(session_id_for_log, &update);
363.                                                 ctx.dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(update));
364.                                             }
365.                                             tracing::debug!(provider = %provider.id(), id = id, method = %method, "Normalized provider extension request in backend");
366.                                             continue;
367.                                         }
368.                                         Ok(None) => {}
369.                                         Err(error) => {
370.                                             tracing::error!(id = id, method = %method, error = %error, "Failed to normalize provider extension request");
371.                                         }
372.                                     }
373.                                 }
374. 
375.                                 if ctx.is_replay_active.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Acquire) {
376.                                     tracing::info!(id = id, method = %method, "Auto-cancelling inbound request during session replay");
377.                                     let cancel_response = json!({
378.                                         "outcome": {
379.                                             "outcome": "cancelled",
380.                                             "reason": "Auto-cancelled during session replay"
381.                                         }
382.                                     });
383.                                     if let Err(error) = send_inbound_response(&ctx.stdin_writer, id, cancel_response).await {
384.                                         tracing::error!(id = id, method = %method, error = %error, "Failed to send auto-cancel response");
385.                                     }
386.                                     continue;
387.                                 }
388. 
389.                                 match route_backend_inbound_request(ctx.app_handle.as_ref(), method, &params, ctx.agent_type).await {
390.                                     InboundRoutingDecision::Handle(result) => {
391.                                         if let Err(error) = send_inbound_response(&ctx.stdin_writer, id, result).await {
392.                                             tracing::error!(id = id, method = %method, error = %error, "Failed to send backend inbound response");
393.                                         } else {
394.                                             tracing::debug!(id = id, method = %method, "Handled inbound request in backend");
395.                                         }
396.                                     }
397.                                     InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi { parsed_arguments, mut synthetic_tool_call } => {
398.                                         let mut forwarded = ForwardedPermissionRequest::new(json.clone());
399.                                         forwarded.inject_parsed_arguments(parsed_arguments.as_ref());
400. 
401.                                         // Normalize sub-agent session ID → root session ID.
402.                                         // Sub-agents use internal child session IDs the frontend doesn't know about.
403.                                         if let Some(root_id) = ctx.active_session_id.lock().ok().and_then(|g| g.clone()) {
404.                                             if forwarded.normalize_session_id(Some(&root_id)) {
405.                                                 tracing::debug!(root_session_id = %root_id, "Normalizing sub-agent session ID");
406.                                             }
407.                                         }
408. 
409.                                         // Remap web search permission IDs to the canonical notification ID.
410.                                         // The notification arrives with a stable ID (e.g. `tool_7319769b-...`)
411.                                         // but the permission arrives with a different ID (`web_search_0`).
412.                                         // We recorded the notification ID in web_search_dedup; remap here
413.                                         // so the synthetic ToolCall and forwarded JSON use the same ID,
414.                                         // preventing a duplicate UI row.
415.                                         if let Ok(mut dedup) = ctx.web_search_dedup.lock() {
416.                                             if let Some(canonical_id) = remap_forwarded_web_search_tool_call_id(
417.                                                 &mut forwarded,
418.                                                 ctx.provider.as_deref(),
419.                                                 &parsed_arguments,
420.                                                 &mut synthetic_tool_call,
421.                                                 &mut dedup,
422.                                             ) {
423.                                                 tracing::debug!(
424.                                                     canonical_id = %canonical_id,
425.                                                     "Remapped web search permission ID to notification canonical ID"
426.                                                 );
427.                                             }
428.                                         }
429. 
430.                                         if let Some(synthetic_ctx) = &synthetic_tool_call {
431.                                             let session_id = forwarded.session_id();
432. 
433.                                             if let Some(ref sid) = session_id {
434.                                                 let synthetic = SessionUpdate::ToolCall {
435.                                                     tool_call: synthetic_ctx.tool_call_data.clone(),
436.                                                     session_id: Some(sid.clone()),
437.                                                 };
438.                                                 ctx.dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(synthetic));
439. 
440.                                                 match ctx.permission_tracker.lock() {
441.                                                     Ok(mut tracker) => {
442.                                                         tracker.track(id, PermissionContext {
443.                                                             session_id: sid.clone(),
444.                                                             tool_call_id: synthetic_ctx.tool_call_data.id.clone(),
445.                                                         });
446.                                                     }
447.                                                     Err(e) => {
448.                                                         tracing::error!("Permission tracker mutex poisoned in track: {e}");
449.                                                     }
450.                                                 }
```

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve confirmed the UI reply path uses a generic interaction API, but the backend still has a dedicated JSON-RPC responder. I’m checking whether Copilot permission requests are going out one way and replies are being sent back on a dead or mismatched channel.

251. pub(super) async fn respond_inbound_request_with_registry(
252.     session_registry: &SessionRegistry,
253.     session_id: &str,
254.     request_id: u64,
255.     result: Value,
256. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
257.     if let Ok(client_mutex) = session_registry.get(session_id) {
258.         let client_guard =
259.             lock_session_client(&client_mutex, "acp_respond_inbound_request: lock").await?;
260. 
261.         timeout(
262.             INBOUND_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT,
263.             client_guard.respond(request_id, result),
264.         )
265.         .await
266.         .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::Timeout {
267.             operation: "acp_respond_inbound_request: operation".to_string(),
268.         })?
269.         .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
270. 
271.         return Ok(());
272.     }
273. 
274.     let responder = session_registry
275.         .get_pending_inbound_responder(session_id)
276.         .ok_or_else(|| {
277.             SerializableAcpError::from(AcpError::SessionNotFound(session_id.to_string()))
278.         })?;
279. 
280.     timeout(
281.         INBOUND_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT,
282.         responder.respond(request_id, result),
283.     )
284.     .await
285.     .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::Timeout {
286.         operation: "acp_respond_inbound_request: bootstrap operation".to_string(),
287.     })?
288.     .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
289. 
290.     Ok(())
291. }
292. 
293. /// Reply to a permission request
294. ///
295. /// For OpenCode HTTP mode: sends reply to POST /permission/reply endpoint
296. /// For ACP mode: "reject" cancels session, "once"/"always" are not supported
297. #[tauri::command]
298. #[specta::specta]
299. pub async fn acp_reply_permission(
300.     app: AppHandle,
301.     session_id: String,
302.     permission_id: String,
303.     reply: String,
304. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
305.     tracing::info!(
306.         session_id = %session_id,
307.         permission_id = %permission_id,
308.         reply = %reply,
309.         "acp_reply_permission called"
310.     );
311.     log_permission_reply_event(&session_id, &permission_id, &reply, "requested", None);
312. 
313.     // Validate reply
314.     if reply != "once" && reply != "always" && reply != "reject" {
315.         return Err(SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
316.             message: format!("Invalid permission reply: {}", reply),
317.         });
318.     }
319. 
320.     let session_registry = app.state::<SessionRegistry>();
```

/Users/alex/Documents/acepe/packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/client_rpc.rs (lines 1-140)

<details>
<summary>140 lines</summary>

```
1. use crate::acp::client_transport::write_serialized_line;
2. use crate::acp::error::{AcpError, AcpResult};
3. use serde_json::{json, Value};
4. use std::collections::HashMap;
5. use std::sync::Arc as StdArc;
6. use tokio::process::ChildStdin;
7. use tokio::sync::{oneshot, Mutex};
8. use tokio::time::{timeout, Duration};
9. 
10. fn next_request_id(request_id: &StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<u64>>) -> AcpResult<u64> {
11.     let mut id = request_id
12.         .lock()
13.         .map_err(|_| AcpError::InvalidState("Request ID mutex poisoned".to_string()))?;
14.     let current = *id;
15.     *id += 1;
16.     Ok(current)
17. }
18. 
19. fn is_subprocess_death_jsonrpc_error(error: &Value) -> bool {
20.     error
21.         .get("code")
22.         .and_then(|code| code.as_i64())
23.         .is_some_and(|code| code == -32001)
24. }
25. 
26. fn is_method_not_found_jsonrpc_error(error: &Value) -> bool {
27.     let code_is_method_not_found = error
28.         .get("code")
29.         .and_then(|code| code.as_i64())
30.         .is_some_and(|code| code == -32601);
31.     if code_is_method_not_found {
32.         return true;
33.     }
34. 
35.     error
36.         .get("message")
37.         .and_then(|message| message.as_str())
38.         .is_some_and(|message| message.contains("Method not found"))
39. }
40. 
41. pub(crate) async fn send_request(
42.     request_id: &StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<u64>>,
43.     pending_requests: &StdArc<Mutex<HashMap<u64, crate::acp::client::PendingRequestEntry>>>,
44.     stdin_writer: &StdArc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>,
45.     process_generation: u64,
46.     method: &str,
47.     params: Value,
48.     request_timeout_secs: u64,
49. ) -> AcpResult<Value> {
50.     let id = next_request_id(request_id)?;
51. 
52.     tracing::debug!(method = %method, id = id, "Sending request");
53.     let request = json!({
54.         "jsonrpc": "2.0",
55.         "id": id,
56.         "method": method,
57.         "params": params
58.     });
59. 
60.     let request_str = serde_json::to_string(&request).map_err(AcpError::SerializationError)?;
61.     let (tx, rx) = oneshot::channel();
62. 
63.     {
64.         let mut pending = pending_requests.lock().await;
65.         pending.insert(
66.             id,
67.             crate::acp::client::PendingRequestEntry {
68.                 generation: process_generation,
69.                 sender: tx,
70.             },
71.         );
72.     }
73. 
74.     tracing::debug!(request = %request_str, "Writing request to stdin");
75. 
76.     if let Err(error) = write_serialized_line(stdin_writer, &request_str).await {
77.         pending_requests.lock().await.remove(&id);
78.         return Err(error);
79.     }
80. 
81.     tracing::debug!(
82.         id = id,
83.         timeout_secs = request_timeout_secs,
84.         "Waiting for response"
85.     );
86. 
87.     let response = match timeout(Duration::from_secs(request_timeout_secs), rx).await {
88.         Ok(Ok(response)) => response,
89.         Ok(Err(_)) => {
90.             tracing::error!(id = id, "Response channel closed");
91.             pending_requests.lock().await.remove(&id);
92.             return Err(AcpError::ChannelClosed);
93.         }
94.         Err(_) => {
95.             tracing::error!(id = id, method = %method, "Request timed out after {}s", request_timeout_secs);
96.             pending_requests.lock().await.remove(&id);
97.             return Err(AcpError::Timeout(format!(
98.                 "{} (after {}s)",
99.                 method, request_timeout_secs
100.             )));
101.         }
102.     };
103. 
104.     tracing::debug!(id = id, "Received response");
105. 
106.     if let Some(error) = response.get("error") {
107.         let error_str = serde_json::to_string(error).unwrap_or_else(|_| format!("{:?}", error));
108.         if is_method_not_found_jsonrpc_error(error) {
109.             tracing::warn!(error = %error_str, "Method not found in response");
110.         } else if is_subprocess_death_jsonrpc_error(error) {
111.             tracing::warn!(error = %error_str, "Subprocess terminated, request failed");
112.         } else {
113.             tracing::error!(error = %error_str, "Error in response");
114.         }
115.         return Err(AcpError::JsonRpcError(error_str));
116.     }
117. 
118.     response
119.         .get("result")
120.         .cloned()
121.         .ok_or_else(|| AcpError::ProtocolError("No result in response".to_string()))
122. }
123. 
124. pub(crate) async fn respond(
125.     stdin_writer: &StdArc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>,
126.     id: u64,
127.     result: Value,
128. ) -> AcpResult<()> {
129.     let response = json!({
130.         "jsonrpc": "2.0",
131.         "id": id,
132.         "result": result
133.     });
134. 
135.     let response_str = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(AcpError::SerializationError)?;
136.     tracing::debug!(id = id, response = %response_str, "Sending response to inbound request");
137.     write_serialized_line(stdin_writer, &response_str).await?;
138.     tracing::debug!(id = id, "Response sent successfully");
139.     Ok(())
140. }
```

</details>

133. #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Deserialize, Type)]
134. #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
135. pub struct CanonicalInteractionReplyRequest {
136.     pub session_id: String,
137.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
138.     pub interaction_id: Option<String>,
139.     pub reply_handler: InteractionReplyHandler,
140.     pub payload: CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload,
141. }
142. 
143. #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Deserialize, Type)]
144. #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case", tag = "kind")]
145. pub enum CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload {
146.     Permission { reply: String, option_id: String },
147.     Question { answers: Value, answer_map: Value },
148.     QuestionCancel,
149.     PlanApproval { approved: bool },
150. }
151. 
152. fn parse_json_rpc_request_id(
153.     reply_handler: &InteractionReplyHandler,
154. ) -> Result<u64, SerializableAcpError> {
155.     if reply_handler.kind != InteractionReplyHandlerKind::JsonRpc {
156.         return Err(SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
157.             message: "Canonical reply requires a JSON-RPC handler".to_string(),
158.         });
159.     }
160. 
161.     reply_handler
162.         .request_id
163.         .parse::<u64>()
164.         .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
165.             message: format!("Invalid JSON-RPC request id '{}'", reply_handler.request_id),
166.         })
167. }
168. 
169. fn interaction_kind_for_payload(payload: &CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload) -> InteractionKind {
170.     match payload {
171.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::Permission { .. } => InteractionKind::Permission,
172.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::Question { .. }
173.         | CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::QuestionCancel => InteractionKind::Question,
174.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::PlanApproval { .. } => InteractionKind::PlanApproval,
175.     }
176. }
177. 
178. fn adapted_result_from_payload(payload: &CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload) -> Value {
179.     match payload {
180.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::Permission { reply, option_id } => {
181.             let allowed = reply != "reject";
182.             json!({
183.                 "outcome": {
184.                     "outcome": if allowed { "selected" } else { "cancelled" },
185.                     "optionId": option_id,
186.                 }
187.             })
188.         }
189.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::Question { answer_map, .. } => {
190.             json!({
191.                 "outcome": {
192.                     "outcome": "selected",
193.                     "optionId": "allow",
194.                 },
195.                 "_meta": {
196.                     "answers": answer_map,
197.                 }
198.             })
199.         }
200.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::QuestionCancel => {
201.             json!({
202.                 "outcome": {
203.                     "outcome": "cancelled",
204.                 }
205.             })
206.         }
207.         CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::PlanApproval { approved } => {
208.             json!({ "approved": approved })
209.         }
210.     }
211. }
212. 
213. async fn persist_canonical_interaction_reply(
214.     app: &AppHandle,
215.     request: &CanonicalInteractionReplyRequest,
216.     adapted_result: &Value,
217. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
218.     let Some(interaction_id) = request.interaction_id.as_deref() else {
219.         return Ok(());
220.     };
221. 
222.     let Some((state, domain_event_kind, response)) = interaction_transition_from_result(
223.         &interaction_kind_for_payload(&request.payload),
224.         adapted_result,
225.     ) else {
226.         return Err(SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
227.             message: "Unable to derive canonical interaction transition from reply".to_string(),
228.         });
229.     };
230. 
231.     let projection_registry = app.state::<Arc<ProjectionRegistry>>();
232.     let db = app.state::<DbConn>();
233.     let dispatcher = app.state::<AcpUiEventDispatcher>();
234. 
235.     persist_interaction_transition(
236.         projection_registry.inner().as_ref(),
237.         Some(db.inner()),
238.         Some(dispatcher.inner()),
239.         &request.session_id,
240.         interaction_id,
```

</details>

470.     }

473. /// Reply to a canonical interaction through one backend-owned command path.
474. #[tauri::command]
475. #[specta::specta]
476. pub async fn acp_reply_interaction(
477.     app: AppHandle,
478.     request: CanonicalInteractionReplyRequest,
479. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
480.     match request.reply_handler.kind {
481.         InteractionReplyHandlerKind::Http => match &request.payload {
482.             CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::Permission { reply, .. } => {
483.                 let interaction_id = request.interaction_id.clone().ok_or_else(|| {
484.                     SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
485.                         message: "HTTP permission replies require interactionId".to_string(),
486.                     }
487.                 })?;
488.                 acp_reply_permission(app, request.session_id, interaction_id, reply.clone()).await
489.             }
490.             CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::Question { answers, .. } => {
491.                 let interaction_id = request.interaction_id.clone().ok_or_else(|| {
492.                     SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
493.                         message: "HTTP question replies require interactionId".to_string(),
494.                     }
495.                 })?;
496.                 acp_reply_question(app, request.session_id, interaction_id, answers.clone()).await
497.             }
498.             CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::QuestionCancel => {
499.                 let interaction_id = request.interaction_id.clone().ok_or_else(|| {
500.                     SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
501.                         message: "HTTP question cancellation requires interactionId".to_string(),
502.                     }
503.                 })?;
504.                 acp_reply_question(app, request.session_id, interaction_id, json!([])).await
505.             }
506.             CanonicalInteractionReplyPayload::PlanApproval { .. } => {
507.                 Err(SerializableAcpError::InvalidState {
508.                     message: "Plan approval replies do not support HTTP routing".to_string(),
509.                 })
510.             }
511.         },
512.         InteractionReplyHandlerKind::JsonRpc => {
513.             let request_id = parse_json_rpc_request_id(&request.reply_handler)?;
514.             let adapted_result = adapted_result_from_payload(&request.payload);
515.             let session_registry = app.state::<SessionRegistry>();
516.             respond_inbound_request_with_registry(
517.                 &session_registry,
518.                 &request.session_id,
519.                 request_id,
520.                 adapted_result.clone(),
521.             )
522.             .await?;
523.             persist_canonical_interaction_reply(&app, &request, &adapted_result).await
524.         }
525.     }
526. }
527. 
528. /// Respond to an inbound JSON-RPC request from the ACP subprocess
529. ///
530. /// This is used to respond to requests like `client/requestPermission` where
531. /// the subprocess needs a response back. The request_id is the JSON-RPC id
532. /// from the inbound request.
533. #[tauri::command]
534. #[specta::specta]
535. pub async fn acp_respond_inbound_request(
536.     app: AppHandle,
537.     session_id: String,
538.     request_id: u64,
539.     result: Value,
540. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
541.     tracing::info!(
542.         session_id = %session_id,
543.         request_id = request_id,
544.         "acp_respond_inbound_request called"
545.     );
546. 
547.     let session_registry = app.state::<SessionRegistry>();
548.     respond_inbound_request_with_registry(&session_registry, &session_id, request_id, result)
549.         .await?;
550. 
551.     tracing::info!(
552.         session_id = %session_id,
553.         request_id = request_id,
554.         "Inbound request response sent successfully"
555.     );
556.     Ok(())
557. }
558.

***Investigating event handling**

I'm considering whether the event dispatcher might drop high-priority events, which leads me to think I should inspect logs or perform tests related to the UI event dispatcher and event bridge SSE. There's a possibility that the permission prompt path could use high priority. If the event bridge stream closes, the UI might not receive requests, which would explain the "Stream closed" message. I’m also looking into how the connection and reconnection are handled in the event source code.*

33. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
34. pub enum AcpUiEventPayload {
35.     SessionUpdate(Box<SessionUpdate>),
36.     SessionDomainEvent(Box<SessionDomainEvent>),
37.     Json(Value),
38. }
39. 
40. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
41. pub struct AcpUiEvent {
42.     pub session_id: Option<String>,
43.     pub event_name: &'static str,
44.     pub payload: AcpUiEventPayload,
45.     pub priority: AcpUiEventPriority,
46.     pub droppable: bool,
47.     pub created_at: Instant,
48. }
49. 
50. impl AcpUiEvent {
51.     #[must_use]
52.     pub fn session_update(update: SessionUpdate) -> Self {
53.         let session_id = update.session_id().map(ToString::to_string);
54.         let priority = match &update {
55.             SessionUpdate::PermissionRequest { .. } | SessionUpdate::QuestionRequest { .. } => {
56.                 AcpUiEventPriority::High
57.             }
58.             _ => AcpUiEventPriority::Normal,
59.         };
60. 
61.         let droppable = match &update {
62.             SessionUpdate::AgentMessageChunk { .. }
63.             | SessionUpdate::AgentThoughtChunk { .. }
64.             | SessionUpdate::UserMessageChunk { .. } => true,
65.             SessionUpdate::ToolCallUpdate { update, .. } => update.streaming_input_delta.is_some(),
66.             _ => false,
67.         };
68. 
69.         Self {
70.             session_id,
71.             event_name: "acp-session-update",
72.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::SessionUpdate(Box::new(update)),
73.             priority,
74.             droppable,
75.             created_at: Instant::now(),
76.         }
77.     }
78. 
79.     #[must_use]
80.     pub fn inbound_request(request: Value) -> Self {
81.         let session_id = request
82.             .get("params")
83.             .and_then(|params| params.get("sessionId"))
84.             .and_then(Value::as_str)
85.             .map(ToString::to_string);
86. 
87.         Self {
88.             session_id,
89.             event_name: "acp-inbound-request",
90.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::Json(request),
91.             priority: AcpUiEventPriority::High,
92.             droppable: false,
93.             created_at: Instant::now(),
94.         }
95.     }
96. 
97.     #[must_use]
98.     pub fn session_domain_event(event: SessionDomainEvent) -> Self {
99.         let session_id = Some(event.session_id.clone());
100. 
101.         Self {
102.             session_id,
103.             event_name: "acp-session-domain-event",
104.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::SessionDomainEvent(Box::new(event)),
105.             priority: AcpUiEventPriority::Normal,
106.             droppable: false,
107.             created_at: Instant::now(),
108.         }
109.     }
110. 
111.     #[must_use]
112.     pub fn json_event(
113.         event_name: &'static str,
114.         payload: Value,
115.         session_id: Option<String>,
116.         priority: AcpUiEventPriority,
117.         droppable: bool,
118.     ) -> Self {
119.         Self {
120.             session_id,
121.             event_name,
122.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::Json(payload),
123.             priority,
124.             droppable,
125.             created_at: Instant::now(),
126.         }
127.     }
128. 
129.     fn to_json_payload(&self) -> Result<Value, serde_json::Error> {
130.         match &self.payload {
131.             AcpUiEventPayload::SessionUpdate(update) => serde_json::to_value(update.as_ref()),
132.             AcpUiEventPayload::SessionDomainEvent(event) => serde_json::to_value(event.as_ref()),
133.             AcpUiEventPayload::Json(value) => Ok(value.clone()),
134.         }
135.     }
136. 
137.     fn publish(&self, hub: &AcpEventHubState) -> Result<(), serde_json::Error> {
138.         let payload = self.to_json_payload()?;
139.         hub.publish(
140.             self.event_name,
```

1. import { err, errAsync, ok, okAsync, Result, type ResultAsync } from "neverthrow";
2. import type { JsonValue } from "$lib/services/converted-session-types.js";
3. import { tauriClient } from "$lib/utils/tauri-client.js";
4. import { LOGGER_IDS } from "../constants/logger-ids.js";
5. import type { AcpError } from "../errors/index.js";
6. import { ProtocolError } from "../errors/index.js";
7. import { createLogger } from "../utils/logger.js";
8. 
9. const logger = createLogger({
10. 	id: LOGGER_IDS.EVENT_SUBSCRIBER,
11. 	name: "ACP Event Bridge",
12. });
13. 
14. type JsonObject = { [key: string]: JsonValue };
15. 
16. export interface AcpEventEnvelope {
17. 	seq: number;
18. 	eventName: string;
19. 	sessionId: string | null;
20. 	payload: JsonValue;
21. 	priority: string;
22. 	droppable: boolean;
23. 	emittedAtMs: number;
24. }
25. 
26. const parseJsonValue = Result.fromThrowable(
27. 	(raw: string): JsonValue => JSON.parse(raw) as JsonValue,
28. 	(error) => new ProtocolError(`Failed to parse ACP event payload: ${String(error)}`, error)
29. );
30. 
31. const createEventSource = Result.fromThrowable(
32. 	(url: string): EventSource => new EventSource(url),
33. 	(error) => new ProtocolError(`Failed to create EventSource: ${String(error)}`, error)
34. );
35. 
36. function isJsonObject(value: JsonValue): value is JsonObject {
37. 	return typeof value === "object" && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);
38. }
39. 
40. function asJsonObject(value: JsonValue): Result<JsonObject, ProtocolError> {
41. 	if (!isJsonObject(value)) {
42. 		return err(new ProtocolError("ACP event envelope must be a JSON object"));
43. 	}
44. 	return ok(value);
45. }
46. 
47. function readNumberField(object: JsonObject, field: string): Result<number, ProtocolError> {
48. 	const value = object[field];
49. 	if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
50. 		return err(new ProtocolError(`ACP event envelope.${field} must be a finite number`));
51. 	}
52. 	return ok(value);
53. }
54. 
55. function readStringField(object: JsonObject, field: string): Result<string, ProtocolError> {
56. 	const value = object[field];
57. 	if (typeof value !== "string") {
58. 		return err(new ProtocolError(`ACP event envelope.${field} must be a string`));
59. 	}
60. 	return ok(value);
61. }
62. 
63. function readOptionalStringField(
64. 	object: JsonObject,
65. 	field: string
66. ): Result<string | null, ProtocolError> {
67. 	const value = object[field];
68. 	if (value === null || value === undefined) {
69. 		return ok(null);
70. 	}
71. 	if (typeof value !== "string") {
72. 		return err(new ProtocolError(`ACP event envelope.${field} must be a string or null`));
73. 	}
74. 	return ok(value);
75. }
76. 
77. function readBooleanField(object: JsonObject, field: string): Result<boolean, ProtocolError> {
78. 	const value = object[field];
79. 	if (typeof value !== "boolean") {
80. 		return err(new ProtocolError(`ACP event envelope.${field} must be a boolean`));
81. 	}
82. 	return ok(value);
83. }
84. 
85. export function parseAcpEventEnvelope(raw: string): Result<AcpEventEnvelope, ProtocolError> {
86. 	return parseJsonValue(raw)
87. 		.andThen(asJsonObject)
88. 		.andThen((object) =>
89. 			readNumberField(object, "seq").andThen((seq) =>
90. 				readStringField(object, "eventName").andThen((eventName) =>
91. 					readOptionalStringField(object, "sessionId").andThen((sessionId) =>
92. 						readStringField(object, "priority").andThen((priority) =>
93. 							readBooleanField(object, "droppable").andThen((droppable) =>
94. 								readNumberField(object, "emittedAtMs").map((emittedAtMs) => {
95. 									const payload = object.payload ?? null;
96. 									return {
97. 										seq,
98. 										eventName,
99. 										sessionId,
100. 										payload,
101. 										priority,
102. 										droppable,
103. 										emittedAtMs,
104. 									};
105. 								})
106. 							)
107. 						)
108. 					)
109. 				)
110. 			)
111. 		);
112. }
113. 
114. export function openAcpEventSource(
115. 	onEnvelope: (envelope: AcpEventEnvelope) => void
116. ): ResultAsync<() => void, AcpError> {
117. 	return tauriClient.acp.getEventBridgeInfo().andThen((bridgeInfo) => {
118. 		const sourceResult = createEventSource(bridgeInfo.eventsUrl);
119. 		if (sourceResult.isErr()) {
120. 			return errAsync(sourceResult.error);
121. 		}
122. 
123. 		const source = sourceResult.value;
124. 		let sseEventCount = 0;
125. 		let sseLastReportTime = Date.now();
126. 		source.onmessage = (event: MessageEvent<string>) => {
127. 			// DEBUG: Track SSE event rate to detect floods
128. 			sseEventCount++;
129. 			const now = Date.now();
130. 			if (now - sseLastReportTime > 1000) {
131. 				if (sseEventCount > 50) {
132. 					console.warn(`[SSE_FLOOD] ${sseEventCount} events in ${now - sseLastReportTime}ms`);
133. 				}
134. 				sseEventCount = 0;
135. 				sseLastReportTime = now;
136. 			}
137. 
138. 			const t0 = performance.now();
139. 			const envelopeResult = parseAcpEventEnvelope(event.data);
140. 			if (envelopeResult.isErr()) {
141. 				logger.warn("Discarding malformed ACP bridge event", { error: envelopeResult.error });
142. 				return;
143. 			}
144. 			onEnvelope(envelopeResult.value);
145. 			const dt = performance.now() - t0;
146. 			if (dt > 50) {
147. 				console.warn(`[SSE_SLOW] Event processing took ${dt.toFixed(1)}ms`, {
148. 					eventName: envelopeResult.value.eventName,
149. 					sessionId: envelopeResult.value.sessionId,
150. 					seq: envelopeResult.value.seq,
151. 				});
152. 			}
153. 		};
154. 		source.onerror = () => {
155. 			logger.warn("ACP bridge EventSource reported an error");
156. 		};
157. 
158. 		const cleanup = () => {
159. 			source.onmessage = null;
160. 			source.onerror = null;
161. 			source.close();
162. 		};
163. 
164. 		return okAsync(cleanup);
165. 	});
166. }
167.
```

<details>
<summary>173 lines</summary>

```
1. import { okAsync, ResultAsync } from "neverthrow";
2. 
3. import type { JsonValue, SessionUpdate } from "../../services/converted-session-types.js";
4. import { LOGGER_IDS } from "../constants/logger-ids.js";
5. import { type AcpError, ProtocolError } from "../errors/index.js";
6. import { createLogger } from "../utils/logger.js";
7. import { openAcpEventSource } from "./acp-event-bridge.js";
8. 
9. /**
10.  * Subscribes to Tauri events for session updates.
11.  *
12.  * This subscriber listens for `acp-session-update` events from the Tauri backend.
13.  * Session updates are already parsed and typed by the Rust backend.
14.  *
15.  * Supports multiple listeners via a single Tauri event listener (fan-out pattern).
16.  * This prevents memory leaks from creating multiple Tauri listeners.
17.  */
18. export class EventSubscriber {
19. 	private unlistenFn: (() => void) | null = null;
20. 	private listeners = new Map<string, (update: SessionUpdate) => void>();
21. 	private listenerIdCounter = 0;
22. 	private isInitializing = false;
23. 	private initPromise: Promise<void> | null = null;
24. 	private readonly logger = createLogger({
25. 		id: LOGGER_IDS.EVENT_SUBSCRIBER,
26. 		name: "Event Subscriber",
27. 	});
28. 
29. 	/**
30. 	 * Subscribe to session update events.
31. 	 * Multiple listeners are supported - they all receive updates from a single Tauri listener.
32. 	 *
33. 	 * @param listener - Callback function to receive session updates
34. 	 * @returns ResultAsync containing a unique listener ID that can be used to unsubscribe
35. 	 */
36. 	subscribe(listener: (update: SessionUpdate) => void): ResultAsync<string, AcpError> {
37. 		const listenerId = `listener-${++this.listenerIdCounter}`;
38. 		this.listeners.set(listenerId, listener);
39. 
40. 		// If we already have a Tauri listener, just return the ID
41. 		if (this.unlistenFn) {
42. 			return okAsync(listenerId);
43. 		}
44. 
45. 		// If initialization is in progress, wait for it
46. 		if (this.isInitializing && this.initPromise) {
47. 			return ResultAsync.fromPromise(
48. 				this.initPromise.then(() => {
49. 					// Initialization may complete after this specific listener unsubscribed.
50. 					if (!this.listeners.has(listenerId)) {
51. 						throw new Error("Listener was removed during subscriber initialization");
52. 					}
53. 					return listenerId;
54. 				}),
55. 				(error) => {
56. 					this.listeners.delete(listenerId);
57. 					return new ProtocolError(`Failed to wait for initialization: ${error}`, error);
58. 				}
59. 			);
60. 		}
61. 
62. 		// Create the single ACP event bridge listener that fans out to all registered callbacks.
63. 		this.isInitializing = true;
64. 		const listenResult = openAcpEventSource((envelope) => {
65. 			if (envelope.eventName !== "acp-session-update") {
66. 				return;
67. 			}
68. 			const update = parseSessionUpdatePayload(envelope.payload);
69. 			if (!update) {
70. 				this.logger.warn("Discarding invalid acp-session-update payload", {
71. 					seq: envelope.seq,
72. 					eventName: envelope.eventName,
73. 				});
74. 				return;
75. 			}
76. 
77. 			// Fan out to all registered listeners
78. 			// Wrap each callback in try-catch so a single failure does not skip subsequent listeners
79. 			for (const [id, cb] of this.listeners.entries()) {
80. 				try {
81. 					cb(update);
82. 				} catch (error) {
83. 					this.logger.error("Listener threw error", { listenerId: id, error });
84. 				}
85. 			}
86. 		})
87. 			.map((unlisten) => {
88. 				this.isInitializing = false;
89. 				// If all listeners unsubscribed while initialization was in flight,
90. 				// immediately release the native Tauri listener to avoid leaks.
91. 				if (this.listeners.size === 0) {
92. 					unlisten();
93. 					this.unlistenFn = null;
94. 				} else {
95. 					this.unlistenFn = unlisten;
96. 				}
97. 				return listenerId;
98. 			})
99. 			.mapErr((error) => {
100. 				this.isInitializing = false;
101. 				this.unlistenFn = null;
102. 				this.listeners.delete(listenerId);
103. 				return new ProtocolError(`Failed to subscribe to session updates: ${error}`, error);
104. 			});
105. 
106. 		// Store promise for awaiting in concurrent subscribe calls
107. 		// Propagate initialization failures so waiting subscribers can recover.
108. 		this.initPromise = listenResult
109. 			.match(
110. 				() => undefined,
111. 				(error) => {
112. 					throw error;
113. 				}
114. 			)
115. 			.then(() => undefined)
116. 			.finally(() => {
117. 				this.initPromise = null;
118. 			});
119. 
120. 		return listenResult;
121. 	}
122. 
123. 	/**
124. 	 * Unsubscribe a specific listener by ID.
125. 	 * The Tauri listener is only removed when all listeners are unsubscribed.
126. 	 *
127. 	 * @param listenerId - The ID returned from subscribe()
128. 	 */
129. 	unsubscribeById(listenerId: string): void {
130. 		this.listeners.delete(listenerId);
131. 
132. 		// If no more listeners, clean up the Tauri listener
133. 		if (this.listeners.size === 0 && this.unlistenFn) {
134. 			this.unlistenFn();
135. 			this.unlistenFn = null;
136. 		}
137. 	}
138. 
139. 	/**
140. 	 * Unsubscribe all listeners and clean up the Tauri listener.
141. 	 * @deprecated Use unsubscribeById for proper cleanup. This removes ALL listeners.
142. 	 */
143. 	unsubscribe(): void {
144. 		this.listeners.clear();
145. 		if (this.unlistenFn) {
146. 			this.unlistenFn();
147. 			this.unlistenFn = null;
148. 		}
149. 	}
150. 
151. 	/**
152. 	 * Get the number of active listeners.
153. 	 */
154. 	get listenerCount(): number {
155. 		return this.listeners.size;
156. 	}
157. }
158. 
159. function isJsonObject(value: JsonValue): value is { [key: string]: JsonValue } {
160. 	return typeof value === "object" && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);
161. }
162. 
163. function parseSessionUpdatePayload(payload: JsonValue): SessionUpdate | null {
164. 	if (!isJsonObject(payload)) {
165. 		return null;
166. 	}
167. 	const updateType = payload.type;
168. 	if (typeof updateType !== "string") {
169. 		return null;
170. 	}
171. 	return payload as SessionUpdate;
172. }
173.
```

<sub>⏱️ 8m 10s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Debugging copilot issues**

I’m assessing a situation where search results are too large. I wonder if using `web_fetch` is right, but maybe trying a bash method like `node -e` or `rg -o` could simplify things. However, I might need to focus on the repository instead. The user seems to want to debug Copilot, and I can share findings. There’s a probable bug with UI strict validation leading to dropped permission requests, causing timeouts. It seems there was a failure with the event bridge and permission logging too.**Analyzing permission issues**

It seems like the subprocess tried to use a permissioned tool, but the stream closed before it could be approved. I found several insights: the Tauri MCP bridge only works in debug builds, and there's an active JSON-RPC permission pipeline. The TypeScript parser is stricter than the Rust forwarder and requires specific parameters. If Copilot emits a sparse request, it can result in dropped permissions and errors. The event logging isn't ideal, and if the bridge dies during a rebuild, prompts get lost. It looks like the user might want us to debug this further.**Planning user interaction**

I’m considering if I should ask the user to reproduce the issue once the app is back up, so we can capture the exact payload. Using "ask_user" might be an option, but since the task is incomplete due to the app being down, it feels better to hold off on that. Still, we can share our current findings. The instructions suggest offering "ask_user" isn't applicable after a GSD command, so I’ll explain our findings clearly and mention the blockage caused by the app rebuild.*

<sub>⏱️ 9m 35s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Findings:** Copilot is reaching the failure point, but Acepe is not surfacing a usable permission prompt before the request dies. In the live Tauri app, the Copilot panel already showed: **`Tool permission request failed: Error: Stream closed`** and a follow-up message about repeated permission timeouts, with no visible permission action UI.

The strongest code-level suspect is the inbound permission normalization seam:

- **Bridge only exists in debug builds**: `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs:585-586`
- **Rust forwards `session/request_permission` permissively**: `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/inbound_request_router/permission_handlers.rs:17-39`
- **Frontend parses that request strictly**: `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/schemas/inbound-request.schema.ts:69-79` requires `sessionId`, `options`, `toolCall.toolCallId`, and `toolCall.rawInput`
- **If parsing fails, the prompt is dropped**: `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/logic/inbound-request-handler.ts:152-157`

So the likely bug is: **Copilot is sending a permission request shape Acepe forwards, but the Svelte-side schema rejects it, so no permission request is created and Copilot eventually reports `Stream closed`.**

One secondary issue also showed up during debugging: the dev app restarted and the Tauri bridge dropped (`ECONNREFUSED` on `127.0.0.1:9223`), so bridge instability during rebuilds can make this worse, but it does not look like the primary logic bug.

---

<sub>⏱️ 9m 37s</sub>

<sub>⏱️ 9m 48s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Implementing a fix**

I need to move forward on implementing a fix, as there's enough evidence to support it. Maybe I should create a todo table, though it might not be necessary.

The user asked to debug copilot since we're not receiving a permissions request, using the Tauri MCP for debugging. I think I should take action autonomously, possibly starting with a failing test due to the nature of the bug fix. 

I need to check existing tests related to inbound-request-normalization and session-event-service to identify the exact fix. My hypothesis is that the schema might be too strict and that I need to confirm certain requests' elements may be missing.**Inspecting payload creation**

I need to check how forwarded payloads are being created, especially to see if anything like the toolCall is missing. There's a possibility that the Copilot event might go through session updates instead of inbound requests. The UI symptoms suggest that the permission stream has failed in the agent.

I could examine the Copilot app bundle for the request_permission shape by searching snippets and maybe using a Node script. It’s likely that the cached binary differs from the global package, so inspecting that could be useful, especially around session/request_permission. I might also need to look into the "Stream closed" message in the Copilot source to identify where the code might be failing. It might be enough to implement a more lenient parser, and creating tests for sparse inbound permission requests could help if they're missing key elements.**Deciding on defaults**

I need to establish what the defaults should be. If the toolCallId is missing, permission can’t be anchored, but the backend can still forward a synthetic_tool_call as None. Currently, the frontend schema requires a toolCallId, but maybe I could use a `request-${id}` fallback or derive it from the request ID instead. Although the permission store requires a toolCallId, I can generate `request-${jsonRpcRequestId}` when it's absent. It's worth noting that the backend doesn’t provide a synthetic tool call if the toolCallId is missing, but the UI could still display a permission card that isn’t anchored. If the permission tool call ID is absent, perhaps I should rely on the request ID string to maintain stability.*

<sub>⏱️ 10m 10s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at /Users/alex/Documents/acepe/packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/schemas/inbound-request.schema.ts.**

/Users/alex/Documents/acepe/packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/schemas/inbound-request.schema.ts (lines 1-180)

<details>
<summary>111 lines</summary>

```
1. /**
2.  * Schemas for inbound JSON-RPC requests from the ACP subprocess.
3.  *
4.  * Validates JSON-RPC envelope and method-specific params (e.g., requestPermission).
5.  * Used by inbound-request-handler to parse unknown payloads without type assertions.
6.  */
7. 
8. import { err, ok, type Result } from "neverthrow";
9. import { z } from "zod";
10. 
11. import type { JsonValue, ToolArguments } from "../../services/converted-session-types.js";
12. import type { AcpError } from "../errors/index.js";
13. import { ProtocolError } from "../errors/index.js";
14. import { QuestionItemSchema } from "./tool-call-content.schema.js";
15. 
16. /**
17.  * Maps a Zod parse error to ProtocolError for neverthrow Result types.
18.  */
19. export function zodErrorToProtocolError(zodError: z.ZodError, context?: string): ProtocolError {
20. 	const message = context ? `${context}: ${zodError.message}` : zodError.message;
21. 	return new ProtocolError(message, zodError);
22. }
23. 
24. /**
25.  * JSON-RPC 2.0 request envelope from the ACP subprocess.
26.  */
27. export const JsonRpcRequestSchema = z.object({
28. 	id: z.number(),
29. 	jsonrpc: z.string().default("2.0"),
30. 	method: z.string(),
31. 	params: z.unknown(),
32. });
33. export type JsonRpcRequest = z.infer<typeof JsonRpcRequestSchema>;
34. 
35. /**
36.  * Question data for AskUserQuestion tool (from _meta.askUserQuestion or rawInput.questions).
37.  */
38. export const AskUserQuestionDataSchema = z.object({
39. 	questions: z.array(QuestionItemSchema),
40. });
41. export type AskUserQuestionData = z.infer<typeof AskUserQuestionDataSchema>;
42. 
43. /**
44.  * rawInput shape when tool is AskUserQuestion (upstream v0.18+).
45.  * Questions come from rawInput.questions instead of _meta.askUserQuestion.
46.  */
47. export const RawInputWithQuestionsSchema = z.object({
48. 	questions: z.array(QuestionItemSchema),
49. });
50. 
51. const PermissionOptionSchema = z.object({
52. 	kind: z.string(),
53. 	name: z.string(),
54. 	optionId: z.string(),
55. });
56. 
57. const ToolCallSchema = z.object({
58. 	toolCallId: z.string(),
59. 	rawInput: z.custom<JsonValue>(),
60. 	/** Rust-parsed ToolArguments from rawInput — agent-agnostic. */
61. 	parsedArguments: z.custom<ToolArguments>().optional(),
62. 	title: z.string().optional(),
63. 	name: z.string().optional(),
64. });
65. 
66. /**
67.  * Parameters for client/requestPermission method.
68.  */
69. export const RequestPermissionParamsSchema = z
70. 	.object({
71. 		sessionId: z.string(),
72. 		options: z.array(PermissionOptionSchema),
73. 		toolCall: ToolCallSchema,
74. 		_meta: z
75. 			.object({
76. 				askUserQuestion: AskUserQuestionDataSchema.optional(),
77. 			})
78. 			.optional(),
79. 	})
80. 	.passthrough();
81. export type RequestPermissionParams = z.infer<typeof RequestPermissionParamsSchema>;
82. 
83. /**
84.  * Minimal schema for sendErrorResponse - only need sessionId from params.
85.  */
86. export const ErrorResponseParamsSchema = z.object({
87. 	sessionId: z.string().optional(),
88. });
89. 
90. /**
91.  * Parses unknown payload into a validated JSON-RPC request.
92.  */
93. export function parseInboundRequest(payload: unknown): Result<JsonRpcRequest, AcpError> {
94. 	const result = JsonRpcRequestSchema.safeParse(payload);
95. 	return result.success
96. 		? ok(result.data)
97. 		: err(zodErrorToProtocolError(result.error, "Invalid JSON-RPC request"));
98. }
99. 
100. /**
101.  * Parses unknown params into validated RequestPermissionParams.
102.  */
103. export function parseRequestPermissionParams(
104. 	params: unknown
105. ): Result<RequestPermissionParams, AcpError> {
106. 	const result = RequestPermissionParamsSchema.safeParse(params);
107. 	return result.success
108. 		? ok(result.data)
109. 		: err(zodErrorToProtocolError(result.error, "Invalid requestPermission params"));
110. }
111.
```

</details>
