```
37.             .into_iter()
38.             .next()

45.

51.         "claude"
52.     }
53.

56.             || command_exists("claude")
57.             || command_exists("claude-code")
58.     }
59.

67.                 env: claude_env(),

69.

71.         args.extend([
72.             "--no-session-persistence".to_string(),
73.             "-p".to_string(),
74.             "Return only the exact current model id. Output the raw model id only, with no markdown or explanation."
75.                 .to_string(),
76.         ]);
77. 
78.         vec![SpawnConfig {
79.             command: primary.command,
80.             args,
81.             env: primary.env,
82.         }]
83.     }
84.
```

$ cd /Users/alex/Documents/acepe && git --no-pager show 1c3259cdf:packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/client_factory.rs | sed -n '1,110p'

<details>
<summary>106 lines</summary>

```
use crate::acp::client::AcpClient;
use crate::acp::client_trait::{AgentClient, CommunicationMode};
use crate::acp::error::{AcpError, AcpResult};
use crate::acp::opencode::{OpenCodeHttpClient, OpenCodeManagerRegistry};
use crate::acp::registry::AgentRegistry;
use crate::acp::types::CanonicalAgentId;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::sync::Arc;
use tauri::AppHandle;
use tokio::time::{timeout, Duration};

const CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);

/// Create a new client for the given agent.
///
/// The `cwd` parameter specifies the working directory where the subprocess will be spawned.
/// This ensures the agent runs in the correct project/worktree directory.
pub async fn create_client(
    registry: &AgentRegistry,
    opencode_manager_registry: &Arc<OpenCodeManagerRegistry>,
    agent_id: CanonicalAgentId,
    app_handle: AppHandle,
    cwd: PathBuf,
) -> AcpResult<Box<dyn AgentClient>> {
    tracing::info!(agent_id = %agent_id.as_str(), cwd = %cwd.display(), "Creating new client for session");

    let provider = registry
        .get(&agent_id)
        .ok_or_else(|| AcpError::AgentNotFound(agent_id.as_str().to_string()))?
        .clone();

    if crate::acp::agent_installer::is_installable(&agent_id) && !provider.is_available() {
        tracing::info!(
            agent_id = %agent_id.as_str(),
            "Managed agent not available; installing before client creation"

        crate::acp::agent_installer::install_agent(agent_id.clone(), app_handle.clone()).await?;
    }

    let client: Box<dyn AgentClient> = match provider.communication_mode() {
        CommunicationMode::Subprocess => {
            tracing::debug!(
                "Creating ACP client for {} in {}",
                agent_id.as_str(),
                cwd.display()
            );
            let mut client = AcpClient::new_with_provider(provider, Some(app_handle), cwd)
                .map_err(|e| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to create ACP client: {}", e))
                })?;
            timeout(CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT, client.start())
                .await
                .map_err(|_| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState("Client start timed out after 30s".to_string())
                })?
                .map_err(|e| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to start ACP client: {}", e))
                })?;
            Box::new(client)
        }
        CommunicationMode::CcSdk => {
            tracing::debug!(
                "Creating cc-sdk client for {} in {}",
                agent_id.as_str(),
                cwd.display()
            );
            let mut client = crate::acp::client::cc_sdk_client::CcSdkClaudeClient::new(
                provider, app_handle, cwd,
            )?;
            timeout(CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT, client.start())
                .await
                .map_err(|_| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState("cc-sdk client start timed out after 30s".to_string())
                })?
                .map_err(|e| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to start cc-sdk client: {}", e))
                })?;
            Box::new(client)
        }
        CommunicationMode::CodexNative => {
            tracing::debug!(
                "Creating native Codex client for {} in {}",
                agent_id.as_str(),
                cwd.display()
            );
            let mut client = crate::acp::client::codex_native_client::CodexNativeClient::new(
                provider, app_handle, cwd,
            )?;
            timeout(CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT, client.start())
                .await
                .map_err(|_| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState(
                        "Native Codex client start timed out after 30s".to_string(),
                    )
                })?
                .map_err(|e| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to start native Codex client: {}", e))
                })?;
            Box::new(client)
        }
        CommunicationMode::Http => {
            let (project_key, manager) = opencode_manager_registry
                .get_or_start(&cwd)
                .await
                .map_err(|error| {
                    AcpError::InvalidState(format!(
                        "Failed to resolve OpenCode manager for {}: {}",
                        cwd.display(),
                        error
                    ))
```

```
//! cc-sdk based AgentClient implementation for Claude Code.
//!
//! [`CcSdkClaudeClient`] communicates with the Claude Code CLI via the `cc_sdk` Rust
//! crate directly — no Bun subprocess or JSON-RPC stdio indirection.

fn stable_json_signature(value: &Value) -> String {
    match value {
        Value::Null => "null".to_string(),
        Value::Bool(boolean) => boolean.to_string(),
        Value::Number(number) => number.to_string(),
        Value::String(string) => serde_json::to_string(string).unwrap_or_default(),
        Value::Array(items) => {
            let parts = items
                .iter()
                .map(stable_json_signature)
                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
                .join(",");
            format!("[{parts}]")
        }
        Value::Object(object) => {
            let parts = object
                .iter()
                .map(|(key, item)| (key.as_str(), stable_json_signature(item)))
                .collect::<std::collections::BTreeMap<_, _>>()
                .into_iter()
                .map(|(key, item)| {
```

```
It’s **primarily misnaming plus incomplete extraction**, with a **minor separation-of-concerns smell** — **not** a DRY violation.

**Evidence:**
1. `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/client_factory.rs:40-79` routes only `CommunicationMode::CcSdk` providers into `CcSdkClaudeClient`.
2. `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/providers/claude_code.rs:46-48` is the one that opts into `CommunicationMode::CcSdk`.
3. `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/provider.rs:232-235` defaults every other provider to `Subprocess`, and `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/providers/copilot.rs` does **not** override that.

So **Copilot is not currently using `CcSdkClaudeClient`**. The file has some provider-agnostic logic in it because recent refactors pushed shared parser/provider/model/session seams into that client (`d40364db3`, `4a69cea72`) without renaming the type or extracting a more neutral `CcSdkClient` layer.

**Judgment:**
- **DRY violation?** No. It’s the opposite: reuse was pulled inward to avoid duplication.
- **SoC violation?** Mildly yes. Shared provider-adaptation concerns now live inside a transport-specific Claude client.
- **Main issue?** **Misnaming and unfinished boundary cleanup.** The class/file name still says Claude, but parts of the implementation were generalized for provider-driven behavior.

Important correction: my earlier statement that Copilot was flowing through this file was wrong based on the current code. The real Copilot runtime path is the subprocess ACP client, so the remaining Copilot permission bug should be investigated under `AcpClient`/stdio transport, not `CcSdkClaudeClient`.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 100m 4s</sub>

### ✓ Task Complete

It’s **primarily misnaming plus incomplete extraction**, with a **minor separation-of-concerns smell** — **not** a DRY violation.

**Evidence:**
1. `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/client_factory.rs:40-79` routes only `CommunicationMode::CcSdk` providers into `CcSdkClaudeClient`.
2. `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/providers/claude_code.rs:46-48` is the one that opts into `CommunicationMode::CcSdk`.
3. `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/provider.rs:232-235` defaults every other provider to `Subprocess`, and `packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/providers/copilot.rs` does **not** override that.

So **Copilot is not currently using `CcSdkClaudeClient`**. The file has some provider-agnostic logic in it because recent refactors pushed shared parser/provider/model/session seams into that client (`d40364db3`, `4a69cea72`) without renaming the type or extracting a more neutral `CcSdkClient` layer.

**Judgment:**
- **DRY violation?** No. It’s the opposite: reuse was pulled inward to avoid duplication.
- **SoC violation?** Mildly yes. Shared provider-adaptation concerns now live inside a transport-specific Claude client.
- **Main issue?** **Misnaming and unfinished boundary cleanup.** The class/file name still says Claude, but parts of the implementation were generalized for provider-driven behavior.

Important correction: my earlier statement that Copilot was flowing through this file was wrong based on the current code. The real Copilot runtime path is the subprocess ACP client, so the remaining Copilot permission bug should be investigated under `AcpClient`/stdio transport, not `CcSdkClaudeClient`.

```
98.     agent_id: &str,
99.     mut base_env: HashMap<String, String>,
100.     overrides: &AgentEnvOverrides,
101. ) -> HashMap<String, String> {
102.     let Some(agent_overrides) = overrides.get(agent_id) else {
103.         return base_env;
104.     };
105. 
106.     for (key, value) in agent_overrides {
107.         if is_protected_agent_env_override_key(key) {
108.             continue;
109.         }
110.         base_env.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());
111.     }
112. 
113.     base_env
114. }
115. 
116. /// ACP Protocol method names
117. /// See: https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/
118. mod acp_methods {
119.     pub const INITIALIZE: &str = "initialize";
120.     pub const AUTHENTICATE: &str = "authenticate";
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>110 lines</summary>

```
1. use crate::acp::client::AcpClient;
2. use crate::acp::client_trait::{AgentClient, CommunicationMode};
3. use crate::acp::error::{AcpError, AcpResult};
4. use crate::acp::opencode::{OpenCodeHttpClient, OpenCodeManagerRegistry};
5. use crate::acp::registry::AgentRegistry;
6. use crate::acp::types::CanonicalAgentId;
7. use std::path::PathBuf;
8. use std::sync::Arc;
9. use tauri::AppHandle;
10. use tokio::time::{timeout, Duration};
11. 
12. const CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);
13. 
14. /// Create a new client for the given agent.
15. ///
16. /// The `cwd` parameter specifies the working directory where the subprocess will be spawned.
17. /// This ensures the agent runs in the correct project/worktree directory.
18. pub async fn create_client(
19.     registry: &AgentRegistry,
20.     opencode_manager_registry: &Arc<OpenCodeManagerRegistry>,
21.     agent_id: CanonicalAgentId,
22.     app_handle: AppHandle,
23.     cwd: PathBuf,
24. ) -> AcpResult<Box<dyn AgentClient>> {
25.     tracing::info!(agent_id = %agent_id.as_str(), cwd = %cwd.display(), "Creating new client for session");
26. 
27.     let provider = registry
28.         .get(&agent_id)
29.         .ok_or_else(|| AcpError::AgentNotFound(agent_id.as_str().to_string()))?
30.         .clone();
31. 
32.     if crate::acp::agent_installer::can_auto_install(&agent_id) && !provider.is_available() {
33.         tracing::info!(
34.             agent_id = %agent_id.as_str(),
35.             "Managed agent not available; installing before client creation"
36.         );
37.         crate::acp::agent_installer::install_agent(agent_id.clone(), app_handle.clone()).await?;
38.     }
39. 
40.     let client: Box<dyn AgentClient> = match provider.communication_mode() {
41.         CommunicationMode::Subprocess => {
42.             tracing::debug!(
43.                 "Creating ACP client for {} in {}",
44.                 agent_id.as_str(),
45.                 cwd.display()
46.             );
47.             let mut client = AcpClient::new_with_provider(provider, Some(app_handle), cwd)
48.                 .map_err(|e| {
49.                     AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to create ACP client: {}", e))
50.                 })?;
51.             timeout(CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT, client.start())
52.                 .await
53.                 .map_err(|_| {
54.                     AcpError::InvalidState("Client start timed out after 30s".to_string())
55.                 })?
56.                 .map_err(|e| {
57.                     AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to start ACP client: {}", e))
58.                 })?;
59.             Box::new(client)
60.         }
61.         CommunicationMode::CcSdk => {
62.             tracing::debug!(
63.                 "Creating cc-sdk client for {} in {}",
64.                 agent_id.as_str(),
65.                 cwd.display()
66.             );
67.             let mut client = crate::acp::client::cc_sdk_client::CcSdkClaudeClient::new(
68.                 provider, app_handle, cwd,
69.             )?;
70.             timeout(CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT, client.start())
71.                 .await
72.                 .map_err(|_| {
73.                     AcpError::InvalidState("cc-sdk client start timed out after 30s".to_string())
74.                 })?
75.                 .map_err(|e| {
76.                     AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to start cc-sdk client: {}", e))
77.                 })?;
78.             Box::new(client)
79.         }
80.         CommunicationMode::CodexNative => {
81.             tracing::debug!(
82.                 "Creating native Codex client for {} in {}",
83.                 agent_id.as_str(),
84.                 cwd.display()
85.             );
86.             let mut client = crate::acp::client::codex_native_client::CodexNativeClient::new(
87.                 provider, app_handle, cwd,
88.             )?;
89.             timeout(CLIENT_START_TIMEOUT, client.start())
90.                 .await
91.                 .map_err(|_| {
92.                     AcpError::InvalidState(
93.                         "Native Codex client start timed out after 30s".to_string(),
94.                     )
95.                 })?
96.                 .map_err(|e| {
97.                     AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to start native Codex client: {}", e))
98.                 })?;
99.             Box::new(client)
100.         }
101.         CommunicationMode::Http => {
102.             let (project_key, manager) = opencode_manager_registry
103.                 .get_or_start(&cwd)
104.                 .await
105.                 .map_err(|error| {
106.                     AcpError::InvalidState(format!(
107.                         "Failed to resolve OpenCode manager for {}: {}",
108.                         cwd.display(),
109.                         error
110.                     ))
```

```
1. use crate::acp::client::PendingRequestEntry;
2. use crate::acp::domain_events::SessionDomainEventKind;
3. use crate::acp::error::{AcpError, AcpResult};
4. use crate::acp::permission_tracker::PermissionTracker;
5. use crate::acp::projections::{
6.     InteractionKind, InteractionResponse, InteractionState, ProjectionRegistry,
7. };
8. use crate::acp::provider::AgentProvider;
9. use crate::acp::provider_extensions::InboundResponseAdapter;
10. use crate::acp::session_update::{SessionUpdate, ToolCallStatus, ToolCallUpdateData};
11. use crate::acp::ui_event_dispatcher::{AcpUiEvent, AcpUiEventDispatcher};
12. use crate::db::repository::SessionJournalEventRepository;
13. use sea_orm::DbConn;
14. use serde_json::{json, Value};
15. use std::collections::HashMap;
16. use std::sync::Arc as StdArc;
17. use tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt;
18. use tokio::process::ChildStdin;
19. use tokio::sync::Mutex;
20. 
21. pub(crate) fn truncate_for_log(line: &str, max_bytes: usize) -> String {
22.     if line.len() <= max_bytes {
23.         return line.to_string();
24.     }
25.     let head: String = line.chars().take(max_bytes).collect();
26.     format!("{head}… [truncated {} bytes]", line.len() - max_bytes)
27. }
28. 
29. fn subprocess_exit_error_response(reason: &str) -> Value {
30.     json!({
31.         "jsonrpc": "2.0",
32.         "error": {
33.             "code": -32001,
34.             "message": reason
35.         }
36.     })
37. }
38. 
39. pub(crate) async fn fail_pending_requests(
40.     pending: &StdArc<Mutex<HashMap<u64, PendingRequestEntry>>>,
41.     process_generation: u64,
42.     reason: &str,
43. ) {
44.     let mut locked = pending.lock().await;
45.     let failed_ids: Vec<u64> = locked
46.         .iter()
47.         .filter_map(|(id, entry)| {
48.             if entry.generation == process_generation {
49.                 Some(*id)
50.             } else {
51.                 None
52.             }
53.         })
54.         .collect();
55.     if failed_ids.is_empty() {
56.         return;
57.     }
58. 
59.     let response = subprocess_exit_error_response(reason);
60.     let pending_count = failed_ids.len();
61.     for id in failed_ids {
62.         let Some(entry) = locked.remove(&id) else {
63.             continue;
64.         };
65.         let _ = entry.sender.send(response.clone());
66.         tracing::warn!(id, reason = %reason, "Failing pending request due to subprocess termination");
67.     }
68.     tracing::warn!(pending_count, reason = %reason, "Failed pending ACP requests after subprocess termination");
69. }
70. 
71. pub(crate) fn drain_permissions_as_failed(
72.     permission_tracker: &StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<PermissionTracker>>,
73.     dispatcher: &AcpUiEventDispatcher,
74. ) {
75.     let drained = match permission_tracker.lock() {
76.         Ok(mut tracker) => tracker.drain_all(),
77.         Err(e) => {
78.             tracing::error!("Permission tracker mutex poisoned in drain: {e}");
79.             return;
80.         }
81.     };
82.     for (_request_id, ctx) in drained {
83.         dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(SessionUpdate::ToolCallUpdate {
84.             update: ToolCallUpdateData {
85.                 tool_call_id: ctx.tool_call_id,
86.                 status: Some(ToolCallStatus::Failed),
87.                 failure_reason: Some("Agent subprocess terminated".into()),
88.                 ..Default::default()
89.             },
90.             session_id: Some(ctx.session_id),
91.         }));
92.     }
93. }
94. 
95. pub(crate) async fn write_serialized_line(
96.     stdin_writer: &StdArc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>,
97.     payload: &str,
98. ) -> AcpResult<()> {
99.     let mut guard = stdin_writer.lock().await;
100.     let stdin = guard.as_mut().ok_or(AcpError::ClientNotStarted)?;
101.     stdin
102.         .write_all(payload.as_bytes())
103.         .await
104.         .map_err(|e| AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to write to stdin: {}", e)))?;
105.     stdin
106.         .write_all(b"\n")
107.         .await
108.         .map_err(|e| AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to write newline to stdin: {}", e)))?;
109.     stdin
110.         .flush()
111.         .await
112.         .map_err(|e| AcpError::InvalidState(format!("Failed to flush stdin: {}", e)))?;
113.     Ok(())
114. }
115. 
116. pub(crate) async fn send_inbound_response(
117.     stdin_writer: &StdArc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>,
118.     id: u64,
119.     result: Value,
120. ) -> AcpResult<()> {
121.     let response = json!({
122.         "jsonrpc": "2.0",
123.         "id": id,
124.         "result": result
125.     });
126. 
127.     let response_str = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(AcpError::SerializationError)?;
128.     write_serialized_line(stdin_writer, &response_str).await
129. }
130. 
131. #[derive(Clone)]
132. pub(crate) struct InboundRequestResponder {
133.     pub session_id: String,
134.     pub provider: Option<StdArc<dyn AgentProvider>>,
135.     pub db: Option<DbConn>,
136.     pub stdin_writer: StdArc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>,
137.     pub permission_tracker: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<PermissionTracker>>,
138.     pub projection_registry: StdArc<ProjectionRegistry>,
139.     pub dispatcher: AcpUiEventDispatcher,
140.     pub inbound_response_adapters: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<u64, InboundResponseAdapter>>>,
141. }
142. 
143. impl InboundRequestResponder {
144.     pub async fn respond(&self, id: u64, result: Value) -> AcpResult<()> {
145.         let adapted_result = match self.inbound_response_adapters.lock() {
146.             Ok(mut adapters) => adapters
147.                 .remove(&id)
148.                 .map(|adapter| {
149.                     self.provider
150.                         .as_ref()
151.                         .map(|provider| provider.adapt_inbound_response(&adapter, &result))
152.                         .unwrap_or_else(|| result.clone())
153.                 })
154.                 .unwrap_or(result.clone()),
155.             Err(error) => {
156.                 tracing::error!("Inbound response adapter mutex poisoned in resolve: {error}");
157.                 result.clone()
158.             }
159.         };
160. 
161.         send_inbound_response(&self.stdin_writer, id, adapted_result.clone()).await?;
162.         self.update_interaction_projection(id, &adapted_result)
163.             .await;
164. 
165.         let ctx = match self.permission_tracker.lock() {
166.             Ok(mut tracker) => tracker.resolve(id),
167.             Err(error) => {
168.                 tracing::error!("Permission tracker mutex poisoned in resolve: {error}");
169.                 None
170.             }
171.         };
172. 
173.         if let Some(ctx) = ctx {
174.             let outcome_str = adapted_result
175.                 .pointer("/outcome/outcome")
176.                 .and_then(|value| value.as_str());
177.             let is_denied = outcome_str.is_some_and(|outcome| outcome == "cancelled");
178. 
179.             if let Some(outcome) = outcome_str {
180.                 if outcome != "cancelled" && outcome != "allowed" && outcome != "selected" {
181.                     tracing::warn!(outcome, tool_call_id = %ctx.tool_call_id, "Unrecognized permission outcome");
182.                 }
183.             }
184. 
185.             if is_denied {
186.                 self.dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(
187.                     SessionUpdate::ToolCallUpdate {
188.                         update: ToolCallUpdateData {
189.                             tool_call_id: ctx.tool_call_id,
190.                             status: Some(ToolCallStatus::Failed),
191.                             failure_reason: Some("Permission denied by user".into()),
192.                             ..Default::default()
193.                         },
194.                         session_id: Some(ctx.session_id),
195.                     },
196.                 ));
197.             }
198.         }
199. 
200.         Ok(())
201.     }
202. 
203.     async fn update_interaction_projection(&self, request_id: u64, adapted_result: &Value) {
204.         apply_interaction_response_for_request(
205.             &self.projection_registry,
206.             self.db.as_ref(),
207.             Some(&self.dispatcher),
208.             &self.session_id,
209.             request_id,
210.             adapted_result,
211.             "inbound responder",
212.         )
213.         .await;
214.     }
215. }
216. 
217. pub(crate) fn interaction_transition_from_result(
218.     interaction_kind: &InteractionKind,
219.     adapted_result: &Value,
220. ) -> Option<(
221.     InteractionState,
222.     SessionDomainEventKind,
223.     InteractionResponse,
224. )> {
225.     match interaction_kind {
226.         InteractionKind::Permission => {
227.             let outcome = adapted_result
228.                 .pointer("/outcome/outcome")
229.                 .and_then(Value::as_str)?;
230.             let option_id = adapted_result
231.                 .pointer("/outcome/optionId")
232.                 .and_then(Value::as_str)
233.                 .map(ToString::to_string);
234.             let accepted = matches!(outcome, "allowed" | "selected");
235.             let state = if accepted {
236.                 InteractionState::Approved
237.             } else if outcome == "cancelled" {
238.                 InteractionState::Rejected
239.             } else {
240.                 return None;
241.             };
242.             let domain_event_kind = if accepted {
243.                 SessionDomainEventKind::InteractionResolved
244.             } else {
245.                 SessionDomainEventKind::InteractionCancelled
246.             };
247.             Some((
248.                 state,
249.                 domain_event_kind,
250.                 InteractionResponse::Permission {
251.                     accepted,
252.                     option_id,
253.                     reply: None,
254.                 },
255.             ))
256.         }
257.         InteractionKind::Question => {
258.             let outcome = adapted_result
259.                 .pointer("/outcome/outcome")
260.                 .and_then(Value::as_str)?;
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 111m 33s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The live Copilot session is again showing the same reconnect pattern on the real path: `Buffered session update while disconnected` followed by `Provider model capabilities on session resume`. I’m drilling into the subprocess lifecycle now, especially where Acepe treats a disconnect as “agent subprocess terminated,” because that can kill pending permission state before the UI reply lands.

<summary>198 lines</summary>

```
1. use std::collections::HashMap;
2. 
3. /// Tracks in-flight permission requests so we can emit synthetic
4. /// `ToolCallUpdate(failed)` events when the user denies a permission.
5. ///
6. /// Populated when a permission request is forwarded to the UI with a
7. /// `SyntheticToolCallHint`. Consumed when the UI responds (allow or deny).
8. /// Cleaned up on subprocess death or session teardown.
9. #[derive(Debug, Default)]
10. pub(crate) struct PermissionTracker {
11.     /// Maps JSON-RPC request ID → permission context.
12.     contexts: HashMap<u64, PermissionContext>,
13. }
14. 
15. /// Context needed to emit a synthetic failed tool call update on deny.
16. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
17. pub(crate) struct PermissionContext {
18.     pub session_id: String,
19.     pub tool_call_id: String,
20. }
21. 
22. impl PermissionTracker {
23.     pub fn new() -> Self {
24.         Self::default()
25.     }
26. 
27.     /// Track a permission request that was forwarded to the UI.
28.     pub fn track(&mut self, request_id: u64, ctx: PermissionContext) {
29.         self.contexts.insert(request_id, ctx);
30.     }
31. 
32.     /// Resolve (consume) a tracked permission by request ID.
33.     /// Returns the context if it was tracked, removing it from the map.
34.     pub fn resolve(&mut self, request_id: u64) -> Option<PermissionContext> {
35.         self.contexts.remove(&request_id)
36.     }
37. 
38.     /// Drain all tracked permissions. Returns all contexts for cleanup emission.
39.     pub fn drain_all(&mut self) -> Vec<(u64, PermissionContext)> {
40.         self.contexts.drain().collect()
41.     }
42. }
43. 
44. /// Deduplicates web search tool calls by remapping permission-created IDs
45. /// to the canonical notification-created IDs.
46. ///
47. /// When Cursor performs a web search, two events arrive with different IDs:
48. /// - Notification: toolCall with ID like `tool_7319769b-...`
49. /// - Permission: session/request_permission with toolCallId like `web_search_0`
50. ///
51. /// This tracker records notification IDs keyed by (session_id, query), then
52. /// provides the canonical ID when the permission arrives so the synthetic
53. /// ToolCall uses the same ID — preventing a duplicate UI row.
54. ///
55. /// Accessed exclusively from the stdout reader task.
56. #[derive(Debug, Default)]
57. pub(crate) struct WebSearchDedup {
58.     /// Maps (session_id, query) → [notification_tool_call_ids] (FIFO).
59.     /// Vec handles the edge case of parallel searches with identical queries.
60.     entries: HashMap<(String, String), Vec<String>>,
61. }
62. 
63. impl WebSearchDedup {
64.     pub fn new() -> Self {
65.         Self::default()
66.     }
67. 
68.     /// Record a web search notification's tool-call ID keyed by (session_id, query).
69.     pub fn record(&mut self, session_id: String, query: String, tool_call_id: String) {
70.         self.entries
71.             .entry((session_id, query))
72.             .or_default()
73.             .push(tool_call_id);
74.     }
75. 
76.     /// Take the first recorded canonical ID for this (session, query).
77.     /// Consumes the entry (FIFO order handles parallel identical queries).
78.     pub fn take(&mut self, session_id: &str, query: &str) -> Option<String> {
79.         let key = (session_id.to_string(), query.to_string());
80.         let ids = self.entries.get_mut(&key)?;
81.         if ids.is_empty() {
82.             self.entries.remove(&key);
83.             return None;
84.         }
85.         let id = ids.remove(0);
86.         if ids.is_empty() {
87.             self.entries.remove(&key);
88.         }
89.         Some(id)
90.     }
91. 
92.     /// Drain all entries. Called on EOF / error / death-monitor cleanup.
93.     pub fn drain_all(&mut self) {
94.         self.entries.clear();
95.     }
96. }
97. 
98. #[cfg(test)]
99. mod tests {
100.     use super::*;
101. 
102.     #[test]
103.     fn track_and_resolve() {
104.         let mut tracker = PermissionTracker::new();
105.         tracker.track(
106.             1,
107.             PermissionContext {
108.                 session_id: "s1".into(),
109.                 tool_call_id: "tc1".into(),
110.             },
111.         );
112. 
113.         let ctx = tracker.resolve(1);
114.         assert!(ctx.is_some());
115.         assert_eq!(ctx.unwrap().tool_call_id, "tc1");
116. 
117.         // Second resolve returns None
118.         assert!(tracker.resolve(1).is_none());
119.     }
120. 
121.     #[test]
122.     fn resolve_unknown_returns_none() {
123.         let mut tracker = PermissionTracker::new();
124.         assert!(tracker.resolve(42).is_none());
125.     }
126. 
127.     #[test]
128.     fn drain_all_empties_tracker() {
129.         let mut tracker = PermissionTracker::new();
130.         tracker.track(
131.             1,
132.             PermissionContext {
133.                 session_id: "s1".into(),
134.                 tool_call_id: "tc1".into(),
135.             },
136.         );
137.         tracker.track(
138.             2,
139.             PermissionContext {
140.                 session_id: "s2".into(),
141.                 tool_call_id: "tc2".into(),
142.             },
143.         );
144. 
145.         let drained = tracker.drain_all();
146.         assert_eq!(drained.len(), 2);
147.         assert!(tracker.resolve(1).is_none());
148.         assert!(tracker.resolve(2).is_none());
149.     }
150. 
151.     // --- WebSearchDedup tests ---
152. 
153.     #[test]
154.     fn web_search_dedup_record_and_take() {
155.         let mut dedup = WebSearchDedup::new();
156.         dedup.record("s1".into(), "rust lang".into(), "tool_abc".into());
157.         let result = dedup.take("s1", "rust lang");
158.         assert_eq!(result, Some("tool_abc".to_string()));
159.         // Second take returns None
160.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s1", "rust lang"), None);
161.     }
162. 
163.     #[test]
164.     fn web_search_dedup_take_unknown_key_returns_none() {
165.         let mut dedup = WebSearchDedup::new();
166.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s1", "nonexistent"), None);
167.     }
168. 
169.     #[test]
170.     fn web_search_dedup_fifo_ordering_for_duplicate_queries() {
171.         let mut dedup = WebSearchDedup::new();
172.         dedup.record("s1".into(), "rust".into(), "tool_first".into());
173.         dedup.record("s1".into(), "rust".into(), "tool_second".into());
174.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s1", "rust"), Some("tool_first".to_string()));
175.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s1", "rust"), Some("tool_second".to_string()));
176.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s1", "rust"), None);
177.     }
178. 
179.     #[test]
180.     fn web_search_dedup_drain_all_empties() {
181.         let mut dedup = WebSearchDedup::new();
182.         dedup.record("s1".into(), "query1".into(), "tool_1".into());
183.         dedup.record("s2".into(), "query2".into(), "tool_2".into());
184.         dedup.drain_all();
185.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s1", "query1"), None);
186.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("s2", "query2"), None);
187.     }
188. 
189.     #[test]
190.     fn web_search_dedup_different_sessions_independent() {
191.         let mut dedup = WebSearchDedup::new();
192.         dedup.record("session_a".into(), "query".into(), "tool_A".into());
193.         dedup.record("session_b".into(), "query".into(), "tool_B".into());
194.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("session_a", "query"), Some("tool_A".to_string()));
195.         assert_eq!(dedup.take("session_b", "query"), Some("tool_B".to_string()));
196.     }
197. }
198.
```

<sub>⏱️ 111m 43s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

There’s a strong parallel here to the earlier wrong-path diagnosis: on subprocess teardown, Acepe explicitly drains tracked permissions and marks their tool calls failed as **“Agent subprocess terminated.”** That makes the key question whether Copilot is briefly losing its stdio process/session during resume, or whether Acepe is overreacting to a transient disconnect. I’m tracing where those permission contexts get registered and where resume/teardown fires.

```
252.     session_registry: &SessionRegistry,
253.     session_id: &str,
254.     request_id: u64,
255.     result: Value,
256. ) -> Result<(), SerializableAcpError> {
257.     if let Ok(client_mutex) = session_registry.get(session_id) {
258.         let client_guard =
259.             lock_session_client(&client_mutex, "acp_respond_inbound_request: lock").await?;
260.

262.             INBOUND_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT,
263.             client_guard.respond(request_id, result),
264.         )
265.         .await
266.         .map_err(|_| SerializableAcpError::Timeout {
267.             operation: "acp_respond_inbound_request: operation".to_string(),
268.         })?

270. 
271.         return Ok(());

287.     })?
288.     .map_err(SerializableAcpError::from)?;
289. 
290.     Ok(())
```

```
1. use super::*;
2. use crate::acp::client_loop::read_stderr_buffer;
3. use tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken;
4. 
5. impl AcpClient {
6.     pub(super) fn has_next_spawn_config(&self) -> bool {
7.         self.provider
8.             .as_ref()
9.             .is_some_and(|provider| self.spawn_config_index + 1 < provider.spawn_configs().len())
10.     }
11. 
12.     pub(super) fn advance_spawn_config(&mut self) -> bool {
13.         if self.has_next_spawn_config() {
14.             self.spawn_config_index += 1;
15.             return true;
16.         }
17.         false
18.     }
19. 
20.     /// Start the ACP agent subprocess
21.     ///
22.     /// Uses tokio::process::Command for non-blocking subprocess spawning.
23.     /// This prevents blocking the async executor during subprocess startup.
24.     pub async fn start(&mut self) -> AcpResult<()> {
25.         tracing::info!("Starting ACP client");
26. 
27.         // Get the working directory
28.         let cwd = self.cwd.clone();
29. 
30.         // Note: cwd was already validated by validate_session_cwd() before reaching here.
31.         // Skipping redundant is_dir() to avoid duplicate macOS TCC prompts for protected folders.
32. 
33.         tracing::info!(cwd = %cwd.display(), "ACP client will spawn in working directory");
34. 
35.         // Get the provider's spawn configurations
36.         let provider_id = match &self.provider {
37.             Some(provider) => provider.id().to_string(),
38.             None => {
39.                 // Fallback to claude-code-acp if no provider is set
40.                 tracing::error!("No provider set, using default claude-code-acp");
41.                 return Err(AcpError::NoProviderConfigured);
42.             }
43.         };
44. 
45.         let saved_overrides = if let Some(app_handle) = &self.app_handle {
46.             match load_saved_agent_env_overrides(app_handle).await {
47.                 Ok(saved_overrides) => Some(saved_overrides),
48.                 Err(error) => {
49.                     tracing::warn!(
50.                         agent_id = %provider_id,
51.                         error = %error,
52.                         "Failed to load saved agent env overrides"
53.                     );
54.                     None
55.                 }
56.             }
57.         } else {
58.             None
59.         };
60. 
61.         let agent_type = self
62.             .provider
63.             .as_ref()
64.             .map(|provider| provider.parser_agent_type())
65.             .unwrap_or(AgentType::ClaudeCode);
66. 
67.         loop {
68.             let provider = self
69.                 .provider
70.                 .as_ref()
71.                 .ok_or(AcpError::NoProviderConfigured)?;
72.             let spawn_configs = provider.spawn_configs();
73.             if spawn_configs.is_empty() {
74.                 return Err(AcpError::InvalidState(format!(
75.                     "No launchers available for provider {}. Install the agent or make its CLI available in PATH.",
76.                     provider_id
77.                 )));
78.             }
79.             let total_spawn_configs = spawn_configs.len();
80.             let Some(mut spawn_config) = spawn_configs.get(self.spawn_config_index).cloned() else {
81.                 return Err(AcpError::InvalidState(format!(
82.                     "Spawn config index {} out of range for provider {}",
83.                     self.spawn_config_index, provider_id
84.                 )));
85.             };
86. 
87.             if let Some(saved_overrides) = &saved_overrides {
88.                 spawn_config.env = apply_saved_agent_env_overrides(
89.                     &provider_id,
90.                     spawn_config.env,
91.                     saved_overrides,
92.                 );
93.             }
94. 
95.             tracing::info!(
96.                 command = %spawn_config.command,
97.                 args = ?spawn_config.args,
98.                 cwd = %cwd.display(),
99.                 attempt = self.spawn_config_index + 1,
100.                 total_attempts = total_spawn_configs,
101.                 "Spawning agent subprocess"
102.             );
103. 
104.             let mut cmd = Command::new(&spawn_config.command);
105.             cmd.args(&spawn_config.args);
106.             cmd.current_dir(&cwd);
107. 
108.             // Clear inherited env and set only what the provider explicitly provides.
109.             // Without env_clear(), subprocess inherits full process env, making
110.             // allowlist-based providers (Cursor, OpenCode) ineffective.
111.             cmd.env_clear();
112.             for (key, value) in &spawn_config.env {
113.                 cmd.env(key, value);
114.             }
115. 
116.             // On Unix, spawn in a new process group (setsid) so we can kill the entire
117.             // subprocess tree when stopping the client. Without this, child processes
118.             // become orphans when we kill the immediate child.
119.             #[cfg(unix)]
120.             unsafe {
121.                 cmd.pre_exec(|| {
122.                     nix::unistd::setsid().map_err(std::io::Error::other)?;
123.                     Ok(())
124.                 });
125.             }
126. 
127.             let command_str = spawn_config.command.clone();
128.             let mut child = match cmd
129.                 .stdin(Stdio::piped())
130.                 .stdout(Stdio::piped())
131.                 .stderr(Stdio::piped())
132.                 .spawn()
133.             {
134.                 Ok(child) => child,
135.                 Err(error) => {
136.                     let redacted_cmd = command_str
137.                         .split_whitespace()
138.                         .next()
139.                         .unwrap_or("unknown")
140.                         .to_string();
141.                     tracing::error!(
142.                         command = %redacted_cmd,
143.                         error = %error,
144.                         attempt = self.spawn_config_index + 1,
145.                         total_attempts = total_spawn_configs,
146.                         "Failed to spawn subprocess"
147.                     );
148. 
149.                     if self.advance_spawn_config() {
150.                         tracing::warn!(
151.                             agent_id = %provider_id,
152.                             next_attempt = self.spawn_config_index + 1,
153.                             total_attempts = total_spawn_configs,
154.                             "Retrying agent startup with fallback launcher after spawn failure"
155.                         );
156.                         continue;
157.                     }
158. 
159.                     return Err(AcpError::SubprocessSpawnFailed {
160.                         command: command_str,
161.                         source: error,
162.                     });
163.                 }
164.             };
165. 
166.             tracing::info!(pid = ?child.id(), "Subprocess spawned successfully");
167. 
168.             // Store the process group ID (same as PID since we used setsid)
169.             #[cfg(unix)]
170.             {
171.                 self.pgid = child.id();
172.                 tracing::info!(pgid = ?self.pgid, "Subprocess process group stored for cleanup");
173.             }
174. 
175.             // Store stdin writer for sending responses to inbound requests
176.             let stdin = child.stdin.take();
177.             {
178.                 let mut guard = self.stdin_writer.lock().await;
179.                 *guard = stdin;
180.             }
181. 
182.             // Spawn a tokio task to read from stderr and log errors
183.             let stderr = child.stderr.take().ok_or_else(|| {
184.                 AcpError::InvalidState("Failed to get stderr handle from subprocess".to_string())
185.             })?;
186.             let stderr_buffer = crate::acp::client_loop::new_stderr_buffer();
187.             self.stderr_buffer = Some(stderr_buffer.clone());
188.             spawn_stderr_reader(
189.                 stderr,
190.                 MAX_LOGGED_SUBPROCESS_LINE_BYTES,
191.                 stderr_buffer.clone(),
192.             );
193. 
194.             // Spawn a tokio task to read from stdout asynchronously
195.             let stdout = child.stdout.take().ok_or_else(|| {
196.                 AcpError::InvalidState("Failed to get stdout handle from subprocess".to_string())
197.             })?;
198.             let app_handle = self.app_handle.clone();
199.             let dispatcher =
200.                 AcpUiEventDispatcher::new(app_handle.clone(), DispatchPolicy::default());
201.             self.dispatcher = Some(dispatcher.clone());
202.             self.process_generation += 1;
203.             let process_generation = self.process_generation;
204.             let stdout_cancel = CancellationToken::new();
205.             self.stdout_reader_cancel = stdout_cancel.clone();
206.             spawn_stdout_reader(
207.                 stdout,
208.                 StdoutLoopContext {
209.                     process_generation,
210.                     pending: self.pending_requests.clone(),
211.                     stdin_writer: self.stdin_writer.clone(),
212.                     prompt_sessions: self.prompt_request_sessions.clone(),
213.                     app_handle: app_handle.clone(),
214.                     dispatcher: dispatcher.clone(),
215.                     permission_tracker: self.permission_tracker.clone(),
216.                     web_search_dedup: self.web_search_dedup.clone(),
217.                     active_session_id: self.active_session_id.clone(),
218.                     inbound_response_adapters: self.inbound_response_adapters.clone(),
219.                     is_replay_active: self.is_replay_active.clone(),
220.                     provider: self.provider.clone(),
```

</details>

```
39. /// Maximum number of stderr lines to retain in the buffer.
40. const STDERR_BUFFER_MAX_LINES: usize = 20;
41. 
42. /// Shared buffer that captures recent stderr output from the subprocess.
43. /// When the subprocess dies, the last lines are included in the error message
44. /// so users can see WHY it crashed, not just that it crashed.
45. pub(crate) type StderrBuffer = StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<VecDeque<String>>>;
46. 
47. /// Create a new empty stderr buffer.
48. pub(crate) fn new_stderr_buffer() -> StderrBuffer {
49.     StdArc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(VecDeque::with_capacity(
50.         STDERR_BUFFER_MAX_LINES,
51.     )))
52. }
53. 
54. /// Read the last lines from the stderr buffer, joined with newlines.
55. /// Returns None if the buffer is empty.
56. pub(crate) fn read_stderr_buffer(buffer: &StderrBuffer) -> Option<String> {
57.     let guard = buffer.lock().ok()?;
58.     if guard.is_empty() {
59.         return None;
60.     }
61.     Some(guard.iter().cloned().collect::<Vec<_>>().join("\n"))
62. }
63. 
64. /// Wrapper that flushes the StreamingDeltaBatcher on drop.
65. /// Ensures buffered deltas are emitted even if the task panics or is cancelled.
66. pub(crate) struct BatcherWithGuard {
67.     batcher: StreamingDeltaBatcher,
68.     dispatcher: AcpUiEventDispatcher,
69. }
70. 
71. impl BatcherWithGuard {
72.     fn new(dispatcher: AcpUiEventDispatcher) -> Self {
73.         Self {
74.             batcher: StreamingDeltaBatcher::new(),
75.             dispatcher,
76.         }
77.     }
78. 
79.     #[cfg(test)]
80.     pub(crate) fn new_for_tests(dispatcher: AcpUiEventDispatcher) -> Self {
81.         Self::new(dispatcher)
82.     }
83. 
84.     pub(crate) fn process(&mut self, update: SessionUpdate) -> Vec<SessionUpdate> {
85.         self.batcher.process(update)
86.     }
87. 
88.     fn process_turn_complete(&mut self, session_id: &str) -> Vec<SessionUpdate> {
89.         self.batcher.process_turn_complete(session_id)
90.     }
91. 
92.     fn process_turn_error(
93.         &mut self,
94.         session_id: &str,
95.         error: crate::acp::session_update::TurnErrorData,
96.     ) -> Vec<SessionUpdate> {
97.         self.batcher.process_turn_error(session_id, error)
98.     }
99. 
100.     /// Flush all buffered updates (e.g. before circuit breaker break).
101.     pub(crate) fn flush_all(&mut self) -> Vec<SessionUpdate> {
102.         self.batcher.flush_all()
103.     }
104. }
105. 
106. /// Drain all fire-and-forget prompt sessions and synthesize TurnError events.
107. ///
108. /// Uses the batcher to ensure buffered text chunks are flushed before TurnError
109. /// (`process_turn_error` internally calls `flush_session` then appends TurnError).
110. ///
111. /// Deduplicates by session_id to avoid sending multiple TurnErrors for the same
112. /// session when multiple prompts are in-flight (e.g., rapid sends).
113. ///
114. /// Only called from the stdout reader task (which owns the batcher). The death
115. /// monitor does NOT drain prompt_sessions because it has no batcher access — if it
116. /// dispatched TurnError directly, the frontend could receive TurnError BEFORE the
117. /// batcher's Drop-flush delivers final text chunks (ordering violation).
118. async fn drain_prompt_sessions_as_turn_errors(
119.     prompt_sessions: &StdArc<Mutex<HashMap<u64, PromptRequestSession>>>,
120.     process_generation: u64,
121.     streaming_batcher: &mut BatcherWithGuard,
122.     dispatcher: &AcpUiEventDispatcher,
123.     reason: &str,
124. ) {
125.     let mut locked: tokio::sync::MutexGuard<'_, HashMap<u64, PromptRequestSession>> =
126.         prompt_sessions.lock().await;
127.     let drained_ids: Vec<u64> = locked
128.         .iter()
129.         .filter_map(|(id, prompt)| {
130.             if prompt.generation == process_generation {
131.                 Some(*id)
132.             } else {
133.                 None
134.             }
135.         })
136.         .collect();
137.     let drained: HashMap<u64, PromptRequestSession> = drained_ids
138.         .into_iter()
139.         .filter_map(|id| locked.remove(&id).map(|prompt| (id, prompt)))
140.         .collect();
141.     if drained.is_empty() {
142.         return;
143.     }
144. 
145.     let unique_sessions: HashSet<String> = drained
146.         .into_values()
147.         .map(|prompt| prompt.session_id)
148.         .collect();
149.     tracing::warn!(
150.         count = unique_sessions.len(),
151.         reason,
152.         "Synthesizing TurnError for orphaned prompt sessions after subprocess death"
153.     );
154. 
155.     for session_id in unique_sessions {
156.         let error = TurnErrorData::Structured(TurnErrorInfo {
157.             message: reason.to_string(),
158.             kind: TurnErrorKind::Fatal,
159.             code: Some(-32001),
160.             source: Some(TurnErrorSource::Process),
161.         });
162.         let updates = streaming_batcher.process_turn_error(&session_id, error);
163.         for update in updates {
164.             dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(update));
165.         }
166.     }
167. }
168. 
169. impl Drop for BatcherWithGuard {
170.     fn drop(&mut self) {
171.         let updates = self.batcher.flush_all();
172.         if !updates.is_empty() {
173.             tracing::info!(count = updates.len(), "BatcherWithGuard flushing on drop");
174.             for update in updates {
175.                 self.dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(update));
176.             }
177.         }
178.     }
179. }
180. 
181. pub(crate) fn spawn_stderr_reader(
182.     stderr: ChildStderr,
183.     max_logged_line_bytes: usize,
184.     buffer: StderrBuffer,
185. ) {
186.     tokio::spawn(async move {
187.         let reader = BufReader::new(stderr);
188.         let mut lines = reader.lines();
189. 
190.         loop {
191.             match lines.next_line().await {
192.                 Ok(Some(line)) => {
193.                     tracing::warn!(
194.                         stderr = %truncate_for_log(&line, max_logged_line_bytes),
195.                         bytes = line.len(),
196.                         "Subprocess stderr"
197.                     );
198.                     if let Ok(mut buf) = buffer.lock() {
199.                         if buf.len() >= STDERR_BUFFER_MAX_LINES {
200.                             buf.pop_front();
201.                         }
202.                         buf.push_back(line);
203.                     }
204.                 }
205.                 Ok(None) => break,
206.                 Err(error) => {
207.                     tracing::error!(error = %error, "Failed reading subprocess stderr");
208.                     break;
209.                 }
210.             }
211.         }
212.         tracing::debug!("Subprocess stderr reader task ended");
213.     });
214. }
215. 
216. pub(crate) struct StdoutLoopContext {
217.     pub process_generation: u64,
218.     pub pending: StdArc<Mutex<HashMap<u64, PendingRequestEntry>>>,
219.     pub stdin_writer: StdArc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>,
220.     pub prompt_sessions: StdArc<Mutex<HashMap<u64, PromptRequestSession>>>,
221.     pub app_handle: Option<AppHandle>,
222.     pub dispatcher: AcpUiEventDispatcher,
223.     pub permission_tracker: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<PermissionTracker>>,
224.     pub web_search_dedup: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<WebSearchDedup>>,
225.     pub active_session_id: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<String>>>,
226.     pub inbound_response_adapters: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<u64, InboundResponseAdapter>>>,
227.     pub is_replay_active: StdArc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicBool>,
228.     pub provider: Option<StdArc<dyn AgentProvider>>,
229.     pub agent_type: AgentType,
230.     pub max_logged_line_bytes: usize,
231.     pub stderr_buffer: StderrBuffer,
232.     pub cancel: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken,
233. }
234. 
235. pub(crate) struct DeathMonitorContext {
236.     pub process_generation: u64,
237.     pub pending_requests: StdArc<Mutex<HashMap<u64, PendingRequestEntry>>>,
238.     pub permission_tracker: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<PermissionTracker>>,
239.     pub web_search_dedup: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<WebSearchDedup>>,
240.     pub dispatcher: AcpUiEventDispatcher,
241.     pub stderr_buffer: StderrBuffer,
242.     pub cancel: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken,
243. }
244. 
245. pub(crate) fn spawn_stdout_reader(stdout: ChildStdout, ctx: StdoutLoopContext) {
246.     tokio::spawn(async move {
247.         let reader = BufReader::new(stdout);
248.         let mut lines = reader.lines();
249.         let mut streaming_batcher = BatcherWithGuard::new(ctx.dispatcher.clone());
250.         let mut non_streaming_batcher = NonStreamingEventBatcher::new();
251. 
252.         let message_id_tracker: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<String, String>>> =
253.             StdArc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
254.         let assistant_text_tracker: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<String, String>>> =
255.             StdArc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(HashMap::new()));
256.         let task_reconciler: StdArc<std::sync::Mutex<TaskReconciler>> =
257.             StdArc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(TaskReconciler::new()));
258. 
259.         /// Circuit breaker: after this many consecutive JSON parse failures, treat stdout as broken.
260.         /// 20 allows transient noise (e.g. logs) without dropping the connection; tune if subprocess emits non-JSON lines.
```

```
1. use crate::acp::parsers::{get_parser, AgentType};
2. use crate::acp::session_update::{build_tool_call_from_raw, RawToolCallInput, ToolCallStatus};
3. use crate::acp::streaming_log::log_streaming_event;
4. use crate::acp::tool_classification::{resolve_raw_tool_identity, ToolClassificationHints};
5. use serde_json::Value;
6. 
7. use super::helpers::{
8.     build_permission_request_log_payload, parse_params, parse_permission_tool_arguments,
9. };
10. use super::types::SessionRequestPermissionParamsRaw;
11. use super::{InboundRoutingDecision, SyntheticToolCallContext};
12. 
13. pub(super) async fn handle_session_request_permission(
14.     params: &Value,
15.     agent_type: AgentType,
16. ) -> InboundRoutingDecision {
17.     let parsed: SessionRequestPermissionParamsRaw = match parse_params(params) {
18.         Ok(parsed) => parsed,
19.         Err(_) => {
20.             return InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi {
21.                 parsed_arguments: None,
22.                 synthetic_tool_call: None,
23.             };
24.         }
25.     };
26. 
27.     let SessionRequestPermissionParamsRaw {
28.         session_id,
29.         tool_call,
30.     } = parsed;
31. 
32.     let tool_call = match tool_call {
33.         Some(tool_call) => tool_call,
34.         None => {
35.             return InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi {
36.                 parsed_arguments: None,
37.                 synthetic_tool_call: None,
38.             };
39.         }
40.     };
41. 
42.     let parser = get_parser(agent_type);
43.     let tool_call_id = tool_call
44.         .tool_call_id
45.         .clone()
46.         .unwrap_or_else(|| "permission-request".to_string());
47.     let identity = resolve_raw_tool_identity(
48.         parser,
49.         &tool_call_id,
50.         Some(&tool_call.raw_input),
51.         ToolClassificationHints {
52.             name: tool_call.name.as_deref(),
53.             title: tool_call.title.as_deref(),
54.             kind: tool_call
55.                 .kind
56.                 .as_deref()
57.                 .map(|kind| parser.detect_tool_kind(kind)),
58.             kind_hint: tool_call.kind.as_deref(),
59.             locations: None,
60.         },
61.     );
62.     let parsed_arguments = parse_permission_tool_arguments(
63.         &tool_call_id,
64.         tool_call.name.as_deref(),
65.         tool_call.title.as_deref(),
66.         tool_call.raw_input.clone(),
67.         tool_call.kind.as_deref(),
68.         agent_type,
69.     );
70. 
71.     if let Some(session_id) = session_id.as_deref() {
72.         let payload = build_permission_request_log_payload(
73.             "session/request_permission",
74.             params,
75.             tool_call.name.as_deref(),
76.             tool_call.title.as_deref(),
77.             parsed_arguments.as_ref(),
78.             false,
79.             None,
80.         );
81.         log_streaming_event(session_id, &payload);
82.     }
83. 
84.     // Build ToolCallData via the same pipeline as normal tool_call events.
85.     let synthetic_tool_call = match (&tool_call.tool_call_id, &session_id) {
86.         (Some(tool_call_id), Some(_)) => {
87.             let raw = RawToolCallInput {
88.                 id: tool_call_id.clone(),
89.                 name: identity.name.clone(),
90.                 arguments: tool_call.raw_input.clone(),
91.                 status: ToolCallStatus::InProgress,
92.                 kind: Some(identity.kind),
93.                 title: tool_call.title.clone(),
94.                 suppress_title_read_path_hint: false,
95.                 parent_tool_use_id: None,
96.                 task_children: None,
97.             };
98.             let tool_call_data = build_tool_call_from_raw(parser, raw);
99.             Some(Box::new(SyntheticToolCallContext { tool_call_data }))
100.         }
101.         _ => None,
102.     };
103. 
104.     InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi {
105.         parsed_arguments,
106.         synthetic_tool_call,
107.     }
108. }
109.
```

```
400. 
401.                                         // Normalize sub-agent session ID → root session ID.
402.                                         // Sub-agents use internal child session IDs the frontend doesn't know about.
403.                                         if let Some(root_id) = ctx.active_session_id.lock().ok().and_then(|g| g.clone()) {
404.                                             if forwarded.normalize_session_id(Some(&root_id)) {
405.                                                 tracing::debug!(root_session_id = %root_id, "Normalizing sub-agent session ID");
406.                                             }
407.                                         }
408. 
409.                                         // Remap web search permission IDs to the canonical notification ID.
410.                                         // The notification arrives with a stable ID (e.g. `tool_7319769b-...`)
411.                                         // but the permission arrives with a different ID (`web_search_0`).
412.                                         // We recorded the notification ID in web_search_dedup; remap here
413.                                         // so the synthetic ToolCall and forwarded JSON use the same ID,
414.                                         // preventing a duplicate UI row.
415.                                         if let Ok(mut dedup) = ctx.web_search_dedup.lock() {
416.                                             if let Some(canonical_id) = remap_forwarded_web_search_tool_call_id(
417.                                                 &mut forwarded,
418.                                                 ctx.provider.as_deref(),
419.                                                 &parsed_arguments,
420.                                                 &mut synthetic_tool_call,
421.                                                 &mut dedup,
422.                                             ) {
423.                                                 tracing::debug!(
424.                                                     canonical_id = %canonical_id,
425.                                                     "Remapped web search permission ID to notification canonical ID"
426.                                                 );
427.                                             }
428.                                         }
429. 
430.                                         if let Some(synthetic_ctx) = &synthetic_tool_call {
431.                                             let session_id = forwarded.session_id();
432. 
433.                                             if let Some(ref sid) = session_id {
434.                                                 let synthetic = SessionUpdate::ToolCall {
435.                                                     tool_call: synthetic_ctx.tool_call_data.clone(),
436.                                                     session_id: Some(sid.clone()),
437.                                                 };
438.                                                 ctx.dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(synthetic));
439. 
440.                                                 match ctx.permission_tracker.lock() {
441.                                                     Ok(mut tracker) => {
442.                                                         tracker.track(id, PermissionContext {
443.                                                             session_id: sid.clone(),
444.                                                             tool_call_id: synthetic_ctx.tool_call_data.id.clone(),
445.                                                         });
446.                                                     }
447.                                                     Err(e) => {
448.                                                         tracing::error!("Permission tracker mutex poisoned in track: {e}");
449.                                                     }
450.                                                 }
451.                                             }
452.                                         }
453. 
454.                                             if let Some(session_id) = forwarded.session_id() {
455.                                                 if let Some(app_handle) = ctx.app_handle.as_ref() {
456.                                                     let db = app_handle.state::<DbConn>();
457.                                                     let registry = app_handle.state::<SessionRegistry>();
458.                                                     let projection_registry =
459.                                                         app_handle.state::<StdArc<ProjectionRegistry>>();
460.                                                     let responder_session_id = session_id.to_string();
461.                                                     registry.store_pending_inbound_responder(
462.                                                     session_id,
463.                                                     StdArc::new(InboundRequestResponder {
464.                                                         session_id: responder_session_id,
465.                                                         provider: ctx.provider.clone(),
466.                                                         db: Some(db.inner().clone()),
467.                                                         stdin_writer: ctx.stdin_writer.clone(),
468.                                                         permission_tracker: ctx.permission_tracker.clone(),
469.                                                         projection_registry: projection_registry.inner().clone(),
470.                                                         dispatcher: ctx.dispatcher.clone(),
```

```
1. use super::*;
2. use crate::acp::client_transport::apply_interaction_response_for_request;
3. 
4. impl AcpClient {
5.     /// Send a JSON-RPC request and wait for response
6.     pub(super) async fn send_request(&mut self, method: &str, params: Value) -> AcpResult<Value> {
7.         client_rpc::send_request(
8.             &self.request_id,
9.             &self.pending_requests,
10.             &self.stdin_writer,
11.             self.process_generation,
12.             method,
13.             params,
14.             REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECS,
15.         )
16.         .await
17.     }
18. 
19.     /// Send a JSON-RPC response to an inbound request
20.     ///
21.     /// This is used to respond to requests from the ACP subprocess,
22.     /// such as `client/requestPermission` for tool approval.
23.     pub async fn respond(&self, id: u64, result: Value) -> AcpResult<()> {
24.         client_rpc::respond(&self.stdin_writer, id, result).await
25.     }
26. 
27.     /// Send a JSON-RPC response to an inbound request, with permission tracking.
28.     ///
29.     /// Inspects the result payload for `outcome.outcome == "cancelled"` to detect
30.     /// user denial. On deny, emits a synthetic `ToolCallUpdate(Failed)` so the
31.     /// tool card transitions from in-progress to failed in the UI.
32.     /// On allow or for non-permission requests, just cleans up the tracker entry.
33.     pub async fn respond_with_permission_tracking(&self, id: u64, result: Value) -> AcpResult<()> {
34.         let provider = self.provider.clone();
35.         let adapted_result = match self.inbound_response_adapters.lock() {
36.             Ok(mut adapters) => adapters
37.                 .remove(&id)
38.                 .map(|adapter| {
39.                     provider
40.                         .as_ref()
41.                         .map(|provider| provider.adapt_inbound_response(&adapter, &result))
42.                         .unwrap_or_else(|| result.clone())
43.                 })
44.                 .unwrap_or(result.clone()),
45.             Err(error) => {
46.                 tracing::error!("Inbound response adapter mutex poisoned in resolve: {error}");
47.                 result.clone()
48.             }
49.         };
50. 
51.         self.respond(id, adapted_result.clone()).await?;
52.         self.update_interaction_projection(id, &adapted_result)
53.             .await;
54. 
55.         // Check if this request was tracked as a permission
56.         let ctx = match self.permission_tracker.lock() {
57.             Ok(mut tracker) => tracker.resolve(id),
58.             Err(e) => {
59.                 tracing::error!("Permission tracker mutex poisoned in resolve: {e}");
60.                 None
61.             }
62.         };
63. 
64.         if let Some(ctx) = ctx {
65.             // Detect denial from the result payload
66.             let outcome_str = adapted_result
67.                 .pointer("/outcome/outcome")
68.                 .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
69.             let is_denied = outcome_str.is_some_and(|o| o == "cancelled");
70. 
71.             if let Some(o) = outcome_str {
72.                 if o != "cancelled" && o != "allowed" && o != "selected" {
73.                     tracing::warn!(outcome = o, tool_call_id = %ctx.tool_call_id, "Unrecognized permission outcome");
74.                 }
75.             }
76. 
77.             if is_denied {
78.                 if let Some(ref dispatcher) = self.dispatcher {
79.                     dispatcher.enqueue(AcpUiEvent::session_update(SessionUpdate::ToolCallUpdate {
80.                         update: ToolCallUpdateData {
81.                             tool_call_id: ctx.tool_call_id,
82.                             status: Some(ToolCallStatus::Failed),
83.                             failure_reason: Some("Permission denied by user".into()),
84.                             ..Default::default()
85.                         },
86.                         session_id: Some(ctx.session_id),
87.                     }));
88.                 }
89.             }
90.         }
91. 
92.         Ok(())
93.     }
94. 
95.     pub(super) async fn update_interaction_projection(
96.         &self,
97.         request_id: u64,
98.         adapted_result: &Value,
99.     ) {
100.         let session_id = match self.active_session_id.lock() {
101.             Ok(active_session_id) => active_session_id.clone(),
102.             Err(error) => {
103.                 tracing::error!("Active session mutex poisoned in resolve: {error}");
104.                 None
105.             }
106.         };
107.         let Some(session_id) = session_id else {
108.             return;
109.         };
110. 
111.         apply_interaction_response_for_request(
112.             &self.projection_registry,
113.             self.db.as_ref(),
114.             self.dispatcher.as_ref(),
115.             &session_id,
116.             request_id,
117.             adapted_result,
118.             "acp client",
119.         )
120.         .await;
```

</details>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Investigating connection issues**

I need to look into the session connection manager and the resume triggers. It might not be a backend issue; it could be a frontend bug causing lost prompts due to disconnected buffering. I'm wondering if a request was buffered while disconnected and appeared later. I'll examine the logic for how disconnected buffering handles replies—is it possible that interactions can't be sent if the session is not connected? The "Tool permission request failed" error could arise from a timeout in the event handler. It might help to check the logs and search for that exact error text.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 112m 13s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve confirmed the subprocess path tracks permission requests in `client_loop.rs` and then **drains them as failed on client teardown**. So the remaining question is whether the bad actor is backend teardown, or frontend resume logic making a live session look disconnected long enough to trigger teardown behavior. I’m now tracing the exact `"Tool permission request failed"` text and the frontend resume path around those buffered updates.

```
320. 
321. 		if (options && options.length > 0) {
322. 			const kindToMatch =
323. 				reply === "always" ? "allow_always" : reply === "once" ? "allow_once" : "reject_once";
324. 			const matchingOption = options.find((o) => o.kind === kindToMatch);
325. 			if (matchingOption) {
326. 				return matchingOption.optionId;
327. 			}
328. 		}
329. 
330. 		// Fallback to generic optionIds if no matching option found
331. 		return reply === "always" ? "allow_always" : reply === "once" ? "allow" : "reject";
332. 	}
333. 
334. 	/**
335. 	 * Reply to a permission request.
336. 	 *
337. 	 * The shared interaction reply layer resolves the correct transport.
338. 	 */
339. 	reply(permissionId: string, reply: "once" | "always" | "reject"): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
340. 		const permission = this.pending.get(permissionId);
341. 		if (!permission) {
342. 			return errAsync(
343. 				new AgentError("replyPermission", new Error(`Permission not found: ${permissionId}`))
344. 			);
345. 		}
346. 
347. 		const totalBeforeReply = this.sessionBatchTotals.get(permission.sessionId);
348. 		const completedBeforeReply = this.sessionBatchCompleted.get(permission.sessionId);
349. 
350. 		// Eagerly remove from pending map so the UI updates immediately.
351. 		// The user's intent is clear — don't wait for the async IPC response.
352. 		this.pending.delete(permissionId);
353. 		this.notePermissionResolved(permission.sessionId);
354. 		logger.debug("Permission request removed", { permissionId });
355. 
356. 		const optionId = this.resolveOptionId(permission, reply);
357. 
358. 		return replyToPermissionRequest(permission, reply, optionId)
359. 			.map(() => {
360. 				logger.debug("Permission reply sent", { permissionId, reply, optionId });
361. 			})
362. 			.mapErr((error) => {
363. 				this.restorePermissionAfterFailedReply(
364. 					permission,
365. 					totalBeforeReply,
366. 					completedBeforeReply
367. 				);
368. 				return error;
369. 			});
370. 	}
371. 
372. 	drainPendingForSession(sessionId: string): ResultAsyncType<void, AppError> {
373. 		const pendingPermissions = this.getForSession(sessionId);
374. 		if (pendingPermissions.length === 0) {
375. 			return okAsync(undefined);
376. 		}
377. 
378. 		return ResultAsync.combine(
379. 			pendingPermissions.map((permission) => {
380. 				if (!this.pending.has(permission.id)) {
381. 					return okAsync(undefined);
382. 				}
383. 				if (isExitPlanPermission(permission)) {
384. 					return okAsync(undefined);
385. 				}
386. 
387. 				logger.info("Draining autonomous permission", {
388. 					permissionId: permission.id,
389. 					sessionId: permission.sessionId,
390. 					tool: permission.permission,
```

<details>

```
1. import type { ResultAsync } from "neverthrow";
2. 
3. import { type AppError } from "../errors/app-error.js";
4. import { api } from "../store/api.js";
5. import type { PlanApprovalInteraction } from "../types/interaction.js";
6. import type { InteractionReplyRequest } from "../types/interaction-reply-request.js";
7. import type { PermissionReply, PermissionRequest } from "../types/permission.js";
8. import type { QuestionAnswer, QuestionRequest } from "../types/question.js";
9. import {
10. 	createLegacyInteractionReplyHandler,
11. 	type InteractionReplyHandler,
12. } from "../types/reply-handler.js";
13. 
14. interface InteractionReplyTarget {
15. 	sessionId: string;
16. 	id?: string;
17. 	jsonRpcRequestId?: number;
18. 	replyHandler?: InteractionReplyHandler;
19. }
20. 
21. type PlanApprovalReplyTarget =
22. 	| PlanApprovalInteraction
23. 	| {
24. 			sessionId: string;
25. 			jsonRpcRequestId: number;
26. 			replyHandler?: InteractionReplyHandler;
27. 			id?: string;
28. 	  };
29. 
30. function resolveReplyHandler(target: InteractionReplyTarget): InteractionReplyHandler {
31. 	return (
32. 		target.replyHandler ??
33. 		createLegacyInteractionReplyHandler(target.id ?? target.sessionId, target.jsonRpcRequestId)
34. 	);
35. }
36. 
37. function createInteractionReplyRequest(
38. 	target: InteractionReplyTarget,
39. 	payload: InteractionReplyRequest["payload"]
40. ): InteractionReplyRequest {
41. 	const base = {
42. 		sessionId: target.sessionId,
43. 		interactionId: target.id,
44. 		replyHandler: resolveReplyHandler(target),
45. 	};
46. 
47. 	switch (payload.kind) {
48. 		case "permission":
49. 			return {
50. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
51. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
52. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
53. 				payload: {
54. 					kind: "permission",
55. 					reply: payload.reply,
56. 					optionId: payload.optionId,
57. 				},
58. 			};
59. 		case "question":
60. 			return {
61. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
62. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
63. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
64. 				payload: {
65. 					kind: "question",
66. 					answers: payload.answers,
67. 					answerMap: payload.answerMap,
68. 				},
69. 			};
70. 		case "question_cancel":
71. 			return {
72. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
73. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
74. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
75. 				payload: {
76. 					kind: "question_cancel",
77. 				},
78. 			};
79. 		case "plan_approval":
80. 			return {
81. 				sessionId: base.sessionId,
82. 				interactionId: base.interactionId,
83. 				replyHandler: base.replyHandler,
84. 				payload: {
85. 					kind: "plan_approval",
86. 					approved: payload.approved,
87. 				},
88. 			};
89. 	}
90. }
91. 
92. export function replyToPermissionRequest(
93. 	permission: PermissionRequest,
94. 	reply: PermissionReply,
95. 	optionId: string
96. ): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
97. 	return api.replyInteraction(
98. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(permission, {
99. 			kind: "permission",
100. 			reply,
101. 			optionId,
102. 		})
103. 	);
104. }
105. 
106. export function replyToQuestionRequest(
107. 	question: QuestionRequest,
108. 	answers: QuestionAnswer[],
109. 	answerMap: Record<string, string | string[]>
110. ): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
111. 	return api.replyInteraction(
112. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(question, {
113. 			kind: "question",
114. 			answers,
115. 			answerMap,
116. 		})
117. 	);
118. }
119. 
120. export function cancelQuestionRequest(question: QuestionRequest): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
121. 	return api.replyInteraction(
122. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(question, {
123. 			kind: "question_cancel",
124. 		})
125. 	);
126. }
127. 
128. export function replyToPlanApprovalRequest(
129. 	targetOrSessionId: PlanApprovalReplyTarget | string,
130. 	jsonRpcRequestIdOrApproved: number | boolean,
131. 	approved: boolean
132. ): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
133. 	const target: PlanApprovalReplyTarget =
134. 		typeof targetOrSessionId === "string"
135. 			? {
136. 					sessionId: targetOrSessionId,
137. 					jsonRpcRequestId: jsonRpcRequestIdOrApproved as number,
138. 				}
139. 			: targetOrSessionId;
140. 	const resolvedApproved =
141. 		typeof targetOrSessionId === "string"
142. 			? approved
143. 			: (jsonRpcRequestIdOrApproved as boolean);
144. 
145. 	return api.replyInteraction(
146. 		createInteractionReplyRequest(target, {
147. 			kind: "plan_approval",
148. 			approved: resolvedApproved,
149. 		})
150. 	);
151. }
152.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 112m 26s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The frontend itself isn’t generating the `"Tool permission request failed"` string — it’s coming through as agent/session content after something lower-level breaks. That pushes the root cause back down into the backend path that writes the permission reply to Copilot’s stdin or decides the subprocess/session is gone. I’m checking the backend `reply_interaction` command and the connection manager around resume errors now.
