<sub>⏱️ 114m 48s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Investigating permission errors**

I see that there are permission stream errors, which are timing out before I can get approval. It looks like the user prompt might not be displaying correctly, possibly due to these timing issues. I need to check if there are any pending permissions in the store or if they need to be flushed. The root cause could relate to frontend delays in processing permission requests. I should also look into the session-event-service logs around the same timeframe for additional insights.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 114m 49s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The live transcript gives the strongest evidence yet: Copilot itself says the permissions are **timing out before approval can happen**. That lines up very well with a frontend-side delay before the permission request becomes actionable, not just a malformed reply. I’m checking whether `session/request_permission` can be buffered behind the reconnect gate and only flushed after the resume handshake finishes.

120. function serializeTaskChildrenFingerprint(
121. 	taskChildren: ReadonlyArray<ToolCallData> | null | undefined
122. ): string {
123. 	if (!taskChildren || taskChildren.length === 0) {
124. 		return "none";
125. 	}
126. 
127. 	return taskChildren.map((child) => serializeToolCallReplayPayload(child)).join(",");
128. }

131. 	taskChildren: ReadonlyArray<ToolCallData> | null | undefined

134. 		return "none";
135. 	}
136.

140. function serializeToolCallUpdateReplayPayload(update: ToolCallUpdateData): string {
141. 	const status = update.status ?? "none";
142. 	const title = update.title ?? "";
143. 	const resultFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.result ?? null);
144. 	const contentFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.content ?? null);
145. 	const rawOutputFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.rawOutput ?? null);
146. 	const locationsFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.locations ?? null);
147. 	const streamingInputDelta = update.streamingInputDelta ?? "";
148. 	const normalizedTodosFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.normalizedTodos ?? null);
149. 	const normalizedQuestionsFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.normalizedQuestions ?? null);
150. 	const streamingArgumentsFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.streamingArguments ?? null);
151. 	const argumentsFingerprint = JSON.stringify(update.arguments ?? null);
152. 	const failureReason = update.failureReason ?? "";
153. 	return `${update.toolCallId}:${status}:${title}:${resultFingerprint}:${contentFingerprint}:${rawOutputFingerprint}:${locationsFingerprint}:${streamingInputDelta}:${normalizedTodosFingerprint}:${normalizedQuestionsFingerprint}:${streamingArgumentsFingerprint}:${argumentsFingerprint}:${failureReason}`;
154. }

156. function isProcessedToolReplay(
157. 	handler: SessionEventHandler,
158. 	sessionId: string,
159. 	update: SessionUpdate,
160. 	replayCount: number
161. ): boolean {
162. 	if (replayCount <= 1) {
163. 		return false;
164. 	}
165. 
166. 	switch (update.type) {
167. 		case "toolCall":
168. 			return hasToolCallEntry(handler, sessionId, update.tool_call.id);
169. 		case "toolCallUpdate":
170. 			return hasToolCallEntry(handler, sessionId, update.update.toolCallId);
171. 		default:
172. 			return false;
173. 	}
174. }

184. 		return {

191. 
192. 	if (previous?.contextBudget?.source === "provider-explicit") {
193. 		return previous.contextBudget;
194. 	}
195. 
196. 	return previous?.contextBudget ?? null;
197. }

<sub>⏱️ 114m 57s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The code confirms a key behavior: **permission requests are subject to the same pending-event buffer as any other session update**, and that buffer only flushes after the full reconnect pipeline finishes. If Copilot is timing out permissions quickly, that buffer is a real suspect. I’m pulling the exact per-session frontend log sequence now to see whether a permission update got stuck behind that gate.

848. 	flushPendingEvents(sessionId: string, handler: SessionEventHandler): void {
849. 		const pending = this.pendingEvents.get(sessionId);
850. 		if (!pending || pending.length === 0) {
851. 			this.pendingEvents.delete(sessionId);
852. 			this.pendingEventTimestamps.delete(sessionId);
853. 			const timeoutId = this.pendingFlushTimeouts.get(sessionId);
854. 			if (timeoutId) {

857. 			}
858. 			return;
859. 		}
860. 
861. 		this.pendingEvents.delete(sessionId);
862. 		this.pendingEventTimestamps.delete(sessionId);

864. 		this.flushPendingEventsChunked(sessionId, pending, handler, 0);
865. 	}

867. 	// ============================================
868. 	// PRIVATE HELPERS
869. 	// ============================================
870. 
871. 	private flushPendingEventsChunked(
872. 		sessionId: string,
873. 		pending: SessionUpdate[],
874. 		handler: SessionEventHandler,
875. 		offset: number
876. 	): void {
877. 		const chunkStart = this.nowMs();
878. 		const end = Math.min(offset + SessionEventService.PENDING_FLUSH_CHUNK_SIZE, pending.length);
879. 		const chunkSize = end - offset;
880. 
881. 		for (let i = offset; i < end; i++) {
882. 			this.handleSessionUpdate(pending[i], handler);
883. 		}
884. 		const chunkDuration = this.nowMs() - chunkStart;
885. 		this.telemetryMaxReplayChunkDurationMs = Math.max(
886. 			this.telemetryMaxReplayChunkDurationMs,
887. 			chunkDuration
888. 		);
889. 		this.telemetryMaxReplayChunkSize = Math.max(this.telemetryMaxReplayChunkSize, chunkSize);
890. 
891. 		if (chunkDuration > SessionEventService.WARN_REPLAY_CHUNK_DURATION_MS) {
892. 			this.warnWithCooldown("chunk", "Replay chunk exceeded frame budget", {
893. 				sessionId,
894. 				chunkDurationMs: Number(chunkDuration.toFixed(2)),
895. 				chunkSize,
896. 				remaining: pending.length - end,
897. 			});
898. 		}
899. 
900. 		if (end >= pending.length) {
901. 			this.pendingFlushTimeouts.delete(sessionId);
902. 			return;
903. 		}
904. 
905. 		const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
906. 			this.pendingFlushTimeouts.delete(sessionId);
907. 			this.flushPendingEventsChunked(sessionId, pending, handler, end);
908. 		}, 0);
909. 		this.pendingFlushTimeouts.set(sessionId, timeoutId);
910. 	}
911. 
912. 	/**
913. 	 * Schedule cleanup of orphaned pending events.
914. 	 */
915. 	private scheduleOrphanedEventCleanup(sessionId: string): void {
916. 		setTimeout(() => {
917. 			const timestamp = this.pendingEventTimestamps.get(sessionId);
918. 			if (timestamp === undefined) {
919. 				// Already cleaned up (session was created and events flushed)
920. 				return;
921. 			}
922. 
923. 			const pending = this.pendingEvents.get(sessionId);
924. 			const count = pending?.length ?? 0;
925. 			this.pendingEvents.delete(sessionId);
926. 			this.pendingEventTimestamps.delete(sessionId);
927. 			logger.warn("Discarded orphaned pending events", {
928. 				sessionId,
929. 				count,
930. 				elapsedMs: Date.now() - timestamp,
931. 			});
932. 		}, SessionEventService.PENDING_EVENT_TIMEOUT_MS + 100);
933. 	}
934.

939. 		return handler.getSessionCold(sessionId) !== undefined;
940. 	}

942. 	/**
943. 	 * Buffer event for session that may still be creating (race condition).
944. 	 */
945. 	private bufferPendingEvent(sessionId: string, update: SessionUpdate): void {
946. 		const pending = this.pendingEvents.get(sessionId) ?? [];
947. 
948. 		// Enforce buffer size limit to prevent unbounded memory growth
949. 		if (pending.length >= SessionEventService.MAX_PENDING_EVENTS_PER_SESSION) {
950. 			// Drop oldest event to make room
951. 			pending.shift();
952. 			logger.warn("Pending events buffer full, dropped oldest event", {
953. 				sessionId,
954. 				bufferSize: pending.length,
955. 			});
956. 		}
957. 
958. 		pending.push(update);
959. 		this.pendingEvents.set(sessionId, pending);
960. 		this.telemetryMaxPendingBacklog = Math.max(this.telemetryMaxPendingBacklog, pending.length);
961. 
962. 		if (pending.length >= SessionEventService.WARN_PENDING_BACKLOG_SIZE) {
963. 			this.warnWithCooldown("backlog", "Pending event backlog reached warning threshold", {
964. 				sessionId,
965. 				backlogSize: pending.length,
966. 			});
967. 		}
968. 
969. 		// Track when we first started buffering for this session
970. 		if (!this.pendingEventTimestamps.has(sessionId)) {
971. 			this.pendingEventTimestamps.set(sessionId, Date.now());
972. 			this.scheduleOrphanedEventCleanup(sessionId);
973. 		}
974. 
975. 		logger.debug("Buffered event for pending session", {
976. 			sessionId,
977. 			type: update.type,
978. 			bufferSize: pending.length,
979. 		});
980. 	}

<sub>⏱️ 115m 7s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The buffering mechanism is more suspicious than it first looked: `flushPendingEvents()` runs **after** the full reconnect/capabilities handshake, and it replays buffered events in chunks via `setTimeout(0)`. If Copilot’s permission waits are short, that can absolutely turn a prompt into “timed out before approval.” I’m pulling the per-session console slice around the permission event now to verify whether it really sat in that queue.

<sub>⏱️ 115m 34s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Analyzing permission request logs**

I'm noticing that there are potentially no permission request logs because the grep pattern doesn’t match any entries containing the word “permission.” They might exist but aren’t logged. I could search through the raw streaming logs file. There's a noticeable 5-second gap between a buffered disconnection event and a resume completion, which could throw off timing for permission requests. I wonder if processing requests immediately upon disconnection might help rather than buffering them.**Designing a safe fix**

I need to focus on creating a safe and effective solution. I see that the code in the disconnected gate is buffering all updates for disconnected sessions. I wonder if I could special-case permission and question requests to bypass that buffering. If the session exists—even when disconnected—maybe they could still be processed. The risk is that the session state machine indicates disconnection, but quick display of the permission prompt is essential. The permission store relies on session ID, so this approach might still work. However, I have to consider potential replay issues from early updates.**Modifying buffering for requests**

I’m thinking it’s safer to exempt interaction requests from buffering. Right now, buffered events occur due to `!hasKnownSession`, but in this scenario, the session is known. So, the disconnected gate is the key concern. I could modify it so that if it’s a disconnected session that isn’t connecting, we can bypass buffering for specific request types like permission and question requests, and maybe even tool call updates. This way, we could prevent timeouts, but I’d need to test this change in the session-event-service. I'll check the related tests next.**Checking TypeScript and tests**

I see I need to check and run tests because I’m working with TypeScript. It might help to use a frontend test. The project instructions remind me to invoke Svelte skills before modifying Svelte code, but since this is TypeScript and not Svelte, that's fine. I should also check the tests related to the session-event-service for any relevant patterns. Let's get searching for those tests!*

<sub>⏱️ 115m 42s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I think I’ve got a right-sized fix candidate now: **don’t hold permission/question interactions behind the disconnected-session buffer**. They’re time-sensitive, and the current reconnect path can spend several seconds replaying history before flush. I’m checking the existing `SessionEventService` tests so I can add a regression test and keep the change surgical.

1. /**
2.  * Session Event Service Streaming Tests
3.  *
4.  * Tests for streaming delta handling, specifically verifying that:
5.  * 1. Empty string deltas are handled by the fast path (not creating placeholder entries)
6.  * 2. Regular updates without streamingInputDelta go through normal path
7.  */
8. 
9. import { okAsync } from "neverthrow";
10. import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
11. 
12. vi.mock("../utils/logger.js", () => ({
13. 	createLogger: () => ({
14. 		debug: vi.fn(),
15. 		info: vi.fn(),
16. 		isLevelEnabled: vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false),
17. 		warn: vi.fn(),
18. 		error: vi.fn(),
19. 	}),
20. }));
21. 
22. import type { SessionUpdate } from "../../../services/converted-session-types.js";
23. import type { SessionEntry } from "../../application/dto/session.js";
24. import { SessionEntryStore } from "../session-entry-store.svelte.js";
25. import type { SessionEventHandler } from "../session-event-handler.js";
26. import { SessionEventService } from "../session-event-service.svelte.js";
27. import type { SessionCold } from "../types.js";
28. 
29. function createMockHandler(): SessionEventHandler {
30. 	return {
31. 		getSessionCold: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ id: "session-123" } as unknown as SessionCold),
32. 		isPreloaded: vi.fn().mockReturnValue(true),
33. 		getEntries: vi.fn().mockReturnValue([]),
34. 		getHotState: vi.fn(),
35. 		aggregateAssistantChunk: vi.fn().mockReturnValue(okAsync(undefined)),
36. 		aggregateUserChunk: vi.fn().mockReturnValue(okAsync(undefined)),
37. 		createToolCallEntry: vi.fn(),
38. 		updateToolCallEntry: vi.fn(),
39. 		updateAvailableCommands: vi.fn(),
40. 		ensureStreamingState: vi.fn(),
41. 		handleStreamEntry: vi.fn(),
42. 		handleStreamComplete: vi.fn(),
43. 		handleTurnError: vi.fn(),
44. 		clearStreamingAssistantEntry: vi.fn(),
45. 		updateCurrentMode: vi.fn(),
46. 		updateConfigOptions: vi.fn(),
47. 		updateUsageTelemetry: vi.fn(),
48. 	};
49. }
50. 
51. function markHandlerTurnAsStreaming(handler: SessionEventHandler): void {
52. 	(handler.getHotState as ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>).mockReturnValue({
53. 		turnState: "streaming",
54. 	});
55. }
56. 
57. function markHandlerTurnAsCompleted(handler: SessionEventHandler): void {
58. 	(handler.getHotState as ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>).mockReturnValue({
59. 		turnState: "completed",
60. 	});
61. }
62. 
63. function createTaskReplayEntry(toolCallId: string): SessionEntry {
64. 	return {
65. 		id: `entry-${toolCallId}`,
66. 		type: "tool_call",
67. 		timestamp: new Date(),
68. 		message: {
69. 			id: toolCallId,
70. 			name: "Agent",
71. 			arguments: {
72. 				kind: "think",
73. 				subagent_type: "reviewer",
74. 				description: "Review the implementation",
75. 				prompt: "Inspect the changes",
76. 			},
77. 			status: "in_progress",
78. 			kind: "task",
79. 			title: "Agent",
80. 			locations: null,
81. 			skillMeta: null,
82. 			result: null,
83. 			taskChildren: null,
84. 			awaitingPlanApproval: false,
85. 		},
86. 	};
87. }
88. 
89. function createTaskReplayChild(
90. 	index: number
91. ): Extract<SessionUpdate, { type: "toolCall" }>["tool_call"] {
92. 	return {
93. 		id: `task-child-${String(index).padStart(2, "0")}-long-child-identifier`,
94. 		name: "Read",
95. 		arguments: { kind: "read", file_path: `/repo/src/task-${index}.ts` },
96. 		status: "completed",
97. 		kind: "read",
98. 		title: `Read file ${index}`,
99. 		locations: null,
100. 		skillMeta: null,
101. 		result: null,
102. 		taskChildren: null,
103. 		awaitingPlanApproval: false,
104. 	};
105. }
106. 
107. describe("SessionEventService streaming delta handling", () => {
108. 	let service: SessionEventService;
109. 	let handler: SessionEventHandler;
110. 
111. 	beforeEach(() => {
112. 		service = new SessionEventService();
113. 		handler = createMockHandler();
114. 	});
115. 
116. 	it("ignores malformed usage telemetry updates without data", () => {
117. 		const malformedUpdate = { type: "usageTelemetryUpdate" } as SessionUpdate;
118. 
119. 		expect(() => service.handleSessionUpdate(malformedUpdate, handler)).not.toThrow();
120. 		expect(handler.updateUsageTelemetry).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
121. 	});
122. 
123. 	it("ignores malformed usage telemetry updates without a valid sessionId", () => {
124. 		const malformedUpdate = {
125. 			type: "usageTelemetryUpdate",
126. 			data: { eventId: "event-1", costUsd: 0.04 },
127. 		} as SessionUpdate;
128. 
129. 		expect(() => service.handleSessionUpdate(malformedUpdate, handler)).not.toThrow();
130. 		expect(handler.updateUsageTelemetry).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
131. 	});
132. 
133. 	it("resolves an explicit provider context budget from usage telemetry updates", () => {
134. 		(handler.getHotState as ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>).mockReturnValue({
135. 			currentModel: { id: "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929" },
136. 			usageTelemetry: null,
137. 		});
138. 
139. 		const update: SessionUpdate = {

847. 	it("buffers updates for disconnected sessions when not connecting", () => {
848. 		const disconnectedHandler = createMockHandler();

856. 			isConnected: false,
857. 			status: "idle",
858. 		});

866. 				status: "in_progress",

881. 	it("accepts updates for disconnected sessions while connecting", () => {
882. 		const connectingHandler = createMockHandler();

884. 			id: "session-123",

886. 		} as unknown as SessionCold;
887. 		(connectingHandler.getSessionCold as ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>).mockReturnValue(session);

889. 		(connectingHandler.getHotState as ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>).mockReturnValue({
890. 			isConnected: false,
891. 			status: "connecting",
892. 		});

896. 			session_id: "session-123",

899. 				name: "WebSearch",

910. 		service.handleSessionUpdate(update, connectingHandler);
```

<sub>⏱️ 116m 9s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve got a small, plausible fix: let `permissionRequest` and `questionRequest` **bypass the disconnected-session buffer** so they don’t sit behind a multi-second resume/replay cycle. I’m checking how duplicate interaction IDs behave in the permission store first, to make sure replaying the same prompt after reconnect won’t create a second broken copy.

<details>

```
1. /**
2.  * Permission Store - Manages permission requests from agents.
3.  *
4.  * This store handles pending permission requests from ACP and OpenCode agents,
5.  * allowing users to grant or deny permissions for file operations.
6.  *
7.  * For ACP mode (Claude Code), permissions can arrive either as session updates
8.  * carrying a JSON-RPC reply route or as legacy inbound JSON-RPC requests.
9.  * Replies are still sent back via JSON-RPC.
10.  *
11.  * For OpenCode HTTP mode, permissions come via session updates
12.  * and responses are sent via HTTP endpoints.
13.  */
14. 
15. import { errAsync, okAsync, ResultAsync, type ResultAsync as ResultAsyncType } from "neverthrow";
16. import { getContext, setContext } from "svelte";
17. import { SvelteMap } from "svelte/reactivity";
18. import type { AppError } from "../errors/app-error.js";
19. import { AgentError } from "../errors/app-error.js";
20. 
21. import { replyToPermissionRequest } from "../logic/interaction-reply.js";
22. import type { Operation } from "../types/operation.js";
23. import type { PermissionRequest } from "../types/permission.js";
24. import type { ToolCall } from "../types/tool-call.js";
25. import { isExitPlanPermission } from "../utils/exit-plan-permission.js";
26. import { createLogger } from "../utils/logger.js";
27. import { permissionMatchesToolCall } from "../utils/permission-tool-match.js";
28. import { findOperationForPermission, permissionMatchesOperation } from "./operation-association.js";
29. import { InteractionStore } from "./interaction-store.svelte.js";
30. import type { OperationStore } from "./operation-store.svelte.js";
31. 
32. const PERMISSION_STORE_KEY = Symbol("permission-store");
33. const logger = createLogger({ id: "permission-store", name: "PermissionStore" });
34. 
35. export class PermissionStore {
36. 	private interactions = new InteractionStore();
37. 	private sessionBatchTotals = new SvelteMap<string, number>();
38. 	private sessionBatchCompleted = new SvelteMap<string, number>();
39. 
40. 	constructor(interactions?: InteractionStore) {
41. 		if (interactions !== undefined) {
42. 			this.interactions = interactions;
43. 		}
44. 	}
45. 
46. 	get pending(): SvelteMap<string, PermissionRequest> {
47. 		return this.interactions.permissionsPending;
48. 	}
49. 
50. 	/** Callback to check if a permission should be auto-accepted (e.g. child sessions or Autonomous). */
51. 	private shouldAutoAccept:
52. 		| ((permission: PermissionRequest) => boolean | "child-session" | "autonomous-live")
53. 		| null = null;
54. 
55. 	private countPendingForSession(sessionId: string): number {
56. 		let count = 0;
57. 		for (const permission of this.pending.values()) {
58. 			if (permission.sessionId === sessionId) {
59. 				count += 1;
60. 			}
61. 		}
62. 		return count;
63. 	}
64. 
65. 	private clearSessionProgress(sessionId: string): void {
66. 		this.sessionBatchTotals.delete(sessionId);
67. 		this.sessionBatchCompleted.delete(sessionId);
68. 	}
69. 
70. 	private notePermissionAdded(sessionId: string, hadPendingBeforeAdd: boolean): void {
71. 		if (!hadPendingBeforeAdd) {
72. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(sessionId, 1);
73. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(sessionId, 0);
74. 			return;
75. 		}
76. 
77. 		const currentTotal = this.sessionBatchTotals.get(sessionId);
78. 		if (currentTotal !== undefined && currentTotal > 0) {
79. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(sessionId, currentTotal + 1);
80. 		} else {
81. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(sessionId, this.countPendingForSession(sessionId));
82. 		}
83. 
84. 		if (!this.sessionBatchCompleted.has(sessionId)) {
85. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(sessionId, 0);
86. 		}
87. 	}
88. 
89. 	private notePermissionResolved(sessionId: string): void {
90. 		const totalValue = this.sessionBatchTotals.get(sessionId);
91. 		const total = totalValue !== undefined ? totalValue : 0;
92. 		if (total > 0) {
93. 			const completedValue = this.sessionBatchCompleted.get(sessionId);
94. 			const completed = completedValue !== undefined ? completedValue : 0;
95. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(sessionId, Math.min(total, completed + 1));
96. 		}
97. 
98. 		if (this.countPendingForSession(sessionId) === 0) {
99. 			this.clearSessionProgress(sessionId);
100. 		}
101. 	}
102. 
103. 	/** Configure auto-accept predicate. Returns a dispose function. */
104. 	setAutoAccept(
105. 		fn: (permission: PermissionRequest) => boolean | "child-session" | "autonomous-live"
106. 	): () => void {
107. 		this.shouldAutoAccept = fn;
108. 		return () => {
109. 			this.shouldAutoAccept = null;
110. 		};
111. 	}
112. 
113. 	private restorePermissionAfterFailedReply(
114. 		permission: PermissionRequest,
115. 		totalBeforeReply: number | undefined,
116. 		completedBeforeReply: number | undefined
117. 	): void {
118. 		this.pending.set(permission.id, permission);
119. 
120. 		if (totalBeforeReply !== undefined) {
121. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(permission.sessionId, totalBeforeReply);
122. 		} else {
123. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.delete(permission.sessionId);
124. 		}
125. 
126. 		if (completedBeforeReply !== undefined) {
127. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(permission.sessionId, completedBeforeReply);
128. 		} else {
129. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.delete(permission.sessionId);
130. 		}
131. 	}
132. 
133. 	private getToolCallId(permission: PermissionRequest): string {
134. 		return permission.tool?.callID ? permission.tool.callID : permission.id;
135. 	}
136. 
137. 	private shouldPreferPermission(
138. 		candidate: PermissionRequest,
139. 		current: PermissionRequest | undefined
140. 	): boolean {
141. 		if (!current) {
142. 			return true;
143. 		}
144. 
145. 		if (candidate.replyHandler !== undefined && current.replyHandler === undefined) {
146. 			return true;
147. 		}
148. 
149. 		if (candidate.replyHandler === undefined && current.replyHandler !== undefined) {
150. 			return false;
151. 		}
152. 
153. 		return true;
154. 	}
155. 
156. 	/**
157. 	 * Add a pending permission request.
158. 	 *
159. 	 * Permissions from child sessions (subtasks) are auto-accepted silently
160. 	 * when an `isChildSession` check is configured via `setChildSessionCheck()`.
161. 	 */
162. 	add(permission: PermissionRequest): void {
163. 		const hadPendingBeforeAdd = this.countPendingForSession(permission.sessionId) > 0;
164. 		this.pending.set(permission.id, permission);
165. 		this.notePermissionAdded(permission.sessionId, hadPendingBeforeAdd);
166. 
167. 		const autoAcceptDecision =

Finished both pieces.

1. **Cleanup:** renamed the Claude-only cc-sdk type to `ClaudeCcSdkClient` and updated the factory/docs so the ownership is explicit instead of looking provider-agnostic.
2. **Copilot fix:** patched `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/store/session-event-service.svelte.ts` so `permissionRequest` and `questionRequest` **do not get buffered behind the disconnected-session replay gate**. The live Copilot transcript was saying permissions were timing out before approval, and the reconnect path was capable of spending several seconds buffering/replaying session updates before surfacing them. This change lets time-sensitive interaction prompts through immediately.

Added regression coverage in `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/store/__tests__/session-event-service-streaming.vitest.ts` for disconnected-session permission/question handling, and the TypeScript check plus focused session-event-service test file passed.

If the running app doesn’t pick up the frontend change automatically, reload/restart the desktop app to apply the `SessionEventService` update.

<sub>⏱️ 117m 21s</sub>

### ✓ Task Complete

Finished both pieces.

1. **Cleanup:** renamed the Claude-only cc-sdk type to `ClaudeCcSdkClient` and updated the factory/docs so the ownership is explicit instead of looking provider-agnostic.
2. **Copilot fix:** patched `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/store/session-event-service.svelte.ts` so `permissionRequest` and `questionRequest` **do not get buffered behind the disconnected-session replay gate**. The live Copilot transcript was saying permissions were timing out before approval, and the reconnect path was capable of spending several seconds buffering/replaying session updates before surfacing them. This change lets time-sensitive interaction prompts through immediately.

Added regression coverage in `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/store/__tests__/session-event-service-streaming.vitest.ts` for disconnected-session permission/question handling, and the TypeScript check plus focused session-event-service test file passed.

If the running app doesn’t pick up the frontend change automatically, reload/restart the desktop app to apply the `SessionEventService` update.

<sub>⏱️ 127m 6s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Not fully.** After the cleanup, **Claude Code’s cc-sdk path** is now clearly Claude-owned (`ClaudeCcSdkClient`), so that part matches the boundary better.

But **Copilot does not live in “Copilot-only runtime files.”** It still runs through the **shared ACP subprocess stack** (`AcpClient`, `client_loop`, transport, session commands, frontend session event service), with Copilot-specific behavior mainly in `providers/copilot.rs` and its parser/normalization layer.

So the accurate picture is:

| Provider | Main runtime path | Ownership |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Claude Code | `cc_sdk_client.rs` | Claude-specific |
| Copilot | shared subprocess ACP files + `providers/copilot.rs` | mostly shared, not Copilot-specific |

So: **Claude yes, Copilot not entirely**. The earlier confusion was mainly that a Claude-specific file still had a too-generic-looking role/name, while Copilot’s real bug was in shared reconnect/buffering code, not in a Copilot-only backend file.

Yes: **Claude Code is now clearly mapped to Claude-specific cc-sdk code**, and **Copilot is no longer being treated as if it flowed through that Claude file**.

The precise boundary is:

| Provider | Runtime ownership |
| --- | --- |
| Claude Code | `cc_sdk_client.rs` via `ClaudeCcSdkClient` |
| Copilot | shared ACP subprocess runtime plus `providers/copilot.rs` |

So the cleanup is done, and the Copilot bug fix landed in the correct shared reconnect/event path rather than the Claude-specific runtime.

/var/folders/rw/_1br_hy5247_g54mh7vlcm740000gn/T/1775775251444-copilot-tool-output-rxuyv6.txt:878:  - button "The issue is clear. The recurring failure in your logs is: \"Permission callback was never received, so the command did not produce output.\" This happens multiple times (lines 50, 99, 363, 490). This is exactly what we experienced earlier in this session — when a tool call requires permission approval (like writing a file), and the permission prompt isn't responded to in time, the tool call fails with that message. The root cause is in how your desktop app handles the permission callback flow. When Claude's backend emits a tool call that needs user approval, your app needs to: Display the permission prompt to the user Capture the user's accept/reject response Send the callback back to the streaming session If any of those steps don't complete (e.g., the UI doesn't render the prompt, or the callback isn't sent back), the tool times out and you get this \"Failed\" state. Things to check in your app: Is the permission prompt UI actually rendering when a toolCallUpdate with a permission request comes in? Is the callback being sent back to the API after the user clicks approve/reject? Is there a timeout that's too short, causing the permission to expire before the user can respond? Would you like me to dig into your app's source code to trace how permission callbacks are handled?" [ref=e1791]:

Using Tauri MCP to debug live sounds like a plan, especially when the user interacts. It could help to inspect the DOM using JavaScript since find_element is broken. I might use execute_js for direct queries and actions, including finding buttons and clicking them to bypass issues. I’ll also check if a permission modal exists—it’s all about inspecting the document text for prompts!**Extracting Text Nodes**

It might also be useful to list buttons on the page, so I could run a script to gather the texts of those buttons. I’m thinking of using JavaScript in an immediately-invoked function expression (IIFE) for this. This approach seems like a good way to collect the necessary data without involving any Node operations.*

```
1. /**
2.  * Permission Store - Manages permission requests from agents.
3.  *
4.  * This store handles pending permission requests from ACP and OpenCode agents,
5.  * allowing users to grant or deny permissions for file operations.
6.  *
7.  * For ACP mode (Claude Code), permissions can arrive either as session updates
8.  * carrying a JSON-RPC reply route or as legacy inbound JSON-RPC requests.
9.  * Replies are still sent back via JSON-RPC.
10.  *
11.  * For OpenCode HTTP mode, permissions come via session updates
12.  * and responses are sent via HTTP endpoints.
13.  */
14. 
15. import { errAsync, okAsync, ResultAsync, type ResultAsync as ResultAsyncType } from "neverthrow";
16. import { getContext, setContext } from "svelte";
17. import { SvelteMap } from "svelte/reactivity";
18. import type { AppError } from "../errors/app-error.js";
19. import { AgentError } from "../errors/app-error.js";
20. 
21. import { replyToPermissionRequest } from "../logic/interaction-reply.js";
22. import type { Operation } from "../types/operation.js";
23. import type { PermissionRequest } from "../types/permission.js";
24. import type { ToolCall } from "../types/tool-call.js";
25. import { isExitPlanPermission } from "../utils/exit-plan-permission.js";
26. import { createLogger } from "../utils/logger.js";
27. import { permissionMatchesToolCall } from "../utils/permission-tool-match.js";
28. import { findOperationForPermission, permissionMatchesOperation } from "./operation-association.js";
29. import { InteractionStore } from "./interaction-store.svelte.js";
30. import type { OperationStore } from "./operation-store.svelte.js";
31. 
32. const PERMISSION_STORE_KEY = Symbol("permission-store");
33. const logger = createLogger({ id: "permission-store", name: "PermissionStore" });
34. 
35. export class PermissionStore {
36. 	private interactions = new InteractionStore();
37. 	private sessionBatchTotals = new SvelteMap<string, number>();
38. 	private sessionBatchCompleted = new SvelteMap<string, number>();
39. 
40. 	constructor(interactions?: InteractionStore) {
41. 		if (interactions !== undefined) {
42. 			this.interactions = interactions;
43. 		}
44. 	}
45. 
46. 	get pending(): SvelteMap<string, PermissionRequest> {
47. 		return this.interactions.permissionsPending;
48. 	}
49. 
50. 	/** Callback to check if a permission should be auto-accepted (e.g. child sessions or Autonomous). */
51. 	private shouldAutoAccept:
52. 		| ((permission: PermissionRequest) => boolean | "child-session" | "autonomous-live")
53. 		| null = null;
54. 
55. 	private countPendingForSession(sessionId: string): number {
56. 		let count = 0;
57. 		for (const permission of this.pending.values()) {
58. 			if (permission.sessionId === sessionId) {
59. 				count += 1;
60. 			}
61. 		}
62. 		return count;
63. 	}
64. 
65. 	private clearSessionProgress(sessionId: string): void {
66. 		this.sessionBatchTotals.delete(sessionId);
67. 		this.sessionBatchCompleted.delete(sessionId);
68. 	}
69. 
70. 	private notePermissionAdded(sessionId: string, hadPendingBeforeAdd: boolean): void {
71. 		if (!hadPendingBeforeAdd) {
72. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(sessionId, 1);
73. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(sessionId, 0);
74. 			return;
75. 		}
76. 
77. 		const currentTotal = this.sessionBatchTotals.get(sessionId);
78. 		if (currentTotal !== undefined && currentTotal > 0) {
79. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(sessionId, currentTotal + 1);
80. 		} else {
81. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(sessionId, this.countPendingForSession(sessionId));
82. 		}
83. 
84. 		if (!this.sessionBatchCompleted.has(sessionId)) {
85. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(sessionId, 0);
86. 		}
87. 	}
88. 
89. 	private notePermissionResolved(sessionId: string): void {
90. 		const totalValue = this.sessionBatchTotals.get(sessionId);
91. 		const total = totalValue !== undefined ? totalValue : 0;
92. 		if (total > 0) {
93. 			const completedValue = this.sessionBatchCompleted.get(sessionId);
94. 			const completed = completedValue !== undefined ? completedValue : 0;
95. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(sessionId, Math.min(total, completed + 1));
96. 		}
97. 
98. 		if (this.countPendingForSession(sessionId) === 0) {
99. 			this.clearSessionProgress(sessionId);
100. 		}
101. 	}
102. 
103. 	/** Configure auto-accept predicate. Returns a dispose function. */
104. 	setAutoAccept(
105. 		fn: (permission: PermissionRequest) => boolean | "child-session" | "autonomous-live"
106. 	): () => void {
107. 		this.shouldAutoAccept = fn;
108. 		return () => {
109. 			this.shouldAutoAccept = null;
110. 		};
111. 	}
112. 
113. 	private restorePermissionAfterFailedReply(
114. 		permission: PermissionRequest,
115. 		totalBeforeReply: number | undefined,
116. 		completedBeforeReply: number | undefined
117. 	): void {
118. 		this.pending.set(permission.id, permission);
119. 
120. 		if (totalBeforeReply !== undefined) {
121. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.set(permission.sessionId, totalBeforeReply);
122. 		} else {
123. 			this.sessionBatchTotals.delete(permission.sessionId);
124. 		}
125. 
126. 		if (completedBeforeReply !== undefined) {
127. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.set(permission.sessionId, completedBeforeReply);
128. 		} else {
129. 			this.sessionBatchCompleted.delete(permission.sessionId);
130. 		}
131. 	}
132. 
133. 	private getToolCallId(permission: PermissionRequest): string {
134. 		return permission.tool?.callID ? permission.tool.callID : permission.id;
135. 	}
136. 
137. 	private shouldPreferPermission(
138. 		candidate: PermissionRequest,
139. 		current: PermissionRequest | undefined
140. 	): boolean {
141. 		if (!current) {
142. 			return true;
143. 		}
144. 
145. 		if (candidate.replyHandler !== undefined && current.replyHandler === undefined) {
146. 			return true;
147. 		}
148. 
149. 		if (candidate.replyHandler === undefined && current.replyHandler !== undefined) {
150. 			return false;
151. 		}
152. 
153. 		return true;
154. 	}
155. 
156. 	/**
157. 	 * Add a pending permission request.
158. 	 *
159. 	 * Permissions from child sessions (subtasks) are auto-accepted silently
160. 	 * when an `isChildSession` check is configured via `setChildSessionCheck()`.
161. 	 */
162. 	add(permission: PermissionRequest): void {
163. 		const hadPendingBeforeAdd = this.countPendingForSession(permission.sessionId) > 0;
164. 		this.pending.set(permission.id, permission);
165. 		this.notePermissionAdded(permission.sessionId, hadPendingBeforeAdd);
166. 
167. 		const autoAcceptDecision =
168. 			this.shouldAutoAccept !== null && !isExitPlanPermission(permission)
169. 				? this.shouldAutoAccept(permission)
170. 				: false;
171. 		const autoAcceptSource =
172. 			autoAcceptDecision === true
173. 				? "auto"
174. 				: autoAcceptDecision === false
175. 					? null
176. 					: autoAcceptDecision;
177. 
178. 		if (autoAcceptSource) {
179. 			logger.info("Auto-accepting permission", {
180. 				permissionId: permission.id,
181. 				sessionId: permission.sessionId,
182. 				tool: permission.permission,
183. 				source: autoAcceptSource,
184. 			});
185. 			void this.reply(permission.id, "once").match(
186. 				() => {},
187. 				(err) => logger.error("Failed to auto-accept permission", { error: err })
188. 			);
189. 			return;
190. 		}
191. 
192. 		logger.debug("Permission request added", {
193. 			permissionId: permission.id,
194. 			toolCallId: permission.tool?.callID,
195. 			jsonRpcRequestId: permission.jsonRpcRequestId,
196. 		});
197. 	}
198. 
199. 	/**
200. 	 * Get the most recent pending permission for a given session-scoped tool call.
201. 	 */
202. 	getForToolCall(
203. 		sessionId: string | undefined,
204. 		toolCallOrId: string | ToolCall
205. 	): PermissionRequest | undefined {
206. 		if (!sessionId) {
207. 			return undefined;
208. 		}
209. 
210. 		const toolCallId = typeof toolCallOrId === "string" ? toolCallOrId : toolCallOrId.id;
211. 		let latest: PermissionRequest | undefined;
212. 		for (const permission of this.pending.values()) {
213. 			const permissionToolCallId = this.getToolCallId(permission);
214. 			if (permission.sessionId !== sessionId) {
215. 				continue;
216. 			}
217. 			const isDirectMatch = permissionToolCallId === toolCallId;
218. 			const isSemanticMatch =
219. 				!isDirectMatch &&
220. 				typeof toolCallOrId !== "string" &&
221. 				permissionMatchesToolCall(permission, toolCallOrId);
222. 			if (!isDirectMatch && !isSemanticMatch) {
223. 				continue;
224. 			}
225. 			if (this.shouldPreferPermission(permission, latest)) {
226. 				latest = permission;
227. 			}
228. 		}
229. 		return latest;
230. 	}
231. 
232. 	getForSession(sessionId: string): PermissionRequest[] {
233. 		const permissions: PermissionRequest[] = [];
234. 		for (const permission of this.pending.values()) {
235. 			if (permission.sessionId === sessionId) {
236. 				permissions.push(permission);
237. 			}
238. 		}
239. 		return permissions;
240. 	}
241. 
242. 	getForOperation(operation: Operation, operationStore: OperationStore): PermissionRequest | undefined {
243. 		let latest: PermissionRequest | undefined;
244. 		for (const permission of this.pending.values()) {
245. 			if (permission.sessionId !== operation.sessionId) {
246. 				continue;
247. 			}
248. 
249. 			if (!permissionMatchesOperation(permission, operation)) {
250. 				continue;
251. 			}
252. 
253. 			const permissionToolCallId = this.getToolCallId(permission);
254. 			const isDirectMatch = permissionToolCallId === operation.toolCallId;
255. 			if (!isDirectMatch) {
256. 				const matchedOperation = findOperationForPermission(operationStore, permission);
257. 				if (matchedOperation?.id !== operation.id) {
258. 					continue;
259. 				}
260. 			}
```

54. 	clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
55. 		for (const [interactionId, permission] of this.permissionsPending) {
56. 			if (permission.sessionId === sessionId) {
57. 				this.permissionsPending.delete(interactionId);

60.

71. 			}
72. 		}
73.

81. 	replaceSessionProjection(projection: SessionProjectionSnapshot): void {
82. 		const sessionId =
83. 			projection.session?.session_id ?? projection.interactions[0]?.session_id;
84. 		if (sessionId === undefined) {
85. 			return;
86. 		}
87. 
88. 		this.clearSession(sessionId);
89. 		for (const interaction of projection.interactions) {
90. 			this.applyProjectionInteraction(interaction);
91. 		}
92. 	}

94. 	private applyProjectionInteraction(interaction: InteractionSnapshot): void {
95. 		if ("Permission" in interaction.payload) {
96. 			this.applyPermissionInteraction(interaction, interaction.payload.Permission);
97. 			return;
98. 		}
99. 
100. 		if ("Question" in interaction.payload) {

110. 	private applyPermissionInteraction(
111. 		interaction: InteractionSnapshot,
112. 		payload: PermissionData
113. 	): void {
114. 		if (interaction.state !== "Pending") {
115. 			return;
116. 		}

123. 				replyHandler:

127. 				permission: payload.permission,
128. 				patterns: payload.patterns,
129. 				metadata: payload.metadata,
130. 				always: payload.always,
131. 				tool: payload.tool,
132. 			})
133. 		);
134. 	}
135. 
136. 	private applyQuestionInteraction(interaction: InteractionSnapshot, payload: QuestionData): void {
137. 		const request = this.buildQuestionRequest(interaction, payload);
138. 		if (interaction.state === "Pending") {
139. 			this.questionsPending.set(request.id, request);
140. 			return;
141. 		}
142. 
143. 		if (interaction.state !== "Answered" && interaction.state !== "Rejected") {
144. 			return;
145. 		}
146. 
147. 		const toolCallId = request.tool?.callID ?? request.id;
148. 		const answeredQuestion: AnsweredQuestion = {
149. 			questions: request.questions,
150. 			answers: this.extractQuestionAnswers(request.questions, interaction.response),
151. 			answeredAt: interaction.responded_at_event_seq ?? 0,
152. 			cancelled: interaction.state === "Rejected" ? true : undefined,
153. 		};
154. 		this.answeredQuestionSessionIds.set(toolCallId, interaction.session_id);
155. 		this.answeredQuestions.set(toolCallId, answeredQuestion);
156. 	}

96. 	// === PRIMARY STATE ===

105.

108. 
109. 	// Capabilities store (ACP configuration - models, modes, commands)
110. 	private readonly capabilitiesStore = new SessionCapabilitiesStore();
111. 
112. 	// Canonical tool execution domain state
113. 	private readonly operationStore = new OperationStore();
114. 
115. 	// Entry store (entries + chunk aggregation)
116. 	private readonly entryStore = new SessionEntryStore(this.operationStore);
117.

120. 	private readonly prDetailsInflight = new Map<string, ResultAsync<PrDetails | null, never>>();

367. 					} else if (actionId === "view") {
368. 						focusOrOpenSessionPanel(permission.sessionId);

24. 		permission?: PermissionRequest | null;

44. 		if (!currentPermission) {
45. 		return false;

57. 		if (!sessionProgress) {
58. 			return "";
59. 		}
60. 
61. 		const currentStep =
62. 			sessionProgress.completed + 1 <= sessionProgress.total
63. 				? sessionProgress.completed + 1
64. 				: sessionProgress.total;
65. 		return `Permission ${currentStep} of ${sessionProgress.total}`;
66. 	});

71. {#if currentPermission}

91. 						{:else if kind === "read"}

96. 							<MagnifyingGlass weight="fill" size={11} class="shrink-0" style="color: {purpleColor}" />
97. 						{:else if kind === "fetch" || kind === "web_search"}

99. 						{:else if kind === "delete"}

101. 						{:else if kind === "move"}
102. 							<ArrowsLeftRight weight="fill" size={11} class="shrink-0" style="color: {purpleColor}" />

### 💭 Reasoning

***Investigating session callbacks**

I’m thinking the session event service callback might not be adding correctly. I should look into the hydrateInteractionProjection; perhaps it populates the interactionStore using server interactions. But I'm curious, does it handle live updates too? Maybe I need to search for permissionStore.add to see how that plays into the whole picture. There's a lot here to analyze to figure out what’s going on!*

<sub>⏱️ 144m 18s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found something suspicious in the wiring: the live `onPermissionRequest` callback in `main-app-view.svelte` shows a notification and hydrates projection state, but I haven’t seen it explicitly add the permission into the pending interaction store yet. I’m checking whether that add happens anywhere else or if the callback is effectively side-effect-only.

345. // Set up SessionStore callbacks for permission/question/plan routing
346. sessionStore.setCallbacks({
347. 	onPermissionRequest: (permission) => {

349. 			showNotification(
350. 				{
351. 					id: permission.id,
352. 					type: "permission",
353. 					title: permission.permission,
354. 					body: permission.patterns.join(", "),

356. 					sessionId: permission.sessionId,
357. 					sourceId: permission.id,
358. 				},
359. 				(actionId) => {
360. 					if (!interactionStore.permissionsPending.has(permission.id)) return;
361. 					if (actionId === "allow") {
362. 						permissionStore.reply(permission.id, "once");
363. 					} else if (actionId === "allow-always") {
364. 						permissionStore.reply(permission.id, "always");
365. 					} else if (actionId === "deny") {
366. 						permissionStore.reply(permission.id, "reject");
367. 					} else if (actionId === "view") {

<sub>⏱️ 144m 25s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Investigating permission handling**

I’m realizing that in the main-app-view callback, permission isn’t added directly. It actually relies on the hydrateInteractionProjection function with the session ID. This function probably loads the session projection from the backend and updates the interactionStore. If hydrateInteractionProjection is asynchronous, or if it doesn’t handle pending interactions, then the UI might not update. I need to inspect the hydrateInteractionProjection function and search both in the main-app-view and utility sections.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 144m 25s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the likely gap. The live callback does **not** insert the permission into `interactionStore.permissionsPending` directly. It only calls `hydrateInteractionProjection(...)` and hopes the projection loader repopulates the pending interaction from the backend snapshot. If that projection path misses or lags, the UI has nothing to render. I’m checking that hydrator next.

---

1. import { ResultAsync, okAsync, type ResultAsync as ResultAsyncType } from "neverthrow";
2. import type { SessionProjectionSnapshot } from "../../../services/acp-types.js";
3. import { AgentError, AppError } from "../../errors/app-error.js";
4. import { api } from "../api.js";
5. 
6. interface SessionProjectionConsumer {
7. 	replaceSessionProjection(projection: SessionProjectionSnapshot): void;
8. 	clearSession(sessionId: string): void;
9. }
10. 
11. export class SessionProjectionHydrator {
12. 	private readonly inflight = new Map<string, ResultAsyncType<void, AppError>>();
13. 	private readonly queuedRefreshes = new Set<string>();
14. 
15. 	constructor(private readonly interactions: SessionProjectionConsumer) {}
16. 
17. 	hydrateSession(sessionId: string): ResultAsync<void, AppError> {
18. 		const existing = this.inflight.get(sessionId);
19. 		if (existing !== undefined) {
20. 			this.queuedRefreshes.add(sessionId);
21. 			return existing;
22. 		}
23. 
24. 		const request = ResultAsync.fromPromise(
25. 			this.hydrateUntilSettled(sessionId),
26. 			(error) => toAppError(error)
27. 		);
28. 
29. 		this.inflight.set(sessionId, request);
30. 		void request.match(
31. 			() => {
32. 				this.inflight.delete(sessionId);
33. 			},
34. 			() => {
35. 				this.inflight.delete(sessionId);
36. 			}
37. 		);
38. 		return request;
39. 	}
40. 
41. 	clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
42. 		this.queuedRefreshes.delete(sessionId);
43. 		this.interactions.clearSession(sessionId);
44. 	}
45. 
46. 	private async hydrateUntilSettled(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
47. 		while (true) {
48. 			this.queuedRefreshes.delete(sessionId);
49. 			const result = await api.getSessionProjection(sessionId).match(
50. 				(projection) => {
51. 					this.interactions.replaceSessionProjection(projection);
52. 					return { ok: true as const };
53. 				},
54. 				(error) => ({ ok: false as const, error })
55. 			);
56. 			if (!result.ok) {
57. 				throw result.error;
58. 			}
59. 
60. 			if (!this.queuedRefreshes.has(sessionId)) {
61. 				return;
62. 			}
63. 		}
64. 	}
65. }
66. 
67. function toAppError(error: AppError | Error | unknown): AppError {
68. 	if (error instanceof AppError) {
69. 		return error;
70. 	}
71. 
72. 	return new AgentError(
73. 		"getSessionProjection",
74. 		error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error))
75. 	);
76. }
77.
```

</details>

```
1. import { okAsync, type ResultAsync } from "neverthrow";
2. import type { SessionDomainEvent } from "../../../services/acp-types.js";
3. import type { AcpError } from "../../errors/index.js";
4. import type { AppError } from "../../errors/app-error.js";
5. import { createLogger } from "../../utils/logger.js";
6. import type { SessionDomainEventSubscriber } from "../../logic/session-domain-event-subscriber.js";
7. import { LOGGER_IDS } from "../../constants/logger-ids.js";
8. 
9. interface InteractionProjectionHydrator {
10. 	hydrateSession(sessionId: string): ResultAsync<void, AppError>;
11. }
12. 
13. type DomainEventSubscriberLike = Pick<
14. 	SessionDomainEventSubscriber,
15. 	"subscribe" | "unsubscribeById"
16. >;
17. 
18. function shouldRefreshProjection(event: SessionDomainEvent): boolean {
19. 	return (
20. 		event.kind === "interaction_upserted" ||
21. 		event.kind === "interaction_resolved" ||
22. 		event.kind === "interaction_cancelled"
23. 	);
24. }
25. 
26. export class LiveInteractionProjectionSync {
27. 	private listenerId: string | null = null;
28. 	private readonly logger = createLogger({
29. 		id: LOGGER_IDS.SESSION_DOMAIN_EVENT_SUBSCRIBER,
30. 		name: "Live Interaction Projection Sync",
31. 	});
32. 
33. 	constructor(
34. 		private readonly subscriber: DomainEventSubscriberLike,
35. 		private readonly hydrator: InteractionProjectionHydrator
36. 	) {}
37. 
38. 	start(): ResultAsync<void, AcpError> {
39. 		if (this.listenerId !== null) {
40. 			return okAsync(undefined);
41. 		}
42. 
43. 		return this.subscriber
44. 			.subscribe((event) => {
45. 				if (!shouldRefreshProjection(event)) {
46. 					return;
47. 				}
48. 
49. 				void this.hydrator.hydrateSession(event.session_id).match(
50. 					() => {},
51. 					(error) => {
52. 						this.logger.error("Failed to hydrate interaction projection from domain event", {
53. 							sessionId: event.session_id,
54. 							kind: event.kind,
55. 							error,
56. 						});
57. 					}
58. 				);
59. 			})
60. 			.map((listenerId) => {
61. 				this.listenerId = listenerId;
62. 				return undefined;
63. 			});
64. 	}
65. 
66. 	stop(): void {
67. 		if (this.listenerId === null) {
68. 			return;
69. 		}
70. 
71. 		this.subscriber.unsubscribeById(this.listenerId);
72. 		this.listenerId = null;
73. 	}
74. }
75.
```

</details>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Exploring Live Interaction Sync**

I'm noticing that LiveInteractionProjectionSync is subscribing to domain events and hydrating, which is interesting. It seems like the permission request should already trigger through these domain events. I want to check if the live sync has started in the main-app-view. I'll need to do some searching to confirm this. There's definitely something intriguing here about how these components are interacting, and I want to make sure I understand it fully!*

---

<sub>⏱️ 144m 39s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The UI path is projection-based: a permission update only becomes visible if the session projection hydrator can fetch a pending interaction snapshot. I’m checking whether that live projection sync is actually running and whether `getSessionProjection(sessionId)` is returning the pending permission.

<sub>⏱️ 145m 1s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Investigating session commands**

I need to inspect the Tauri session commands, particularly get_session_projection and projection registry runtime state, to see if the current interaction is included. It seems like the live app might use a projection hydrator, but the backend isn't recording interactions for permission requests. I should search the projections module for request_permission to identify the root cause, as I've noticed permission requests in the logs without any updates to the UI projection. I'll also look into projection updates on inbound permission in Rust.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 145m 3s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The projection-based UI only works if the backend records a pending interaction snapshot. I’m checking whether `session/request_permission` actually writes into the projection registry, because if it doesn’t, the hydrator has nothing to load and the prompt stays invisible.

---

1. use crate::acp::parsers::{get_parser, AgentType};
2. use crate::acp::session_update::{build_tool_call_from_raw, RawToolCallInput, ToolCallStatus};
3. use crate::acp::streaming_log::log_streaming_event;
4. use crate::acp::tool_classification::{resolve_raw_tool_identity, ToolClassificationHints};
5. use serde_json::Value;
6. 
7. use super::helpers::{
8.     build_permission_request_log_payload, parse_params, parse_permission_tool_arguments,
9. };
10. use super::types::SessionRequestPermissionParamsRaw;
11. use super::{InboundRoutingDecision, SyntheticToolCallContext};
12. 
13. pub(super) async fn handle_session_request_permission(
14.     params: &Value,
15.     agent_type: AgentType,
16. ) -> InboundRoutingDecision {
17.     let parsed: SessionRequestPermissionParamsRaw = match parse_params(params) {
18.         Ok(parsed) => parsed,
19.         Err(_) => {
20.             return InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi {
21.                 parsed_arguments: None,
22.                 synthetic_tool_call: None,
23.             };
24.         }
25.     };
26. 
27.     let SessionRequestPermissionParamsRaw {
28.         session_id,
29.         tool_call,
30.     } = parsed;
31. 
32.     let tool_call = match tool_call {
33.         Some(tool_call) => tool_call,
34.         None => {
35.             return InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi {
36.                 parsed_arguments: None,
37.                 synthetic_tool_call: None,
38.             };
39.         }
40.     };
41. 
42.     let parser = get_parser(agent_type);
43.     let tool_call_id = tool_call
44.         .tool_call_id
45.         .clone()
46.         .unwrap_or_else(|| "permission-request".to_string());
47.     let identity = resolve_raw_tool_identity(
48.         parser,
49.         &tool_call_id,
50.         Some(&tool_call.raw_input),
51.         ToolClassificationHints {
52.             name: tool_call.name.as_deref(),
53.             title: tool_call.title.as_deref(),
54.             kind: tool_call
55.                 .kind
56.                 .as_deref()
57.                 .map(|kind| parser.detect_tool_kind(kind)),
58.             kind_hint: tool_call.kind.as_deref(),
59.             locations: None,
60.         },
61.     );
62.     let parsed_arguments = parse_permission_tool_arguments(
63.         &tool_call_id,
64.         tool_call.name.as_deref(),
65.         tool_call.title.as_deref(),
66.         tool_call.raw_input.clone(),
67.         tool_call.kind.as_deref(),
68.         agent_type,
69.     );
70. 
71.     if let Some(session_id) = session_id.as_deref() {
72.         let payload = build_permission_request_log_payload(
73.             "session/request_permission",
74.             params,
75.             tool_call.name.as_deref(),
76.             tool_call.title.as_deref(),
77.             parsed_arguments.as_ref(),
78.             false,
79.             None,
80.         );
81.         log_streaming_event(session_id, &payload);
82.     }
83. 
84.     // Build ToolCallData via the same pipeline as normal tool_call events.
85.     let synthetic_tool_call = match (&tool_call.tool_call_id, &session_id) {
86.         (Some(tool_call_id), Some(_)) => {
87.             let raw = RawToolCallInput {
88.                 id: tool_call_id.clone(),
89.                 name: identity.name.clone(),
90.                 arguments: tool_call.raw_input.clone(),
91.                 status: ToolCallStatus::InProgress,
92.                 kind: Some(identity.kind),
93.                 title: tool_call.title.clone(),
94.                 suppress_title_read_path_hint: false,
95.                 parent_tool_use_id: None,
96.                 task_children: None,
97.             };
98.             let tool_call_data = build_tool_call_from_raw(parser, raw);
99.             Some(Box::new(SyntheticToolCallContext { tool_call_data }))
100.         }
101.         _ => None,
102.     };
103. 
104.     InboundRoutingDecision::ForwardToUi {
105.         parsed_arguments,
106.         synthetic_tool_call,
107.     }
108. }
109.
```

</details>

139.     operation_id_by_tool_key: Arc<DashMap<String, String>>,
140.     session_operation_ids: Arc<DashMap<String, Vec<String>>>,
141.     interactions_by_id: Arc<DashMap<String, InteractionSnapshot>>,
142.     interaction_id_by_request_key: Arc<DashMap<String, String>>,
143.     session_interaction_ids: Arc<DashMap<String, Vec<String>>>,
144. }
145. 
146. impl ProjectionRegistry {
147.     #[must_use]
148.     pub fn new() -> Self {
149.         Self {
150.             snapshots: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
151.             operations_by_id: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
152.             operation_id_by_tool_key: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
153.             session_operation_ids: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
154.             interactions_by_id: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
155.             interaction_id_by_request_key: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
156.             session_interaction_ids: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),
157.         }
158.     }
159. 
160.     pub fn register_session(&self, session_id: String, agent_id: CanonicalAgentId) {
161.         let _ = self
162.             .snapshots
163.             .entry(session_id.clone())
164.             .and_modify(|snapshot| {
165.                 snapshot.agent_id = Some(agent_id.clone());
166.             })
167.             .or_insert_with(|| SessionSnapshot::new(session_id, Some(agent_id)));
168.     }
169. 
170.     pub fn restore_session_projection(&self, projection: SessionProjectionSnapshot) {
171.         let session_id = projection
172.             .session
173.             .as_ref()
174.             .map(|session| session.session_id.clone())
175.             .or_else(|| {
176.                 projection
177.                     .operations
178.                     .first()
179.                     .map(|operation| operation.session_id.clone())
180.             })
181.             .or_else(|| {
182.                 projection
183.                     .interactions
184.                     .first()
185.                     .map(|interaction| interaction.session_id.clone())
186.             });
187.         let Some(session_id) = session_id else {
188.             return;
189.         };
190. 
191.         self.remove_session(&session_id);
192.         if let Some(session) = projection.session {
193.             self.snapshots.insert(session_id.clone(), session);
194.         }
195.         for operation in projection.operations {
196.             self.operations_by_id
197.                 .insert(operation.id.clone(), operation.clone());
198.             self.operation_id_by_tool_key.insert(
199.                 create_session_tool_key(&session_id, &operation.tool_call_id),
200.                 operation.id.clone(),
201.             );
202.             self.insert_session_operation_id(&session_id, &operation.id);
203.         }
204.         for interaction in projection.interactions {
205.             self.upsert_interaction(interaction);
206.         }
207.     }
208. 
209.     #[must_use]
210.     pub fn project_converted_session(
211.         session_id: &str,
212.         agent_id: Option<CanonicalAgentId>,
213.         converted_session: &ConvertedSession,
214.     ) -> SessionProjectionSnapshot {
215.         let registry = Self::new();
216.         registry.import_converted_session(session_id, agent_id, converted_session);
217.         registry.session_projection(session_id)
218.     }
219. 
220.     pub fn remove_session(&self, session_id: &str) {
221.         self.snapshots.remove(session_id);
222.         if let Some((_, operation_ids)) = self.session_operation_ids.remove(session_id) {
223.             for operation_id in operation_ids {
224.                 if let Some((_, operation)) = self.operations_by_id.remove(&operation_id) {
225.                     self.operation_id_by_tool_key
226.                         .remove(&create_session_tool_key(
227.                             session_id,
228.                             &operation.tool_call_id,
229.                         ));
230.                 }
231.             }
232.         }
233.         if let Some((_, interaction_ids)) = self.session_interaction_ids.remove(session_id) {
234.             for interaction_id in interaction_ids {
235.                 if let Some((_, interaction)) = self.interactions_by_id.remove(&interaction_id) {
236.                     if let Some(request_id) = interaction.json_rpc_request_id {
237.                         self.interaction_id_by_request_key
238.                             .remove(&create_session_request_key(session_id, request_id));
239.                     }
240.                 }
241.             }
242.         }
243.     }
244. 
245.     #[must_use]
246.     pub fn snapshot_for_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<SessionSnapshot> {
247.         self.snapshots.get(session_id).map(|entry| entry.clone())
248.     }
249. 
250.     pub fn apply_session_update(&self, session_id: &str, update: &SessionUpdate) {
251.         let mut snapshot = self
252.             .snapshots
253.             .entry(session_id.to_string())
254.             .or_insert_with(|| SessionSnapshot::new(session_id.to_string(), None));
255. 
256.         snapshot.last_event_seq = snapshot.last_event_seq.saturating_add(1);
257. 
258.         match update {
259.             SessionUpdate::UserMessageChunk { .. } => {
260.                 snapshot.message_count = snapshot.message_count.saturating_add(1);

216. 
217. pub(crate) fn interaction_transition_from_result(
218.     interaction_kind: &InteractionKind,
219.     adapted_result: &Value,
220. ) -> Option<(
221.     InteractionState,
222.     SessionDomainEventKind,
223.     InteractionResponse,
224. )> {
225.     match interaction_kind {
226.         InteractionKind::Permission => {
227.             let outcome = adapted_result
228.                 .pointer("/outcome/outcome")
229.                 .and_then(Value::as_str)?;
230.             let option_id = adapted_result
231.                 .pointer("/outcome/optionId")
232.                 .and_then(Value::as_str)
233.                 .map(ToString::to_string);
234.             let accepted = matches!(outcome, "allowed" | "selected");
235.             let state = if accepted {
236.                 InteractionState::Approved
237.             } else if outcome == "cancelled" {
238.                 InteractionState::Rejected
239.             } else {
240.                 return None;
241.             };
242.             let domain_event_kind = if accepted {
243.                 SessionDomainEventKind::InteractionResolved
244.             } else {
245.                 SessionDomainEventKind::InteractionCancelled
246.             };
247.             Some((
248.                 state,
249.                 domain_event_kind,
250.                 InteractionResponse::Permission {
251.                     accepted,
252.                     option_id,
253.                     reply: None,
254.                 },
255.             ))
256.         }
257.         InteractionKind::Question => {
258.             let outcome = adapted_result
259.                 .pointer("/outcome/outcome")
260.                 .and_then(Value::as_str)?;
```

554.         };
555.         let Some(existing) = self
556.             .operations_by_id
557.             .get(&operation_id)
558.             .map(|operation| operation.clone())
559.         else {
560.             return;
561.         };
562. 
563.         let next_status = update.status.clone().unwrap_or(existing.status);
564.         let next_arguments = update
565.             .arguments
566.             .clone()
567.             .unwrap_or_else(|| existing.arguments.clone());
568.         let next_progressive_arguments =
569.             if let Some(streaming_arguments) = update.streaming_arguments.clone() {
570.                 Some(streaming_arguments)
571.             } else if update.arguments.is_some()
572.                 || update
573.                     .status
574.                     .as_ref()
575.                     .is_some_and(is_terminal_tool_call_status)
576.             {
577.                 None
578.             } else {
579.                 existing.progressive_arguments.clone()
580.             };
581.         let next_title = update.title.clone().or(existing.title.clone());
582.         let next_result = update.result.clone().or(existing.result.clone());
583. 
584.         self.operations_by_id.insert(
585.             operation_id,
586.             OperationSnapshot {
587.                 id: existing.id,
588.                 session_id: existing.session_id,
589.                 tool_call_id: existing.tool_call_id,
590.                 name: existing.name,
591.                 kind: existing.kind,
592.                 status: next_status,
593.                 title: next_title.clone(),
594.                 arguments: next_arguments.clone(),
595.                 progressive_arguments: next_progressive_arguments.clone(),
596.                 result: next_result,
597.                 command: extract_operation_command(
598.                     Some(&next_arguments),
599.                     next_progressive_arguments.as_ref(),
600.                     next_title.as_deref(),
601.                 ),
602.                 parent_tool_call_id: existing.parent_tool_call_id,
603.                 parent_operation_id: existing.parent_operation_id,
604.                 child_tool_call_ids: existing.child_tool_call_ids,
605.                 child_operation_ids: existing.child_operation_ids,
606.             },
607.         );
608.     }
609. 
610.     fn insert_session_operation_id(&self, session_id: &str, operation_id: &str) {
611.         let mut operation_ids = self
612.             .session_operation_ids
613.             .entry(session_id.to_string())
614.             .or_default();
615.         if operation_ids
616.             .iter()
617.             .any(|candidate| candidate == operation_id)
618.         {
619.             return;
620.         }
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 145m 22s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating permission interaction**

I'm trying to figure out if the projection registry can register permission interactions when `apply_session_update` sees a `SessionUpdate::PermissionRequest`. Is this occurring for inbound requests? I should inspect the `client_loop` or `dispatcher` to see if they call `projection_registry.apply_session_update` during the `inbound_request` event. Maybe they don’t. I think I should search for where `apply_session_update` is called to clarify this.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 145m 23s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The backend *can* project pending permissions, but only when it sees a `SessionUpdate::PermissionRequest`. I’m tracing whether the Copilot inbound request path actually emits that session update into the projection registry, because that’s the exact bridge the UI depends on.

```
1. use crate::acp::domain_events::{SessionDomainEvent, SessionDomainEventKind};
2. use crate::acp::event_hub::AcpEventHubState;
3. use crate::acp::projections::ProjectionRegistry;
4. use crate::acp::session_update::SessionUpdate;
5. use crate::db::repository::SessionJournalEventRepository;
6. use sea_orm::DbConn;
7. use serde_json::Value;
8. use std::collections::{HashMap, VecDeque};
9. use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicI64, Ordering};
10. use std::sync::Arc;
11. use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
12. use tauri::{AppHandle, Manager};
13. use tokio::sync::mpsc;
14. use uuid::Uuid;
15. 
16. const TELEMETRY_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5);
17. 
18. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
19. pub enum AcpUiEventPriority {
20.     Normal,
21.     High,
22. }
23. 
24. impl AcpUiEventPriority {
25.     fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
26.         match self {
27.             Self::Normal => "normal",
28.             Self::High => "high",
29.         }
30.     }
31. }
32. 
33. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
34. pub enum AcpUiEventPayload {
35.     SessionUpdate(Box<SessionUpdate>),
36.     SessionDomainEvent(Box<SessionDomainEvent>),
37.     Json(Value),
38. }
39. 
40. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
41. pub struct AcpUiEvent {
42.     pub session_id: Option<String>,
43.     pub event_name: &'static str,
44.     pub payload: AcpUiEventPayload,
45.     pub priority: AcpUiEventPriority,
46.     pub droppable: bool,
47.     pub created_at: Instant,
48. }
49. 
50. impl AcpUiEvent {
51.     #[must_use]
52.     pub fn session_update(update: SessionUpdate) -> Self {
53.         let session_id = update.session_id().map(ToString::to_string);
54.         let priority = match &update {
55.             SessionUpdate::PermissionRequest { .. } | SessionUpdate::QuestionRequest { .. } => {
56.                 AcpUiEventPriority::High
57.             }
58.             _ => AcpUiEventPriority::Normal,
59.         };
60. 
61.         let droppable = match &update {
62.             SessionUpdate::AgentMessageChunk { .. }
63.             | SessionUpdate::AgentThoughtChunk { .. }
64.             | SessionUpdate::UserMessageChunk { .. } => true,
65.             SessionUpdate::ToolCallUpdate { update, .. } => update.streaming_input_delta.is_some(),
66.             _ => false,
67.         };
68. 
69.         Self {
70.             session_id,
71.             event_name: "acp-session-update",
72.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::SessionUpdate(Box::new(update)),
73.             priority,
74.             droppable,
75.             created_at: Instant::now(),
76.         }
77.     }
78. 
79.     #[must_use]
80.     pub fn inbound_request(request: Value) -> Self {
81.         let session_id = request
82.             .get("params")
83.             .and_then(|params| params.get("sessionId"))
84.             .and_then(Value::as_str)
85.             .map(ToString::to_string);
86. 
87.         Self {
88.             session_id,
89.             event_name: "acp-inbound-request",
90.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::Json(request),
91.             priority: AcpUiEventPriority::High,
92.             droppable: false,
93.             created_at: Instant::now(),
94.         }
95.     }
96. 
97.     #[must_use]
98.     pub fn session_domain_event(event: SessionDomainEvent) -> Self {
99.         let session_id = Some(event.session_id.clone());
100. 
101.         Self {
102.             session_id,
103.             event_name: "acp-session-domain-event",
104.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::SessionDomainEvent(Box::new(event)),
105.             priority: AcpUiEventPriority::Normal,
106.             droppable: false,
107.             created_at: Instant::now(),
108.         }
109.     }
110. 
111.     #[must_use]
112.     pub fn json_event(
113.         event_name: &'static str,
114.         payload: Value,
115.         session_id: Option<String>,
116.         priority: AcpUiEventPriority,
117.         droppable: bool,
118.     ) -> Self {
119.         Self {
120.             session_id,
121.             event_name,
122.             payload: AcpUiEventPayload::Json(payload),
123.             priority,
124.             droppable,
125.             created_at: Instant::now(),
126.         }
127.     }
128. 
129.     fn to_json_payload(&self) -> Result<Value, serde_json::Error> {
130.         match &self.payload {
131.             AcpUiEventPayload::SessionUpdate(update) => serde_json::to_value(update.as_ref()),
132.             AcpUiEventPayload::SessionDomainEvent(event) => serde_json::to_value(event.as_ref()),
133.             AcpUiEventPayload::Json(value) => Ok(value.clone()),
134.         }
135.     }
136. 
137.     fn publish(&self, hub: &AcpEventHubState) -> Result<(), serde_json::Error> {
138.         let payload = self.to_json_payload()?;
139.         hub.publish(
140.             self.event_name,
141.             self.session_id.clone(),
142.             payload,
143.             self.priority.as_str(),
144.             self.droppable,
145.         );
146.         Ok(())
147.     }
148. }
149. 
150. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
151. pub struct DispatchPolicy {
152.     pub tokens_per_sec: f64,
153.     pub burst: f64,
154.     pub max_global_backlog: usize,
155.     pub max_session_backlog: usize,
156.     pub high_backlog_threshold: usize,
157.     pub base_batch_size: usize,
158.     pub high_backlog_batch_size: usize,
159.     pub min_spacing_ms: u64,
160.     pub high_backlog_spacing_ms: u64,
161. }
162. 
163. impl Default for DispatchPolicy {
164.     fn default() -> Self {
165.         Self {
166.             tokens_per_sec: 300.0,
167.             burst: 30.0,
168.             max_global_backlog: 5000,
169.             max_session_backlog: 500,
170.             high_backlog_threshold: 1000,
171.             base_batch_size: 32,
172.             high_backlog_batch_size: 8,
173.             min_spacing_ms: 0,
174.             high_backlog_spacing_ms: 5,
175.         }
176.     }
177. }
178. 
179. struct DispatcherTelemetry {
180.     enqueued: u64,
181.     emitted: u64,
182.     dropped: u64,
183.     max_backlog: usize,
184.     max_wait_ms: u128,
185.     last_report: Instant,
186. }
187. 
188. impl DispatcherTelemetry {
189.     fn new() -> Self {
190.         Self {
191.             enqueued: 0,
192.             emitted: 0,
193.             dropped: 0,
194.             max_backlog: 0,
195.             max_wait_ms: 0,
196.             last_report: Instant::now(),
197.         }
198.     }
199. 
200.     fn maybe_report(&mut self, backlog: usize) {
201.         self.max_backlog = self.max_backlog.max(backlog);
202. 
203.         if self.last_report.elapsed() < TELEMETRY_INTERVAL {
204.             return;
205.         }
206. 
207.         tracing::debug!(
208.             enqueued = self.enqueued,
209.             emitted = self.emitted,
210.             dropped = self.dropped,
211.             max_backlog = self.max_backlog,
212.             max_wait_ms = self.max_wait_ms,
213.             "ACP UI dispatcher telemetry"
214.         );
215. 
216.         if self.dropped > 0 {
217.             tracing::warn!(
218.                 dropped = self.dropped,
219.                 max_backlog = self.max_backlog,
220.                 "ACP UI dispatcher dropped events"
221.             );
222.         }
223. 
224.         self.enqueued = 0;
225.         self.emitted = 0;
226.         self.dropped = 0;
227.         self.max_backlog = 0;
228.         self.max_wait_ms = 0;
229.         self.last_report = Instant::now();
230.     }
231. }
232.

<sub>⏱️ 145m 36s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the missing bridge.

The backend only updates live interaction projection state for **`SessionUpdate`** events. But Copilot permissions are going out as **`acp-inbound-request` JSON only**, plus a synthetic tool call. That means the permission responder exists, but the projection registry never records a pending permission interaction for the UI to hydrate.

I’m confirming that the inbound-request frontend handler also doesn’t insert it directly, because if so this is the actual hole.

---
