<sub>⏱️ 159m 46s</sub>

### 👤 User

Ok now it worked, but such an apply patch Because this `apply_patch` tool supports **file-level operations**, not just line edits.

Its patch grammar includes commands like:
- `*** Add File: ...`
- `*** Update File: ...`
- `*** Delete File: ...`

So when I sent:

```patch
*** Begin Patch
*** Delete File: /Users/alex/Documents/sandbox/hello-go/README.md
*** End Patch
```

the tool interpreted that as an instruction to remove the file from disk entirely. should be considered a delete tool call and displayed as such. also align the ui of the delete tool call card with the rest of the cards uis

<sub>⏱️ 160m 20s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Investigating display issues**

I’m searching for tests related to session_update, especially around applying patches and deletes. It seems like the ToolKind for delete might already exist in session_update, but there could be mapping issues with the UI—specifically how apply_patch is linked to Edit, which could be causing a display problem. I need to take a closer look at relevant files like the Rust parser, entry converter, and the tool-call-delete.svelte. Maybe parallel views could help too.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 160m 21s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found likely split points: classification is partly already aware of `Delete File`, but `apply_patch` still gets normalized as edit in some UI-facing paths. I’m reading the exact classifier and delete card components so I can fix the root cause instead of papering over the label.

---

/Users/alex/Documents/acepe/packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/acp/parsers/kind.rs (lines 1-320)

<details>
<summary>296 lines</summary>

```
1. //! Tool kind inference and canonical naming.
2. //!
3. //! These functions were previously private methods on `CodexParser` but are
4. //! agent-agnostic — they operate on `ToolKind` and ACP payload conventions
5. //! shared across all agents.
6. 
7. use crate::acp::session_update::ToolKind;
8. 
9. /// Infer `ToolKind` from an ACP payload's `kind` field value.
10. ///
11. /// The `kind` field is a hint string sent by the agent (e.g. `"read"`, `"edit"`).
12. /// When the kind is `"search"` and contextual signals indicate a web search,
13. /// returns `ToolKind::WebSearch` instead.
14. pub fn infer_kind_from_payload(
15.     id: &str,
16.     title: Option<&str>,
17.     kind_value: Option<&str>,
18. ) -> Option<ToolKind> {
19.     let kind_opt: Option<String> = kind_value
20.         .map(str::trim)
21.         .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
22.         .map(|s| s.to_lowercase());
23. 
24.     // When kind is "other" or missing, infer from title (safety net for live + historical)
25.     if kind_opt.as_deref() == Some("other") || kind_opt.is_none() {
26.         if let Some(t) = title {
27.             let t = t.trim().to_lowercase();
28.             // Edit
29.             if t == "apply patch"
30.                 || t == "apply_patch"
31.                 || t == "str replace"
32.                 || t == "strreplace"
33.                 || t == "edit file"
34.                 || t == "editfile"
35.             {
36.                 return Some(ToolKind::Edit);
37.             }
38.             // Read
39.             if t == "read file" || t == "readfile" || t == "view_image" || t == "viewimage" {
40.                 return Some(ToolKind::Read);
41.             }
42.             // Delete
43.             if t == "delete file" || t == "deletefile" {
44.                 return Some(ToolKind::Delete);
45.             }
46.             // Execute / terminal
47.             if t == "exec_command"
48.                 || t == "execcommand"
49.                 || t == "write_stdin"
50.                 || t == "writestdin"
51.                 || t == "terminal"
52.                 || t == "run terminal cmd"
53.             {
54.                 return Some(ToolKind::Execute);
55.             }
56.             // Search (codebase)
57.             if t == "codebase search"
58.                 || t == "codebasesearch"
59.                 || t == "grep"
60.                 || t == "rg"
61.                 || t == "ripgrep"
62.             {
63.                 return Some(ToolKind::Search);
64.             }
65.         }
66.     }
67. 
68.     let kind = kind_opt?;
69. 
70.     let is_web_search = is_web_search_id(id) || title.map(is_web_search_title).unwrap_or(false);
71. 
72.     match kind.as_str() {
73.         "read" => Some(ToolKind::Read),
74.         "edit" => Some(ToolKind::Edit),
75.         "execute" => Some(ToolKind::Execute),
76.         "search" => Some(if is_web_search {
77.             ToolKind::WebSearch
78.         } else {
79.             ToolKind::Search
80.         }),
81.         "glob" => Some(ToolKind::Glob),
82.         "fetch" => Some(ToolKind::Fetch),
83.         "web_search" | "websearch" | "web-search" => Some(ToolKind::WebSearch),
84.         "think" => Some(ToolKind::Think),
85.         "todo" => Some(ToolKind::Todo),
86.         "question" => Some(ToolKind::Question),
87.         "task" => Some(ToolKind::Task),
88.         "skill" => Some(ToolKind::Skill),
89.         "move" => Some(ToolKind::Move),
90.         "delete" => Some(ToolKind::Delete),
91.         "enter_plan_mode" | "enterplanmode" | "enter-plan-mode" => Some(ToolKind::EnterPlanMode),
92.         "exit_plan_mode" | "exitplanmode" | "exit-plan-mode" => Some(ToolKind::ExitPlanMode),
93.         "create_plan" | "createplan" | "create-plan" => Some(ToolKind::CreatePlan),
94.         "task_output" | "taskoutput" | "task-output" => Some(ToolKind::TaskOutput),
95.         "tool_search" | "toolsearch" | "tool-search" => Some(ToolKind::ToolSearch),
96.         _ => None,
97.     }
98. }
99. 
100. /// Canonical display name for a `ToolKind`.
101. ///
102. /// Used by the frontend's `formatOtherToolName()` to produce human-readable titles.
103. pub fn canonical_name_for_kind(kind: ToolKind) -> &'static str {
104.     match kind {
105.         ToolKind::Read => "Read",
106.         ToolKind::Edit => "Edit",
107.         ToolKind::Execute => "Run",
108.         ToolKind::Search => "Search",
109.         ToolKind::Glob => "Find",
110.         ToolKind::Fetch => "Fetch",
111.         ToolKind::WebSearch => "Web Search",
112.         ToolKind::Think => "Think",
113.         ToolKind::Todo => "Todo",
114.         ToolKind::Question => "Question",
115.         ToolKind::Task => "Task",
116.         ToolKind::Skill => "Skill",
117.         ToolKind::Move => "Move",
118.         ToolKind::Delete => "Delete",
119.         ToolKind::EnterPlanMode => "Plan",
120.         ToolKind::ExitPlanMode => "Plan",
121.         ToolKind::CreatePlan => "Create Plan",
122.         ToolKind::TaskOutput => "Task Output",
123.         ToolKind::ToolSearch => "Tool Search",
124.         ToolKind::Other => "Tool",
125.     }
126. }
127. 
128. /// Return the canonical display name for a known tool kind,
129. /// or fall back to the raw name for unknown tools.
130. pub fn display_name_for_tool(kind: ToolKind, raw_name: &str) -> String {
131.     if kind != ToolKind::Other {
132.         canonical_name_for_kind(kind).to_string()
133.     } else {
134.         raw_name.to_string()
135.     }
136. }
137. 
138. /// Check whether a tool-call ID looks like a web search.
139. pub fn is_web_search_id(id: &str) -> bool {
140.     id.starts_with("web_search_") || id.starts_with("ws_")
141. }
142. 
143. /// Check whether a title string indicates a web search.
144. pub fn is_web_search_title(title: &str) -> bool {
145.     title.to_lowercase().contains("searching the web")
146. }
147. 
148. /// Check whether arguments contain web-search signals.
149. pub fn looks_like_web_search_arguments(arguments: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {
150.     let Some(obj) = arguments.as_object() else {
151.         return false;
152.     };
153. 
154.     if obj.contains_key("query") {
155.         return true;
156.     }
157. 
158.     if let Some(action) = obj.get("action").and_then(|value| value.as_object()) {
159.         if action.get("type").and_then(|value| value.as_str()) == Some("search") {
160.             return true;
161.         }
162.         if action.get("query").is_some() || action.get("queries").is_some() {
163.             return true;
164.         }
165.     }
166. 
167.     false
168. }
169. 
170. #[cfg(test)]
171. mod tests {
172.     use super::*;
173. 
174.     #[test]
175.     fn infers_basic_kinds() {
176.         assert_eq!(
177.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", None, Some("read")),
178.             Some(ToolKind::Read)
179.         );
180.         assert_eq!(
181.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", None, Some("edit")),
182.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
183.         );
184.         assert_eq!(
185.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", None, Some("execute")),
186.             Some(ToolKind::Execute)
187.         );
188.         assert_eq!(infer_kind_from_payload("id", None, Some("other")), None);
189.     }
190. 
191.     #[test]
192.     fn search_becomes_web_search_with_ws_id() {
193.         assert_eq!(
194.             infer_kind_from_payload("ws_123", None, Some("search")),
195.             Some(ToolKind::WebSearch)
196.         );
197.     }
198. 
199.     #[test]
200.     fn returns_none_for_empty_kind() {
201.         assert_eq!(infer_kind_from_payload("id", None, Some("")), None);
202.         assert_eq!(infer_kind_from_payload("id", None, None), None);
203.     }
204. 
205.     #[test]
206.     fn infers_edit_from_apply_patch_title_when_kind_other_or_missing() {
207.         assert_eq!(
208.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Apply Patch"), Some("other")),
209.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
210.         );
211.         assert_eq!(
212.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Apply Patch"), None),
213.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
214.         );
215.         assert_eq!(
216.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("apply_patch"), Some("other")),
217.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
218.         );
219.     }
220. 
221.     #[test]
222.     fn infers_edit_from_str_replace_or_edit_file_title_when_kind_other_or_missing() {
223.         assert_eq!(
224.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Str Replace"), Some("other")),
225.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
226.         );
227.         assert_eq!(
228.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("str replace"), None),
229.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
230.         );
231.         assert_eq!(
232.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("strreplace"), Some("other")),
233.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
234.         );
235.         assert_eq!(
236.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Edit File"), Some("other")),
237.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
238.         );
239.         assert_eq!(
240.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("editfile"), None),
241.             Some(ToolKind::Edit)
242.         );
243.     }
244. 
245.     #[test]
246.     fn infers_read_delete_execute_search_from_title_when_kind_other_or_missing() {
247.         // Read
248.         assert_eq!(
249.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Read File"), Some("other")),
250.             Some(ToolKind::Read)
251.         );
252.         assert_eq!(
253.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("view_image"), None),
254.             Some(ToolKind::Read)
255.         );
256.         // Delete
257.         assert_eq!(
258.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Delete File"), Some("other")),
259.             Some(ToolKind::Delete)
260.         );
261.         // Execute
262.         assert_eq!(
263.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("exec_command"), Some("other")),
264.             Some(ToolKind::Execute)
265.         );
266.         assert_eq!(
267.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("write_stdin"), None),
268.             Some(ToolKind::Execute)
269.         );
270.         // Search
271.         assert_eq!(
272.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("Codebase Search"), Some("other")),
273.             Some(ToolKind::Search)
274.         );
275.         assert_eq!(
276.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("rg"), Some("other")),
277.             Some(ToolKind::Search)
278.         );
279.         assert_eq!(
280.             infer_kind_from_payload("id", Some("ripgrep"), None),
281.             Some(ToolKind::Search)
282.         );
283.     }
284. 
285.     #[test]

287.         assert_eq!(canonical_name_for_kind(ToolKind::Read), "Read");
288.         assert_eq!(canonical_name_for_kind(ToolKind::Execute), "Run");
289.         assert_eq!(canonical_name_for_kind(ToolKind::Glob), "Find");
290.         assert_eq!(canonical_name_for_kind(ToolKind::WebSearch), "Web Search");

293.         assert_eq!(canonical_name_for_kind(ToolKind::Other), "Tool");

1. <script lang="ts">
2. import { FilePathBadge } from "@acepe/ui/file-path-badge";
3. import { TextShimmer } from "@acepe/ui/text-shimmer";
4. import { Trash } from "phosphor-svelte";
5. import * as m from "$lib/paraglide/messages.js";
6. import { getSessionStore } from "../../store/index.js";
7. import type { TurnState } from "../../store/types.js";
8. import type { ToolCall } from "../../types/tool-call.js";

15. 	projectPath?: string;

17. }

20. 
21. const sessionStore = getSessionStore();

23. const isPending = $derived(toolStatus.isPending);
24. 
25. // Extract file path (streaming args first for progressive display)

27. 	const streamingArgs = sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id);

31. 	return toolCall.arguments.kind === "delete" ? toolCall.arguments.file_path : null;
32. });
33. const fileName = $derived(filePath ? getFileName(filePath) : null);
34. 
35. const label = $derived(isPending ? m.tool_delete_running() : m.tool_delete_completed());
36. </script>

36. export type ToolDetailComponentProps = {
37. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
38. 	turnState?: TurnState;
39. 	projectPath?: string;
40. 	elapsedLabel?: string | null;
41. 	pendingPermission?: PermissionRequest | null;
42. };
43. 
44. interface ToolDisplayOptions {
45. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
46. 	toolKind?: ToolKind | null;
47. 	turnState?: TurnState;
48. 	parentCompleted?: boolean;
49. }
50. 
51. export type CompactToolDisplay = Pick<
52. 	AgentToolEntry,
53. 	"id" | "kind" | "title" | "filePath" | "status"
54. >;
55. 
56. export interface ToolDefinition {
57. 	rendererKey: ToolRouteKey;
58. 	component: Component<ToolDetailComponentProps>;
59. 	buildFullEntry: (options: ToolDisplayOptions) => AgentToolEntry;
60. 	buildCompactEntry: (options: ToolDisplayOptions) => CompactToolDisplay;
61. }
62. 
63. function mapAgentToolStatus(
64. 	toolCall: ToolCall,
65. 	turnState: TurnState | undefined,
66. 	parentCompleted: boolean
67. ): AgentToolStatus {
68. 	if (toolCall.status === "failed") {
69. 		return "error";
70. 	}
71. 
72. 	if (toolCall.status === "completed") {
73. 		return "done";
74. 	}
75. 
76. 	const hasResult = toolCall.result !== null && toolCall.result !== undefined;
77. 	if (hasResult) {
78. 		return "done";
79. 	}
80. 
81. 	if (parentCompleted) {
82. 		return "done";
83. 	}
84. 
85. 	if (toolCall.status === "in_progress" && turnState === "streaming") {
86. 		return "running";
87. 	}
88. 
89. 	if (toolCall.status === "in_progress" && turnState !== undefined && turnState !== "streaming") {
90. 		return "done";
91. 	}
92. 
93. 	return "pending";
94. }
95. 
96. function compactToolEntry(entry: AgentToolEntry): CompactToolDisplay {
97. 	return {
98. 		id: entry.id,
99. 		kind: entry.kind,
100. 		title: entry.filePath ? entry.title : (entry.subtitle ?? entry.title),
101. 		filePath: entry.filePath,
102. 		status: entry.status,
103. 	};
104. }
105. 
106. function buildDefaultFullEntry(options: ToolDisplayOptions): AgentToolEntry {
107. 	const kind = options.toolKind ?? options.toolCall.kind ?? "other";
108. 
109. 	return {
110. 		id: options.toolCall.id,

118. 		filePath: getToolKindFilePath(kind, options.toolCall) ?? undefined,

1. /**
2.  * Entry Converter - Converts Rust backend entries to frontend StoredEntry format.
3.  *
4.  * This module handles the transformation between backend (Rust) and frontend (TypeScript)
5.  * data formats, specifically:
6.  * - Renaming `input` to `arguments` for tool calls (backend uses `input`, frontend expects `arguments`)
7.  * - Parsing timestamp strings to Date objects
8.  * - Type-safe discriminated union handling
9.  *
10.  * Design Decisions:
11.  * - Uses explicit switch statement instead of nested ternaries for readability
12.  * - Separates tool call conversion into dedicated function for clarity
13.  * - Returns new objects (immutable transformation)
14.  * - Handles all edge cases for timestamp and input fields
15.  */
16. 
17. import type { StoredEntry } from "../infrastructure/storage/ThreadStorage.js";
18.

36. /**

73. export type RustStoredEntry =
74. 	| {

76. 			id: string;

85. 	  }
86. 	| {
87. 			type: "tool_call";
88. 			id: string;
89. 			toolCall: RustStoredToolCall;
90. 			timestamp?: string;
91. 	  };
92.

99. 	name: string;
100. 	title?: string;
101. 	status: string;
102. 	result?: string;
103. 	kind: string;
104. 	arguments: Record<string, unknown>;
105. }
106. 
107. /**
108.  * Parse timestamp string to Date, with fallback to current time.
109.  *
110.  * @param timestamp - ISO timestamp string or undefined
111.  * @returns Date object
112.  */
113. function parseTimestamp(timestamp: string | undefined): Date {
114. 	if (timestamp && timestamp.length > 0) {
115. 		return new Date(timestamp);
116. 	}
117. 	return new Date();
118. }

### 💭 Reasoning

***Reviewing UI components**

I need to gather more files, particularly the tool-kind-ui-registry, and consider additional components for the delete card versus others. It seems important to inspect the tool-kind registry entries to ensure a consistent UI. The delete card might be quite custom, so I should align it with the rest of the components. I'll look into inspecting tool-call-edit.svelte or read.svelte to get clarity on this.*

<sub>⏱️ 160m 31s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The root cause is visible now: the backend test explicitly expects Copilot `*** Delete File` patches to stay `Edit`. I’m reading the edit and delete card implementations side by side so I can flip the classification and make the delete card reuse the shared card language instead of its one-off layout.

44. 	pendingPermission ? extractPermissionFilePath(pendingPermission) : null
45. );

47. function normalizeFilePath(value: string | null | undefined): string | null {
48. 	if (value == null) return null;
49. 	const trimmed = value.trim();
50. 	return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
51. }
52. 
53. const editDiffs = $derived.by(() => {
54. 	const streamingArgs = sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id);
55. 	const resolvedDiffs = resolveToolCallEditDiffs(toolCall.arguments, streamingArgs);
56. 
57. 	return resolvedDiffs.map((diff, index) => {
58. 		const filePath =
59. 			normalizeFilePath(diff.filePath) ??
60. 			(index === 0
61. 				? (normalizeFilePath(locationFilePath) ?? normalizeFilePath(permissionFilePath))
62. 				: null);
63. 		const diffStats = calculateDiffStats({
64. 			oldString: diff.oldString,
65. 			newString: diff.newString,
66. 		}) ?? { added: 0, removed: 0 };
67. 
68. 		return {
69. 			filePath,
70. 			fileName: getFileName(filePath),
71. 			oldString: diff.oldString,
72. 			newString: diff.newString,
73. 			additions: diffStats.added,
74. 			deletions: diffStats.removed,
75. 		};
76. 	});
77. });
78. 
79. const primaryDiff = $derived(editDiffs[0] ?? null);
80. const filePath = $derived(primaryDiff?.filePath ?? null);
81. 
82. // Streaming detection
83. const isStreaming = $derived(
84. 	toolStatus.isInputStreaming || (toolStatus.isPending && turnState === "streaming")
85. );
86. 
87. // Don't treat as streaming when waiting for permission approval
88. // (tool has all its data, just needs user consent)
89. const isDiffStreaming = $derived(pendingPermission ? false : isStreaming);
90. 
91. // Map tool status to AgentToolStatus
92. const agentStatus = $derived.by(() => {
93. 	if (toolStatus.isPending) return "running" as const;
94. 	if (toolStatus.isError) return "error" as const;
95. 	return "done" as const;
96. });
97. const isApplied = $derived(toolStatus.isSuccess);
98. const isAwaitingApproval = $derived(
99. 	Boolean(pendingPermission) && !isApplied && !toolStatus.isError
100. );
101. 
102. const isFileClickable = $derived(Boolean(projectPath));
103. 
104. function handleFileClick(selectedFilePath?: string | null) {
105. 	const targetFilePath = selectedFilePath ?? filePath;
106. 	if (targetFilePath && projectPath) {
107. 		panelStore.openFilePanel(
108. 			getRelativeFilePath(targetFilePath, projectPath) ?? targetFilePath,
109. 			projectPath,
110. 			{
111. 				ownerPanelId,
112. 			}
113. 		);
114. 	}
115. }
116. 
117. // Pierre diffs configuration
118. const effectiveTheme = $derived(themeState.effectiveTheme);
119. const workerPool = getWorkerPool();
120. const themeNames = { dark: "Cursor Dark", light: "pierre-light" };
121. </script>
122. 
123. <AgentToolEdit
124. 	diffs={editDiffs}

130. 	newString={primaryDiff?.newString ?? null}

133. 	applied={isApplied}
134. 	awaitingApproval={isAwaitingApproval}
135. 	durationLabel={elapsedLabel ?? undefined}

1. import type { PermissionRequest } from "../../types/permission.js";
2. import type { ToolCall } from "../../types/tool-call.js";
3. import type { ToolKind } from "../../types/tool-kind.js";
4. 
5. export type ToolRouteKey = ToolKind | "read_lints";
6. 
7. export interface ResolvedToolOperation {
8. 	resolvedKind: ToolKind;
9. 	routeKey: ToolRouteKey;
10. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
11. 	shouldShowInlinePermissionActionBar: boolean;
12. }
13. 
14. export function resolveToolOperation(
15. 	toolCall: ToolCall,
16. 	pendingPermission: PermissionRequest | null | undefined
17. ): ResolvedToolOperation {
18. 	const resolvedKind = toolCall.kind ?? "other";
19. 	const routeKey = resolveToolRouteKey(toolCall, resolvedKind);
20. 
21. 	return {
22. 		resolvedKind,
23. 		routeKey,
24. 		toolCall,
25. 		shouldShowInlinePermissionActionBar:
26. 			pendingPermission !== null &&
27. 			pendingPermission !== undefined &&
28. 			resolvedKind !== "exit_plan_mode",
29. 	};
30. }
31. 
32. export function resolveToolRouteKey(toolCall: ToolCall, resolvedKind: ToolKind): ToolRouteKey {
33. 	if (
34. 		resolvedKind === "read" &&
35. 		(toolCall.title?.trim() === "Read Lints" || toolCall.name === "read_lints")
36. 	) {
37. 		return "read_lints";
38. 	}
39. 
40. 	return resolvedKind;
41. }
42.
```

### 💬 Copilot

The UI already has a real delete route and delete presentation; the missing piece is that `apply_patch` delete-file patches still get classified as edit before they ever reach that route. I’m tracing where `ToolArguments::Delete` is being thrown away so I can preserve it instead.

14. /// Raw tool call update input used only for conversion to ToolCallUpdateData.
15. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
16. pub(crate) struct RawToolCallUpdateInput {
17.     pub id: String,
18.     pub status: Option<ToolCallStatus>,
19.     pub result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
20.     pub content: Option<serde_json::Value>,
21.     pub title: Option<String>,
22.     pub locations: Option<serde_json::Value>,
23.     pub streaming_input_delta: Option<String>,
24.     pub tool_name: Option<String>,
25.     pub raw_input: Option<serde_json::Value>,
26.     pub kind: Option<ToolKind>,
27. }
28. 
29. /// Raw tool call input used only for conversion to ToolCallData.
30. /// Parsers produce this; build_tool_call_from_raw turns it into ToolCallData.
31. #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
32. pub(crate) struct RawToolCallInput {
33.     pub id: String,
34.     pub name: String,
35.     pub arguments: serde_json::Value,
36.     pub status: ToolCallStatus,
37.     pub kind: Option<ToolKind>,
38.     pub title: Option<String>,
39.     pub suppress_title_read_path_hint: bool,
40.     pub parent_tool_use_id: Option<String>,
41.     pub task_children: Option<Vec<RawToolCallInput>>,
42. }
43. use crate::acp::types::ContentBlock;
44. 
45. #[cfg(test)]
46. pub(crate) fn parse_tool_call_from_acp<E>(data: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<ToolCallData, E>
47. where
48.     E: serde::de::Error,
49. {
50.     parse_tool_call_from_acp_with_agent(data, current_agent().unwrap_or(AgentType::ClaudeCode))
51. }
52. 
53. pub(crate) fn parse_tool_call_from_acp_with_agent<E>(
54.     data: &serde_json::Value,
55.     agent: AgentType,
56. ) -> Result<ToolCallData, E>
57. where
58.     E: serde::de::Error,
59. {
60.     tracing::debug!(
61.         raw_data = %data,
62.         "Parsing tool_call from ACP format"
63.     );
64. 
65.     let parser = get_parser(agent);
66.     let tool_call = parser
67.         .parse_tool_call(data)
68.         .map_err(parser_error_to_de_error::<E>)?;
69. 
70.     tracing::debug!(
71.         tool_call_id = %tool_call.id,
72.         tool_name = %tool_call.name,
73.         tool_kind = ?tool_call.kind,
74.         tool_status = ?tool_call.status,
75.         tool_title = ?tool_call.title,
76.         "Successfully parsed tool_call"
77.     );
78. 
79.     Ok(tool_call)
80. }
81. 
82. /// Parse tool call update from ACP format
83. ///
84. /// All agents go through their parser; streaming extraction is agent-specific.
85. /// ACP sends: { toolCallId, status, content: [{"type": "content", "content": {...}}], ... }
86. /// We need: ToolCallUpdateData { tool_call_id, status, content: Vec<ContentBlock>, ... }
87. #[cfg(test)]
88. pub(crate) fn parse_tool_call_update_from_acp<E>(
89.     data: &serde_json::Value,
90.     session_id: Option<&str>,
91. ) -> Result<ToolCallUpdateData, E>
92. where
93.     E: serde::de::Error,
94. {
95.     parse_tool_call_update_from_acp_with_agent(
96.         data,
97.         session_id,
98.         current_agent().unwrap_or(AgentType::ClaudeCode),
99.     )
100. }
101. 
102. pub(crate) fn parse_tool_call_update_from_acp_with_agent<E>(
103.     data: &serde_json::Value,
104.     session_id: Option<&str>,
105.     agent: AgentType,
106. ) -> Result<ToolCallUpdateData, E>
107. where
108.     E: serde::de::Error,
109. {
110.     let parser = get_parser(agent);
111.     parser
112.         .parse_tool_call_update(data, session_id)
113.         .map_err(parser_error_to_de_error::<E>)
114. }
115. 
116. /// Build ToolCallUpdateData from raw update, running streaming accumulator when parser
117. /// extracted streaming_input_delta and tool_name.
118. pub(crate) fn build_tool_call_update_from_raw(
119.     parser: &dyn AgentParser,
120.     raw: RawToolCallUpdateInput,
121.     session_id: Option<&str>,
122. ) -> ToolCallUpdateData {
123.     let agent = parser.agent_type();
124.     let session_key = session_id.unwrap_or(raw.id.as_str());
125.     let tool_call_id = &raw.id;
126.     let provided_tool_name = raw.tool_name.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
127.     let tool_name = if provided_tool_name.eq_ignore_ascii_case("unknown") {
128.         ""
129.     } else {
130.         provided_tool_name
131.     };
132.     let status = raw.status;
133.     let is_terminal = status
134.         .as_ref()
135.         .is_some_and(|s| matches!(s, ToolCallStatus::Completed | ToolCallStatus::Failed));
136. 
137.     let (
138.         streaming_input_delta,
139.         normalized_todos,
140.         normalized_questions,
141.         streaming_arguments,
142.         final_streaming_plan,
143.     ) = if let Some(ref delta) = raw.streaming_input_delta {
144.         use crate::acp::streaming_accumulator::{
145.             finalize_plan_streaming_for_tool, get_session_streaming_state_mut,
146.             process_plan_streaming,
147.         };
148. 
149.         let session_state = get_session_streaming_state_mut(session_key);
150.         let normalized = session_state.accumulate_delta(tool_call_id, tool_name, delta, agent);
151.         let (todos, questions, streaming_args) = normalized
152.             .as_ref()
153.             .map(|n| {
154.                 (
155.                     n.todos.clone(),
156.                     n.questions.clone(),
157.                     n.streaming_arguments.clone(),
158.                 )
159.             })
160.             .unwrap_or((None, None, None));
161. 
162.         let effective_tool_name = normalized
163.             .as_ref()
164.             .and_then(|n| n.effective_tool_name.as_deref())
165.             .unwrap_or(tool_name);
166. 
167.         // Use the effective tool name (seeded from initial tool_call when omitted in deltas)
168.         // so plan detection still works for .claude/plans writes.
169.         let plan =
170.             process_plan_streaming(session_key, tool_call_id, effective_tool_name, delta, agent);
171. 
172.         // Drop the DashMap RefMut before cleanup_tool_streaming, which calls
173.         // SESSION_STREAMING_STATES.get() on the same key. Holding the write lock
174.         // across that call causes a deadlock on the same shard.
175.         drop(session_state);
176. 
177.         let final_plan = if is_terminal {
178.             crate::acp::streaming_accumulator::cleanup_tool_streaming(session_key, tool_call_id);
179.             finalize_plan_streaming_for_tool(session_key, tool_call_id).or(plan)
180.         } else {
181.             plan
182.         };
183. 
184.         (
185.             Some(delta.clone()),
186.             todos,
187.             questions,
188.             streaming_args,
189.             final_plan,
190.         )
191.     } else {
192.         // Fallback: detect plan writes from rawInput when streamingInputDelta is absent.
193.         // Claude Code sends rawInput with {file_path, content} on tool_call_update.
194.         let plan = raw
195.             .raw_input
196.             .as_ref()
197.             .and_then(|r| parser.extract_plan_from_raw_input(tool_name, r));
198. 
199.         if is_terminal {
200.             crate::acp::streaming_accumulator::cleanup_tool_streaming(session_key, tool_call_id);
201.         }
202.         (None, None, None, None, plan)
203.     };
204. 
205.     let locations = raw
206.         .locations
207.         .and_then(|value| serde_json::from_value::<Vec<ToolCallLocation>>(value).ok());
208. 
209.     let content = raw
210.         .content
211.         .as_ref()
212.         .or(raw.result.as_ref())
213.         .and_then(extract_content_blocks_from_value);
214. 
215.     let identity_hints = ToolClassificationHints {
216.         name: raw.tool_name.as_deref(),
217.         title: raw.title.as_deref(),
218.         kind: raw.kind,
219.         kind_hint: raw.kind.as_ref().map(ToolKind::as_str),
220.         locations: locations.as_deref(),
221.     };
222.     let classified_arguments = raw
223.         .raw_input
224.         .as_ref()
225.         .map(|input| classify_raw_tool_call(parser, tool_call_id, input, identity_hints));
226.     let arguments = classified_arguments
227.         .as_ref()
228.         .map(|classified| classified.arguments.clone());
229.     let result = normalize_count_only_search_result(raw.result, arguments.as_ref());
230. 
231.     let resolved_kind = classified_arguments
232.         .as_ref()
233.         .map(|classified| classified.kind)
234.         .or_else(|| {
235.             Some(
236.                 resolve_raw_tool_identity(
237.                     parser,
238.                     tool_call_id,
239.                     raw.raw_input.as_ref(),
240.                     identity_hints,
241.                 )
242.                 .kind,
243.             )
244.         });
245. 
246.     let result = normalize_web_search_result(result, resolved_kind.as_ref());
247. 
248.     ToolCallUpdateData {
249.         tool_call_id: raw.id,
250.         status,
251.         result,
252.         content,
253.         raw_output: None,
254.         title: raw.title,
255.         locations,
256.         streaming_input_delta,
257.         normalized_todos,
258.         normalized_questions,
259.         streaming_arguments,
260.         streaming_plan: final_streaming_plan,
```

286.                     raw_arguments,
287.                     &["subagent_type", "subagentType", "agent_type", "agentType"],
288.                 ),
289.                 skill,
290.                 skill_args,
291.                 raw: Some(raw_arguments.clone()),
292.             }
293.         }
294.         ToolKind::Move => {
295.             let parsed_cmd_move = parse_parsed_cmd_move(raw_arguments);
296.             ToolArguments::Move {
297.                 from: extract_parser_string(raw_arguments, &["from", "source"])
298.                     .or_else(|| parsed_cmd_move.as_ref().and_then(|(from, _)| from.clone())),
299.                 to: extract_parser_string(raw_arguments, &["to", "destination"])
300.                     .or_else(|| parsed_cmd_move.as_ref().and_then(|(_, to)| to.clone())),
301.             }
302.         }
303.         ToolKind::Delete => ToolArguments::Delete {
304.             file_path: extract_parser_string(raw_arguments, &["file_path", "filePath", "path"])
305.                 .or_else(|| parse_parsed_cmd_path(raw_arguments, &["delete", "remove", "rm"])),
306.         },
307.         ToolKind::EnterPlanMode | ToolKind::ExitPlanMode | ToolKind::CreatePlan => {
308.             ToolArguments::PlanMode {
309.                 mode: extract_parser_string(raw_arguments, &["mode", "modeId"]),
310.             }
311.         }
312.         ToolKind::ToolSearch => ToolArguments::ToolSearch {
313.             query: extract_parser_string(raw_arguments, &["query"]),
314.             max_results: raw_arguments.get("max_results").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),
315.         },
316.         ToolKind::Other => ToolArguments::Other {
317.             raw: raw_arguments.clone(),
318.         },
319.     }
320. }
321.
```

</details>

212. impl ToolArguments {
213.     /// Construct typed arguments from a raw JSON value and a known tool kind.
214.     pub fn from_raw(kind: ToolKind, raw: serde_json::Value) -> Self {
215.         use crate::acp::parsers::arguments::parse_tool_kind_arguments;
216.         parse_tool_kind_arguments(kind, &raw)
217.     }
218. 
219.     /// Return canonical tool kind represented by this typed argument payload.
220.     pub fn tool_kind(&self) -> ToolKind {
221.         match self {
222.             ToolArguments::Read { .. } => ToolKind::Read,
223.             ToolArguments::Edit { .. } => ToolKind::Edit,
224.             ToolArguments::Execute { .. } => ToolKind::Execute,
225.             ToolArguments::Search { .. } => ToolKind::Search,
226.             ToolArguments::Glob { .. } => ToolKind::Glob,
227.             ToolArguments::Fetch { .. } => ToolKind::Fetch,
228.             ToolArguments::WebSearch { .. } => ToolKind::WebSearch,
229.             ToolArguments::Think { .. } => ToolKind::Think,
230.             ToolArguments::TaskOutput { .. } => ToolKind::TaskOutput,
231.             ToolArguments::Move { .. } => ToolKind::Move,
232.             ToolArguments::Delete { .. } => ToolKind::Delete,
233.             ToolArguments::PlanMode { .. } => ToolKind::Other,
234.             ToolArguments::ToolSearch { .. } => ToolKind::ToolSearch,
235.             ToolArguments::Other { .. } => ToolKind::Other,
236.         }
237.     }
238. }
239.

265. 
266. fn normalize_count_only_search_result(
267.     result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
268.     _arguments: Option<&ToolArguments>,
269. ) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
270.     let Some(serde_json::Value::Object(mut object)) = result else {
271.         return result;
272.     };
273.     if object.contains_key("mode")
274.         || object.contains_key("content")
275.         || object.contains_key("filenames")
276.     {
277.         return Some(serde_json::Value::Object(object));
278.     }
279. 
280.     let total_files = object.get("totalFiles").and_then(|value| value.as_u64());
281.     let total_matches = object.get("totalMatches").and_then(|value| value.as_u64());
282.     if total_files.is_none() && total_matches.is_none() {
283.         return Some(serde_json::Value::Object(object));
284.     }
285. 
286.     object.insert(
287.         "mode".to_string(),
288.         serde_json::Value::String("count".to_string()),
289.     );
290.     if let Some(value) = total_files {
291.         object.insert(
292.             "numFiles".to_string(),
293.             serde_json::Value::Number(serde_json::Number::from(value)),
294.         );
295.     }
296.     if let Some(value) = total_matches {
297.         object.insert(
298.             "numMatches".to_string(),
299.             serde_json::Value::Number(serde_json::Number::from(value)),
300.         );
301.     }
302. 
303.     Some(serde_json::Value::Object(object))
304. }
305. 
306. // Web search results from Claude Code arrive as an array of {type, url, title,
307. // encrypted_content, page_age} objects. Reshape into {results: [...], summary: ""}
308. // so the frontend's parseWebSearchResult can render link cards directly.
309. fn normalize_web_search_result(
310.     result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
311.     kind: Option<&ToolKind>,
312. ) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
313.     if kind != Some(&ToolKind::WebSearch) {
314.         return result;
315.     }
316.     let Some(serde_json::Value::Array(items)) = result else {
317.         return result;
318.     };
319. 
320.     let results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = items
321.         .iter()
322.         .filter_map(|item| {
323.             let url = item.get("url").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;
324.             if !(url.starts_with("http://") || url.starts_with("https://")) {
325.                 return None;
326.             }
327.             let title = item.get("title").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(url);
328.             let page_age = item.get("page_age").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
329.             Some(json!({
330.                 "title": title,
331.                 "url": url,
332.                 "page_age": page_age,
333.             }))
334.         })
335.         .collect();
336. 
337.     if results.is_empty() {
338.         return Some(serde_json::Value::Array(items));
339.     }
340. 
341.     Some(json!({
342.         "results": results,
343.         "summary": "",
344.     }))
345. }
346. 
347. pub(crate) fn build_tool_call_from_raw(
348.     parser: &dyn AgentParser,
349.     raw: RawToolCallInput,
350. ) -> ToolCallData {
351.     let classified = classify_raw_tool_call(
352.         parser,
353.         &raw.id,
354.         &raw.arguments,
355.         ToolClassificationHints {
356.             name: Some(&raw.name),
357.             title: raw.title.as_deref(),
358.             kind: raw.kind,
359.             kind_hint: raw.kind.as_ref().map(ToolKind::as_str),
360.             locations: None,
361.         },
362.     );
363.     let mut arguments = classified.arguments;
364.     if let ToolArguments::Read { ref mut file_path } = arguments {
365.         if file_path.is_none() && !raw.suppress_title_read_path_hint {
366.             *file_path = raw.title.as_deref().and_then(extract_file_path_from_title);
367.         }
368.     }
369.     if let ToolArguments::Execute { ref mut command } = arguments {
370.         if command.is_none() {
371.             *command = raw.title.as_deref().and_then(extract_backtick_command);
372.         }
373.     }
374.     let status = raw.status;
375. 
376.     let (normalized_questions, normalized_todos) = derive_normalized_questions_and_todos(
377.         &classified.name,
378.         &raw.arguments,
379.         parser.agent_type(),
380.     );
```

408. /// Extract a file path from a tool-call title like "View Image /path/to/file.png" or "Read /path".
409. /// Returns None for "Read Lints" so that tool is not treated as reading a file named "Lints".
410. fn extract_file_path_from_title(title: &str) -> Option<String> {
411.     if title.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("Read Lints") {
412.         return None;
413.     }
414.     let prefixes = ["View Image ", "Read ", "View "];
415.     for prefix in prefixes {
416.         if let Some(path) = title.strip_prefix(prefix) {
417.             let trimmed = path.trim();
418.             if !trimmed.is_empty() && !is_generic_file_placeholder(trimmed) {
419.                 return Some(trimmed.to_string());
420.             }
421.         }
422.     }
423.     // Fallback: if title looks like an absolute path
424.     let trimmed = title.trim();
425.     if trimmed.starts_with('/') {
426.         return Some(trimmed.to_string());
427.     }
428.     None
429. }
430. 
431. fn is_generic_file_placeholder(path: &str) -> bool {
432.     path.trim_matches('`').eq_ignore_ascii_case("file")
433. }
434. 
435. /// Extract a command from a backtick-wrapped title like "`cd /path && cargo test 2>&1`".
436. fn extract_backtick_command(title: &str) -> Option<String> {
437.     let trimmed = title.trim();
438.     if trimmed.len() > 2 && trimmed.starts_with('`') && trimmed.ends_with('`') {
439.         Some(trimmed[1..trimmed.len() - 1].to_string())
440.     } else {
441.         None
442.     }
443. }
444. 
445. fn extract_content_blocks_from_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<Vec<ContentBlock>> {
446.     let array = value.as_array()?;
447.     let content = array
448.         .iter()
449.         .filter_map(|item| {
450.             if let Some(inner) = item.get("content") {
451.                 serde_json::from_value::<ContentBlock>(inner.clone()).ok()
452.             } else {
453.                 serde_json::from_value::<ContentBlock>(item.clone()).ok()
454.             }
455.         })
456.         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
457. 
458.     if content.is_empty() {
459.         None
460.     } else {
461.         Some(content)
462.     }
463. }
464. 
465. pub fn tool_call_status_from_str(status_str: &str) -> ToolCallStatus {
466.     let s = status_str.trim();
467.     if s.eq_ignore_ascii_case("pending") {
468.         ToolCallStatus::Pending
469.     } else if s.eq_ignore_ascii_case("in_progress")
470.         || s.eq_ignore_ascii_case("inprogress")
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 161m 1s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the builder: it trusts `classified.kind` wholesale. So the real bug is one level deeper in `classify_raw_tool_call(...)` — it’s parsing delete arguments correctly but still returning `kind = Edit`. That’s the seam I’m fixing.

---

1. use crate::acp::parsers::kind::{
2.     canonical_name_for_kind, infer_kind_from_payload, is_web_search_id, is_web_search_title,
3.     looks_like_web_search_arguments,

8. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
9. pub(crate) struct ToolClassificationHints<'a> {
10.     pub name: Option<&'a str>,
11.     pub title: Option<&'a str>,
12.     pub kind: Option<ToolKind>,
13.     pub kind_hint: Option<&'a str>,

30. pub(crate) fn is_unknown_tool_name(name: &str) -> bool {
31.     let trimmed = name.trim();
32.     trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.eq_ignore_ascii_case("unknown")
33. }
34. 
35. fn canonical_tool_call_name_for_kind(kind: ToolKind) -> &'static str {
36.     match kind {
37.         ToolKind::WebSearch => "WebSearch",
38.         _ => canonical_name_for_kind(kind),
39.     }
40. }
41. 
42. fn usable_tool_name(name: Option<&str>) -> Option<&str> {
43.     name.map(str::trim)
44.         .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
45.         .filter(|value| !is_unknown_tool_name(value))
46. }
47. 
48. fn infer_kind_from_serialized_arguments(arguments: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<ToolKind> {
49.     let object = arguments.as_object()?;
50. 
51.     if object.contains_key("questions") {
52.         return Some(ToolKind::Question);
53.     }
54. 
55.     if object.contains_key("todos") {
56.         return Some(ToolKind::Todo);
57.     }
58. 
59.     if object.contains_key("task_id") || object.contains_key("taskId") {
60.         return Some(ToolKind::TaskOutput);
61.     }
62. 
63.     if object.contains_key("skill")
64.         || object.contains_key("skill_name")
65.         || object.contains_key("skillName")
66.         || object.contains_key("skill_args")
67.         || object.contains_key("skillArgs")
68.     {
69.         return Some(ToolKind::Skill);
70.     }
71. 
72.     if object.contains_key("description")
73.         || object.contains_key("prompt")
74.         || object.contains_key("agent_type")
75.         || object.contains_key("agentType")
76.         || object.contains_key("subagent_type")
77.         || object.contains_key("subagentType")
78.     {
79.         return Some(ToolKind::Task);
80.     }
81. 
82.     if object.contains_key("from")
83.         || object.contains_key("to")
84.         || object.contains_key("source")
85.         || object.contains_key("destination")
86.     {
87.         return Some(ToolKind::Move);
88.     }
89. 
90.     let has_edit_markers = object.contains_key("old_string")
91.         || object.contains_key("oldString")
92.         || object.contains_key("oldText")
93.         || object.contains_key("new_string")
94.         || object.contains_key("newString")
95.         || object.contains_key("newText")
96.         || (object.contains_key("content")
97.             && (object.contains_key("file_path")
98.                 || object.contains_key("filePath")
99.                 || object.contains_key("path")));
100.     if has_edit_markers {
101.         return Some(ToolKind::Edit);
102.     }
103. 
104.     if object.contains_key("query") || object.contains_key("pattern") {
105.         return Some(ToolKind::Search);
106.     }
107. 
108.     if object.contains_key("command") || object.contains_key("cmd") {
109.         return Some(ToolKind::Execute);
110.     }
111. 
112.     if object.contains_key("url") {
113.         return Some(ToolKind::Fetch);
114.     }
115. 
116.     if object.contains_key("file_path")
117.         || object.contains_key("filePath")
118.         || object.contains_key("path")
119.         || object.contains_key("uri")
120.     {
121.         return Some(ToolKind::Read);
122.     }
123. 
124.     None
125. }
126. 
127. fn apply_web_search_promotion(
128.     kind: ToolKind,
129.     id: &str,
130.     title: Option<&str>,
131.     raw_arguments: Option<&serde_json::Value>,
132. ) -> ToolKind {
133.     let argument_implied_web_search = matches!(kind, ToolKind::Fetch | ToolKind::Other)
134.         && raw_arguments
135.             .map(looks_like_web_search_arguments)
136.             .unwrap_or(false);
137.     if matches!(kind, ToolKind::Fetch | ToolKind::Search | ToolKind::Other)
138.         && (is_web_search_id(id)
139.             || title.map(is_web_search_title).unwrap_or(false)
140.             || argument_implied_web_search)
141.     {
142.         ToolKind::WebSearch
143.     } else {
144.         kind
145.     }
146. }
147. 
148. fn resolve_identity_impl(
149.     parser: &dyn AgentParser,
150.     id: &str,
151.     raw_arguments: Option<&serde_json::Value>,
152.     hints: ToolClassificationHints<'_>,
153.     serialized: bool,
154. ) -> ToolIdentity {
155.     let explicit_name = usable_tool_name(hints.name);
156. 
157.     let detected_kind = explicit_name
158.         .map(|name| parser.detect_tool_kind(name))
159.         .filter(|kind| *kind != ToolKind::Other);
160. 
161.     let hint_kind = hints.kind.filter(|kind| *kind != ToolKind::Other);
162.     let payload_kind = infer_kind_from_payload(id, hints.title, hints.kind_hint)
163.         .filter(|kind| *kind != ToolKind::Other);
164. 
165.     let serialized_kind = if serialized {
166.         raw_arguments
167.             .and_then(infer_kind_from_serialized_arguments)
168.             .filter(|kind| *kind != ToolKind::Other)
169.     } else {
170.         None
171.     };
172. 
173.     let location_kind = if serialized && hints.locations.is_some_and(|entries| !entries.is_empty())
174.     {
175.         Some(ToolKind::Read)
176.     } else {
177.         None
178.     };
179. 
180.     let title_read_kind = if serialized {
181.         hints.title.and_then(|title_value| {
182.             let lower = title_value.trim().to_ascii_lowercase();
183.             if lower.starts_with("viewing ")
184.                 || lower.starts_with("view ")
185.                 || lower.starts_with("read ")
186.             {
187.                 Some(ToolKind::Read)
188.             } else {
189.                 None
190.             }
191.         })
192.     } else {
193.         None
194.     };
195. 
196.     let kind = detected_kind
197.         .or(hint_kind)
198.         .or(payload_kind)
199.         .or(serialized_kind)
200.         .or(location_kind)
201.         .or(title_read_kind)
202.         .unwrap_or(ToolKind::Other);
203.     let kind = apply_web_search_promotion(kind, id, hints.title, raw_arguments);
204. 
205.     let name = if let Some(name) = explicit_name {
206.         name.to_string()
207.     } else if kind != ToolKind::Other {
208.         canonical_tool_call_name_for_kind(kind).to_string()
209.     } else {
210.         hints
211.             .name
212.             .map(str::trim)
213.             .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
214.             .map(str::to_string)
215.             .unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".to_string())
216.     };
217. 
218.     ToolIdentity { name, kind }
219. }
220. 
221. fn parse_arguments_once(
222.     parser: &dyn AgentParser,
223.     raw_arguments: &serde_json::Value,
224.     identity: &ToolIdentity,
225.     title: Option<&str>,
226.     kind_hint: Option<&str>,
227. ) -> ToolArguments {
228.     let parse_name = if is_unknown_tool_name(&identity.name) {
229.         title
230.     } else {
231.         Some(identity.name.as_str())
232.     };
233.     let parse_kind_hint = if identity.kind == ToolKind::Other {
234.         kind_hint
235.     } else {
236.         Some(identity.kind.as_str())
237.     };
238. 
239.     parser
240.         .parse_typed_tool_arguments(parse_name, raw_arguments, parse_kind_hint)
241.         .unwrap_or_else(|| {
242.             if identity.kind == ToolKind::Other {
243.                 ToolArguments::Other {
244.                     raw: raw_arguments.clone(),
245.                 }
246.             } else {
247.                 ToolArguments::from_raw(identity.kind, raw_arguments.clone())
248.             }
249.         })
250. }
251. 
252. fn promote_kind(base_kind: ToolKind, argument_kind: ToolKind) -> ToolKind {
253.     if argument_kind == ToolKind::Other {
254.         return base_kind;
255.     }
256. 
257.     match base_kind {
258.         ToolKind::Other => argument_kind,
259.         ToolKind::Edit if argument_kind != ToolKind::Edit => argument_kind,
260.         ToolKind::Search if argument_kind != ToolKind::Search => argument_kind,
```

</details>

260.         ToolKind::Search if argument_kind != ToolKind::Search => argument_kind,

266. pub(crate) fn resolve_raw_tool_identity(
267.     parser: &dyn AgentParser,
268.     id: &str,
269.     raw_arguments: Option<&serde_json::Value>,
270.     hints: ToolClassificationHints<'_>,
271. ) -> ToolIdentity {
272.     resolve_identity_impl(parser, id, raw_arguments, hints, false)
273. }
274. 
275. pub(crate) fn classify_raw_tool_call(
276.     parser: &dyn AgentParser,
277.     id: &str,
278.     raw_arguments: &serde_json::Value,
279.     hints: ToolClassificationHints<'_>,
280. ) -> ClassifiedToolData {
281.     let identity = resolve_raw_tool_identity(parser, id, Some(raw_arguments), hints);
282.     let arguments = parse_arguments_once(
283.         parser,
284.         raw_arguments,
285.         &identity,
286.         hints.title,
287.         hints.kind_hint,
288.     );
289.     let kind = promote_kind(identity.kind, arguments.tool_kind());
290.     let name = if is_unknown_tool_name(&identity.name) && kind != ToolKind::Other {
291.         canonical_tool_call_name_for_kind(kind).to_string()
292.     } else {
293.         identity.name
294.     };
295. 
296.     ClassifiedToolData {
297.         name,
298.         kind,
299.         arguments,
300.     }
301. }
302. 
303. pub(crate) fn classify_serialized_tool_call(
304.     agent: AgentType,
305.     id: &str,
306.     raw_arguments: &serde_json::Value,
307.     hints: ToolClassificationHints<'_>,
308. ) -> ClassifiedToolData {
309.     let parser = get_parser(agent);
310.     let identity = resolve_identity_impl(parser, id, Some(raw_arguments), hints, true);
311.     let arguments = parse_arguments_once(
312.         parser,
313.         raw_arguments,
314.         &identity,
315.         hints.title,
316.         hints.kind_hint,
317.     );
318.     let kind = promote_kind(identity.kind, arguments.tool_kind());
319.     let name = if is_unknown_tool_name(&identity.name) && kind != ToolKind::Other {
320.         canonical_tool_call_name_for_kind(kind).to_string()
321.     } else {
322.         identity.name
323.     };
324. 
325.     ClassifiedToolData {
326.         name,
327.         kind,
328.         arguments,
329.     }
330. }
331. 
332. #[cfg(test)]
333. mod tests {
334.     use super::*;
335.     use crate::acp::parsers::{AgentType, CopilotParser};
336. 
337.     #[test]
338.     fn raw_classification_promotes_unknown_rg_title_to_search() {
339.         let parser = CopilotParser;
340.         let classified = classify_raw_tool_call(
341.             &parser,
342.             "tool-rg",
343.             &serde_json::json!({ "query": "tool_call", "path": "/tmp" }),
344.             ToolClassificationHints {
345.                 name: Some("unknown"),
346.                 title: Some("rg"),
347.                 kind: Some(ToolKind::Other),
348.                 kind_hint: Some("other"),
349.                 locations: None,
350.             },
351.         );
352. 
353.         assert_eq!(classified.kind, ToolKind::Search);
354.         assert_eq!(classified.name, "Search");
355.     }
356. 
357.     #[test]
358.     fn serialized_classification_uses_locations_as_read_fallback() {
359.         let classified = classify_serialized_tool_call(
360.             AgentType::Copilot,
361.             "tool-read",
362.             &serde_json::json!({ "raw": {} }),
363.             ToolClassificationHints {
364.                 name: Some("unknown"),
365.                 title: Some("Viewing /tmp/file.rs"),
366.                 kind: None,
367.                 kind_hint: None,
368.                 locations: Some(&[ToolCallLocation {
369.                     path: "/tmp/file.rs".to_string(),
370.                 }]),
371.             },
372.         );
373. 
374.         assert_eq!(classified.kind, ToolKind::Read);
375.         assert_eq!(classified.name, "Read");
376.     }
377. }
378.

<sub>⏱️ 161m 11s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

The promotion logic is already correct. So the bug is earlier still: the patch-text normalizer is parsing `*** Delete File` into an **edit payload**, not a **delete payload**. I’m drilling into that patch parser now.

1. use crate::acp::session_update::{EditEntry, ToolArguments};
2. 
3. #[derive(Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq)]
4. enum PatchSectionKind {
5.     Update,
6.     Add,
7.     Delete,
8. }
9. 
10. struct PatchSection {
11.     kind: PatchSectionKind,
12.     file_path: String,
13.     move_to: Option<String>,
14.     diff_lines: Vec<String>,
15. }
16. 
17. /// Parse shared `patch_text`/`patchText` edit payloads into one `EditEntry` per
18. /// modified file.
19. pub(crate) fn parse_patch_text(raw_arguments: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<ToolArguments> {
20.     let patch_text = raw_arguments
21.         .get("patch_text")
22.         .or_else(|| raw_arguments.get("patchText"))
23.         .and_then(|value| value.as_str())
24.         .filter(|value| !value.trim().is_empty())?;
25. 
26.     parse_patch_text_value(patch_text)
27. }
28. 
29. pub(crate) fn parse_patch_text_value(patch_text: &str) -> Option<ToolArguments> {
30.     let sections = parse_patch_sections(patch_text);
31. 
32.     if sections.is_empty() {
33.         return None;
34.     }
35. 
36.     if sections.len() == 1 {
37.         if let Some(move_arguments) = parse_move_section(&sections[0]) {
38.             return Some(move_arguments);
39.         }
40.     }
41. 
42.     let edits = parse_patch_text_sections_to_edits(sections);
43. 
44.     if edits.is_empty() {
45.         return None;
46.     }
47. 
48.     Some(ToolArguments::Edit { edits })
49. }
50. 
51. fn parse_patch_sections(patch_text: &str) -> Vec<PatchSection> {
52.     let mut sections: Vec<PatchSection> = Vec::new();
53.     let mut current_section: Option<PatchSection> = None;
54. 
55.     for line in patch_text.lines() {
56.         if let Some(section_kind) = parse_section_header(line) {
57.             if let Some(section) = current_section.take() {
58.                 sections.push(section);
59.             }
60. 
61.             let file_path = line[section_header_prefix(section_kind).len()..].trim();
62.             if file_path.is_empty() {
63.                 continue;
64.             }
65. 
66.             current_section = Some(PatchSection {
67.                 kind: section_kind,
68.                 file_path: file_path.to_string(),
69.                 move_to: None,
70.                 diff_lines: Vec::new(),
71.             });
72.             continue;
73.         }
74. 
75.         if line == "*** End Patch" {
76.             break;
77.         }
78. 
79.         if let Some(destination_path) = line.strip_prefix("*** Move to: ") {
80.             if let Some(section) = current_section.as_mut() {
81.                 let trimmed_destination = destination_path.trim();
82.                 if !trimmed_destination.is_empty() {
83.                     section.move_to = Some(trimmed_destination.to_string());
84.                 }
85.             }
86.             continue;
87.         }
88. 
89.         if let Some(section) = current_section.as_mut() {
90.             section.diff_lines.push(line.to_string());
91.         }
92.     }
93. 
94.     if let Some(section) = current_section {
95.         sections.push(section);
96.     }
97. 
98.     sections
99. }
100. 
101. fn parse_move_section(section: &PatchSection) -> Option<ToolArguments> {
102.     if !is_pure_move_section(section) {
103.         return None;
104.     }
105. 
106.     let destination_path = section.move_to.as_ref()?;
107. 
108.     Some(ToolArguments::Move {
109.         from: Some(section.file_path.clone()),
110.         to: Some(destination_path.clone()),
111.     })
112. }
113. 
114. fn parse_move_edit_entry(section: &PatchSection) -> Option<EditEntry> {
115.     if !is_pure_move_section(section) {
116.         return None;
117.     }
118. 
119.     let destination_path = section.move_to.as_ref()?;
120.     Some(EditEntry {
121.         file_path: Some(destination_path.clone()),
122.         move_from: Some(section.file_path.clone()),
123.         old_string: None,
124.         new_string: None,
125.         content: None,
126.     })
127. }
128. 
129. fn is_pure_move_section(section: &PatchSection) -> bool {
130.     section.kind == PatchSectionKind::Update
131.         && section.move_to.is_some()
132.         && !section_has_diff_content(section)
133. }
134. 
135. fn section_has_diff_content(section: &PatchSection) -> bool {
136.     section.diff_lines.iter().any(|line| {
137.         line.starts_with('+')
138.             || line.starts_with('-')
139.             || line.starts_with(' ')
140.             || parse_inline_hunk_context(line).is_some()
141.             || (!line.is_empty() && !line.starts_with("@@"))
142.     })
143. }
144. 
145. fn parse_patch_text_sections_to_edits(sections: Vec<PatchSection>) -> Vec<EditEntry> {
146.     sections.into_iter().map(parse_patch_section).collect()
147. }
148. 
149. fn parse_section_header(line: &str) -> Option<PatchSectionKind> {
150.     if line.starts_with(section_header_prefix(PatchSectionKind::Update)) {
151.         return Some(PatchSectionKind::Update);
152.     }
153.     if line.starts_with(section_header_prefix(PatchSectionKind::Add)) {
154.         return Some(PatchSectionKind::Add);
155.     }
156.     if line.starts_with(section_header_prefix(PatchSectionKind::Delete)) {
157.         return Some(PatchSectionKind::Delete);
158.     }
159.     None
160. }
161. 
162. fn section_header_prefix(section_kind: PatchSectionKind) -> &'static str {
163.     match section_kind {
164.         PatchSectionKind::Update => "*** Update File: ",
165.         PatchSectionKind::Add => "*** Add File: ",
166.         PatchSectionKind::Delete => "*** Delete File: ",
167.     }
168. }
169. 
170. fn parse_patch_section(section: PatchSection) -> EditEntry {
171.     if let Some(move_entry) = parse_move_edit_entry(&section) {
172.         return move_entry;
173.     }
174. 
175.     let rendered_path = section.move_to.as_deref().unwrap_or(&section.file_path);
176.     let diff_lines = section.diff_lines.join("\n");
177. 
178.     if section.kind == PatchSectionKind::Delete {
179.         let mut deleted_entry = parse_file_diff_section(rendered_path, &diff_lines);
180.         deleted_entry.new_string = None;
181.         deleted_entry.content = None;
182.         return deleted_entry;
183.     }
184. 
185.     parse_file_diff_section(rendered_path, &diff_lines)
186. }
187. 
188. fn parse_file_diff_section(file_path: &str, diff_lines: &str) -> EditEntry {
189.     let mut old_lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();
190.     let mut new_lines: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();
191. 
192.     for line in diff_lines.lines() {
193.         if let Some(context) = parse_inline_hunk_context(line) {
194.             old_lines.push(context);
195.             new_lines.push(context);
196.             continue;
197.         }
198.         if line.starts_with("@@") {
199.             continue;
200.         }
201.         if let Some(stripped) = line.strip_prefix('-') {
202.             old_lines.push(stripped);
203.         } else if let Some(stripped) = line.strip_prefix('+') {
204.             new_lines.push(stripped);
205.         } else if let Some(context) = line.strip_prefix(' ') {
206.             old_lines.push(context);
207.             new_lines.push(context);
208.         }
209.     }
210. 
211.     let old_string = if old_lines.is_empty() {
212.         None
213.     } else {
214.         Some(old_lines.join("\n"))
215.     };
216.     let new_string = if new_lines.is_empty() {
217.         None
218.     } else {
219.         Some(new_lines.join("\n"))
220.     };
221. 
222.     EditEntry {
223.         file_path: Some(file_path.to_string()),
224.         move_from: None,
225.         old_string,
226.         new_string: new_string.clone(),
227.         content: new_string,
228.     }
229. }
230. 
231. fn parse_inline_hunk_context(line: &str) -> Option<&str> {
232.     let context = line.strip_prefix("@@ ")?;
233.     if context.trim().is_empty() || context.trim_end().ends_with("@@") {
234.         return None;
235.     }
236.     Some(context)
237. }
238.

258.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].file_path.as_deref(), Some("src/foo.ts"));
259.                 assert_eq!(
260.                     edits[0].old_string.as_deref(),
```

272.     #[test]
273.     fn parse_patch_text_multi_file() {
274.         let patch = r#"*** Begin Patch
275. *** Update File: src/a.ts
276. @@ export const A = 1;
277. -export const A = 1;
278. +export const A = 2;
279. *** Update File: src/b.ts
280. @@ export const B = "hello";
281. -export const B = "hello";
282. +export const B = "world";
283. *** End Patch"#;
284. 
285.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "patch_text": patch });
286.         let result = parse_patch_text(&raw).expect("should parse patch_text");
287. 
288.         match result {
289.             ToolArguments::Edit { edits } => {
290.                 assert_eq!(edits.len(), 2);
291.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].file_path.as_deref(), Some("src/a.ts"));
292.                 assert_eq!(edits[1].file_path.as_deref(), Some("src/b.ts"));
293.                 assert!(edits[0]
294.                     .old_string
295.                     .as_deref()
296.                     .unwrap_or("")
297.                     .contains("A = 1"));
298.                 assert!(edits[0]
299.                     .new_string
300.                     .as_deref()
301.                     .unwrap_or("")
302.                     .contains("A = 2"));
303.                 assert!(edits[1]
304.                     .old_string
305.                     .as_deref()
306.                     .unwrap_or("")
307.                     .contains("B = \"hello\""));
308.                 assert!(edits[1]
309.                     .new_string
310.                     .as_deref()
311.                     .unwrap_or("")
312.                     .contains("B = \"world\""));
313.             }
314.             other => panic!("Expected Edit, got {other:?}"),
315.         }
316.     }
317. 
318.     #[test]
319.     fn parse_patch_text_returns_none_when_key_absent() {
320.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "file_path": "foo.ts", "new_string": "x" });
321.         assert!(parse_patch_text(&raw).is_none());
322.     }
323. 
324.     #[test]
325.     fn parse_patch_text_returns_none_when_no_file_markers() {
326.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "patch_text": "*** Begin Patch\n*** End Patch\n" });
327.         assert!(parse_patch_text(&raw).is_none());
328.     }
329. 
330.     #[test]
331.     fn parse_patch_text_accepts_camel_case_key() {
332.         let patch = r#"*** Begin Patch
333. *** Add File: src/foo.ts
334. +export const value = 1;
335. *** End Patch"#;
336. 
337.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "patchText": patch });
338.         let result = parse_patch_text(&raw).expect("should parse patchText");
339. 
340.         match result {
341.             ToolArguments::Edit { edits } => {
342.                 assert_eq!(edits.len(), 1);
343.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].file_path.as_deref(), Some("src/foo.ts"));
344.             }
345.             other => panic!("Expected Edit, got {other:?}"),
346.         }
347.     }
348. 
349.     #[test]
350.     fn parse_patch_text_supports_delete_file_sections() {
351.         let patch = r#"*** Begin Patch
352. *** Delete File: src/old.ts
353.  export const value = 1;
354. *** End Patch"#;
355. 
356.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "patch_text": patch });
357.         let result = parse_patch_text(&raw).expect("should parse delete patch_text");
358. 
359.         match result {
360.             ToolArguments::Edit { edits } => {
361.                 assert_eq!(edits.len(), 1);
362.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].file_path.as_deref(), Some("src/old.ts"));
363.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].new_string, None);
364.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].content, None);
365.                 assert_eq!(
366.                     edits[0].old_string.as_deref(),
367.                     Some("export const value = 1;")
368.                 );
369.             }
370.             other => panic!("Expected Edit, got {other:?}"),
371.         }
372.     }
373. 
374.     #[test]
375.     fn parse_patch_text_preserves_move_destination_path() {
376.         let patch = r#"*** Begin Patch
377. *** Update File: src/old.ts
378. *** Move to: src/new.ts
379. @@
380. -old
381. +new
382. *** End Patch"#;
383. 
384.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "patch_text": patch });
385.         let result = parse_patch_text(&raw).expect("should parse move patch_text");
386. 
387.         match result {
388.             ToolArguments::Edit { edits } => {
389.                 assert_eq!(edits.len(), 1);
390.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].file_path.as_deref(), Some("src/new.ts"));
391.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].old_string.as_deref(), Some("old"));
392.                 assert_eq!(edits[0].new_string.as_deref(), Some("new"));
393.             }
394.             other => panic!("Expected Edit, got {other:?}"),
395.         }
396.     }
397. 
398.     #[test]
399.     fn parse_patch_text_emits_move_arguments_for_pure_rename_sections() {
400.         let patch = r#"*** Begin Patch
401. *** Update File: src/old.ts
402. *** Move to: src/new.ts
403. *** End Patch"#;
404. 
405.         let raw = serde_json::json!({ "patch_text": patch });
406.         let result = parse_patch_text(&raw).expect("should parse rename patch_text");
407. 
408.         match result {
409.             ToolArguments::Move { from, to } => {
410.                 assert_eq!(from.as_deref(), Some("src/old.ts"));
411.                 assert_eq!(to.as_deref(), Some("src/new.ts"));
412.             }
413.             other => panic!("Expected Move, got {other:?}"),
414.         }
415.     }
416.

419.         let patch = r#"*** Begin Patch
420. *** Update File: src/context.ts

1. //! Shared edit normalization for chat-style ACP agents.
2. 
3. use crate::acp::parsers::arguments::parse_generic_edit_arguments;
4. use crate::acp::parsers::edit_normalizers::parse_changes_map_edit;
5. use crate::acp::session_update::ToolArguments;
6. 
7. use super::patch_text::{parse_patch_text, parse_patch_text_value};
8. 
9. fn parse_inline_patch_string(raw_arguments: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<ToolArguments> {
10.     let patch_text = raw_arguments
11.         .as_str()
12.         .map(str::trim)
13.         .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())?;
14.     parse_patch_text_value(patch_text)
15. }
16. 
17. pub(crate) fn parse_shared_chat_edit_arguments(raw_arguments: &serde_json::Value) -> ToolArguments {
18.     if let Some(arguments_from_patch) =
19.         parse_patch_text(raw_arguments).or_else(|| parse_inline_patch_string(raw_arguments))
20.     {
21.         return arguments_from_patch;
22.     }
23.     if let Some(arguments_from_changes) = parse_changes_map_edit(raw_arguments) {
24.         return arguments_from_changes;
25.     }
26.     parse_generic_edit_arguments(raw_arguments)
27. }
28.

1. <script lang="ts">

19. 
20. interface ToolCallEditProps {
21. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
22. 	turnState?: TurnState;
23. 	projectPath?: string;
24. 	elapsedLabel?: string | null;
25. 	pendingPermission?: PermissionRequest | null;

29. 	toolCall,
30. 	turnState,
31. 	projectPath,
32. 	elapsedLabel,
33. 	pendingPermission = null,

36. const sessionStore = getSessionStore();

40. const themeState = useTheme();
41. const toolStatus = $derived(getToolStatus(toolCall, turnState));
42. const locationFilePath = $derived(toolCall.locations?.[0]?.path ?? null);
43. const permissionFilePath = $derived(
44. 	pendingPermission ? extractPermissionFilePath(pendingPermission) : null
45. );
46. 
47. function normalizeFilePath(value: string | null | undefined): string | null {
48. 	if (value == null) return null;
49. 	const trimmed = value.trim();
50. 	return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
51. }
52. 
53. const editDiffs = $derived.by(() => {
54. 	const streamingArgs = sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id);
55. 	const resolvedDiffs = resolveToolCallEditDiffs(toolCall.arguments, streamingArgs);
56. 
57. 	return resolvedDiffs.map((diff, index) => {
58. 		const filePath =
59. 			normalizeFilePath(diff.filePath) ??
60. 			(index === 0
61. 				? (normalizeFilePath(locationFilePath) ?? normalizeFilePath(permissionFilePath))
62. 				: null);
63. 		const diffStats = calculateDiffStats({
64. 			oldString: diff.oldString,
65. 			newString: diff.newString,
66. 		}) ?? { added: 0, removed: 0 };
67. 
68. 		return {
69. 			filePath,
70. 			fileName: getFileName(filePath),
71. 			oldString: diff.oldString,
72. 			newString: diff.newString,
73. 			additions: diffStats.added,
74. 			deletions: diffStats.removed,
75. 		};
76. 	});
77. });
78. 
79. const primaryDiff = $derived(editDiffs[0] ?? null);
80. const filePath = $derived(primaryDiff?.filePath ?? null);
81. 
82. // Streaming detection
83. const isStreaming = $derived(
84. 	toolStatus.isInputStreaming || (toolStatus.isPending && turnState === "streaming")
85. );
86. 
87. // Don't treat as streaming when waiting for permission approval
88. // (tool has all its data, just needs user consent)
89. const isDiffStreaming = $derived(pendingPermission ? false : isStreaming);
90. 
91. // Map tool status to AgentToolStatus
92. const agentStatus = $derived.by(() => {
93. 	if (toolStatus.isPending) return "running" as const;
94. 	if (toolStatus.isError) return "error" as const;
95. 	return "done" as const;
96. });
97. const isApplied = $derived(toolStatus.isSuccess);
98. const isAwaitingApproval = $derived(
99. 	Boolean(pendingPermission) && !isApplied && !toolStatus.isError
100. );
101. 
102. const isFileClickable = $derived(Boolean(projectPath));
103. 
104. function handleFileClick(selectedFilePath?: string | null) {
105. 	const targetFilePath = selectedFilePath ?? filePath;
106. 	if (targetFilePath && projectPath) {
107. 		panelStore.openFilePanel(
108. 			getRelativeFilePath(targetFilePath, projectPath) ?? targetFilePath,
109. 			projectPath,
110. 			{
111. 				ownerPanelId,
112. 			}
113. 		);
114. 	}
115. }
116. 
117. // Pierre diffs configuration
118. const effectiveTheme = $derived(themeState.effectiveTheme);
119. const workerPool = getWorkerPool();
120. const themeNames = { dark: "Cursor Dark", light: "pierre-light" };
121. </script>
122. 
123. <AgentToolEdit
124. 	diffs={editDiffs}
125. 	{filePath}
126. 	fileName={primaryDiff?.fileName ?? null}
127. 	additions={primaryDiff?.additions ?? 0}
128. 	deletions={primaryDiff?.deletions ?? 0}
129. 	oldString={primaryDiff?.oldString ?? null}
130. 	newString={primaryDiff?.newString ?? null}
131. 	isStreaming={isDiffStreaming}
132. 	status={agentStatus}
133. 	applied={isApplied}
134. 	awaitingApproval={isAwaitingApproval}
135. 	durationLabel={elapsedLabel ?? undefined}

137. 	interactive={isFileClickable}
138. 	onSelect={isFileClickable ? handleFileClick : undefined}
139. 	theme={effectiveTheme}
140. 	{themeNames}
141. 	{workerPool}

143. 	unsafeCSS={pierreDiffsUnsafeCSS}

145. 	editedLabel={m.tool_edit_edited()}

### 💭 Reasoning

***Considering tool modifications**

I’m thinking about deleting the card in a minimal way, but I need to align everything properly. I wonder whether I could use the existing `AgentToolEdit` for this, focusing just on deletions. However, there’s no `AgentToolDelete`, so I could consider creating one in the UI package. The user wants the UI to align with the rest of the cards, which requires some creative effort. Although brainstorming is needed here, the user did explicitly request this change. But I just realized I haven’t asked for approval yet.**Evaluating user requests and design approval**

The user’s request is clear, and I’m aware I have autonomy. But there’s a gate that says no implementation without design approval. The tricky part is that no design has been presented yet. We seem to be building off previous work, which feels like it could be seen as design approval. However, the current message is just a summary from the system, not a new user request. Still, since the user directly asked, I should probably proceed without further clarification, even if it means navigating the brainstorming requirement.**Addressing user instructions and ambiguity**

The instructions say I should handle one question at a time when there's ambiguity. I'm seeing some ambiguity around mixed patches, which leaves me thinking whether I should ask the user directly about it, as per the guidelines for behavioral choices. I could also choose a safe default myself, but the instructions are clear about asking first. I need to check AGENTS.md to review project-specific rules before proceeding. Oh no, and I have to remember to include a report of intent with every initial tool call after a user message—I've already missed that in previous calls. I need to be careful moving forward!**Planning task execution**

I should probably read AGENTS.md now. I wonder if I need to create a plan for tasks across several files, or if that’s unnecessary. Using SQL for tracking could be overkill, but the instructions say to run baseline lint, builds, and tests first. I’ll inspect the package commands and run relevant tests before and after. The guidelines suggest running repository linters and established tests, even if it may be a large task. Targeted tests might be better.**Running tests and inspections**

Since I’m dealing with code changes in TypeScript and Rust, I probably need to run the relevant tests. I could do `cd packages/desktop && bun run check`, along with targeted `cargo test` for Rust. It seems wise to establish a baseline before making changes since prior Rust tests have passed. Ensuring relevant tests like rust session_update tests and TS compile checks are updated is important. I might also need to inspect AGENTS.md and the current git status to ensure everything is clean. I'll check for `tool-call-delete.svelte` in the registry later.**Planning implementation changes**

I need to consider whether to ask the user before implementing the mixed patch behavior. The instructions from the brainstorming session suggest I should. However, the user has specified that the delete-file should be handled as a single delete tool call, which means I can just implement that directly without asking further. In my design, I’m thinking: first, parse the patch text to return Delete when it’s a single delete section. Then update the test expectations and possibly add a tool_classification test. Finally, I’ll restyle the UI for `tool-call-delete.svelte` to match the other elements.*

11. 		command: string | null;
12. 		stdout?: string | null;
13. 		stderr?: string | null;
14. 		exitCode?: number;
15. 		status?: AgentToolStatus;
16. 		durationLabel?: string;
17. 		/** Label when running with no command yet */
18. 		runningNoCmdLabel?: string;
19. 		/** Label when running with command */
20. 		runningLabel?: string;

25. 		/** Label for failed exit */
26. 		failedLabel?: string;
27. 		/** Aria label to collapse output */
28. 		ariaCollapseOutput?: string;
29. 		/** Aria label to expand output */
30. 		ariaExpandOutput?: string;
31. 	}
32. 
33. 	let {
34. 		command,
35. 		stdout,
36. 		stderr,
37. 		exitCode,
38. 		status = "done",
39. 		durationLabel,
40. 		runningNoCmdLabel = "Running command…",
41. 		runningLabel = "Running command:",
42. 		doneLabel = "Ran command:",
43. 		successLabel = "Success",
44. 		failedLabel = "Failed",
45. 		ariaCollapseOutput = "Collapse output",
46. 		ariaExpandOutput = "Expand output",

48. 
49. 	let isExpanded = $state(false);

53. 	const isError = $derived(exitCode !== undefined && exitCode !== 0);
54. 	const hasOutput = $derived(Boolean(stdout || stderr));
55. 
56. 	function extractCommandSummary(cmd: string): string {
57. 		const normalizedCommand = cmd.replace(/\\\s*\n\s*/g, " ");
58. 		const parts = normalizedCommand.split(/\s*(?:&&|\|\||;|\|)\s*/);
59. 		const firstWords = parts.map((p) => p.trim().split(/\s+/)[0]).filter(Boolean);
60. 		const limited = firstWords.slice(0, 4);
61. 		if (firstWords.length > 4) {
62. 			return limited.join(", ") + "...";
63. 		}
64. 		return limited.join(", ");
65. 	}
66. 
67. 	const commandSummary = $derived(command ? extractCommandSummary(command) : "");
68. 
69. 	const stderrColorClass = $derived(
70. 		exitCode === 0 || exitCode === undefined
71. 			? "text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400"
72. 			: "text-rose-500 dark:text-rose-400"
73. 	);
74. </script>
