32. import ToolCallWebSearch from "./tool-call-web-search.svelte";
33. import { parseToolResultWithExitCode } from "./tool-call-execute/logic/parse-tool-result.js";
34. import { toAgentToolKind } from "./tool-kind-to-agent-tool-kind.js";
35. 
36. export type ToolDetailComponentProps = {
37. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
38. 	turnState?: TurnState;
39. 	projectPath?: string;
40. 	elapsedLabel?: string | null;
41. 	pendingPermission?: PermissionRequest | null;
42. };
43. 
44. interface ToolDisplayOptions {
45. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
46. 	toolKind?: ToolKind | null;
47. 	turnState?: TurnState;
48. 	parentCompleted?: boolean;
49. }
50. 
51. export type CompactToolDisplay = Pick<
52. 	AgentToolEntry,
53. 	"id" | "kind" | "title" | "filePath" | "status"
54. >;
55. 
56. export interface ToolDefinition {
57. 	rendererKey: ToolRouteKey;
58. 	component: Component<ToolDetailComponentProps>;
59. 	buildFullEntry: (options: ToolDisplayOptions) => AgentToolEntry;
60. 	buildCompactEntry: (options: ToolDisplayOptions) => CompactToolDisplay;
61. }
62. 
63. function mapAgentToolStatus(
64. 	toolCall: ToolCall,
65. 	turnState: TurnState | undefined,
66. 	parentCompleted: boolean
67. ): AgentToolStatus {
68. 	if (toolCall.status === "failed") {
69. 		return "error";
70. 	}
71. 
72. 	if (toolCall.status === "completed") {
73. 		return "done";
74. 	}
75. 
76. 	const hasResult = toolCall.result !== null && toolCall.result !== undefined;
77. 	if (hasResult) {
78. 		return "done";
79. 	}
80. 
81. 	if (parentCompleted) {
82. 		return "done";
83. 	}
84. 
85. 	if (toolCall.status === "in_progress" && turnState === "streaming") {
86. 		return "running";
87. 	}
88. 
89. 	if (toolCall.status === "in_progress" && turnState !== undefined && turnState !== "streaming") {
90. 		return "done";
91. 	}
92. 
93. 	return "pending";
94. }
95. 
96. function compactToolEntry(entry: AgentToolEntry): CompactToolDisplay {
97. 	return {
98. 		id: entry.id,
99. 		kind: entry.kind,
100. 		title: entry.filePath ? entry.title : (entry.subtitle ?? entry.title),
101. 		filePath: entry.filePath,
102. 		status: entry.status,
103. 	};
104. }
105. 
106. function resolveToolEntryTitle(options: ToolDisplayOptions, kind: ToolKind): string {
107. 	const semanticTitle =
108. 		getToolKindTitle(kind, options.toolCall, options.turnState) ?? options.toolCall.name;
109. 	const rawTitle = options.toolCall.title?.trim();
110. 
111. 	if (!rawTitle) {
112. 		return semanticTitle;
113. 	}
114. 
115. 	if (
116. 		kind === "delete" &&
117. 		rawTitle.localeCompare("apply_patch", undefined, { sensitivity: "accent" }) === 0
118. 	) {
119. 		return semanticTitle;
120. 	}
121. 
122. 	return rawTitle;
123. }
124. 
125. function buildDefaultFullEntry(options: ToolDisplayOptions): AgentToolEntry {
126. 	const kind = options.toolKind ?? options.toolCall.kind ?? "other";
127. 
128. 	return {
129. 		id: options.toolCall.id,
130. 		type: "tool_call",
131. 		kind: toAgentToolKind(kind),
132. 		title: resolveToolEntryTitle(options, kind),
133. 		subtitle: getToolKindSubtitle(kind, options.toolCall) || undefined,
134. 		filePath: getToolKindFilePath(kind, options.toolCall) ?? undefined,
135. 		status: mapAgentToolStatus(
136. 			options.toolCall,
137. 			options.turnState,
138. 			options.parentCompleted === true
139. 		),
140. 	};
141. }
142. 
143. function createToolDefinition(
144. 	rendererKey: ToolRouteKey,
145. 	component: Component<ToolDetailComponentProps>
146. ): ToolDefinition {
147. 	return {
148. 		rendererKey,
149. 		component,
150. 		buildFullEntry: buildDefaultFullEntry,
151. 		buildCompactEntry: (options) => compactToolEntry(buildDefaultFullEntry(options)),
152. 	};
153. }
154. 
155. function buildExecuteFullEntry(options: ToolDisplayOptions): AgentToolEntry {
156. 	const baseEntry = buildDefaultFullEntry(options);
157. 	const parsedResult = parseToolResultWithExitCode(options.toolCall.result);
158. 	const command =
159. 		options.toolCall.arguments.kind === "execute"
160. 			? options.toolCall.arguments.command
161. 			: (baseEntry.subtitle ?? null);
162. 
163. 	return {
164. 		id: baseEntry.id,
165. 		type: baseEntry.type,
166. 		kind: baseEntry.kind,
167. 		title: baseEntry.title,
168. 		subtitle: baseEntry.subtitle,
169. 		filePath: baseEntry.filePath,
170. 		status: baseEntry.status,
171. 		command,
172. 		stdout: parsedResult.stdout,
173. 		stderr: parsedResult.stderr,
174. 		exitCode: parsedResult.exitCode,
175. 	};
176. }
177. 
178. const TOOL_DEFINITIONS: Partial<Record<ToolRouteKey, ToolDefinition>> = {
179. 	read: createToolDefinition("read", ToolCallRead),
180. 	read_lints: createToolDefinition("read_lints", ToolCallReadLints),
181. 	edit: createToolDefinition("edit", ToolCallEdit),
182. 	execute: {
183. 		rendererKey: "execute",
184. 		component: ToolCallExecute,
185. 		buildFullEntry: buildExecuteFullEntry,
186. 		buildCompactEntry: (options) => compactToolEntry(buildExecuteFullEntry(options)),
187. 	},
188. 	search: createToolDefinition("search", ToolCallSearch),
189. 	glob: createToolDefinition("glob", ToolCallSearch),
190. 	fetch: createToolDefinition("fetch", ToolCallFetch),
191. 	web_search: createToolDefinition("web_search", ToolCallWebSearch),
192. 	enter_plan_mode: createToolDefinition("enter_plan_mode", ToolCallEnterPlanMode),
193. 	exit_plan_mode: createToolDefinition("exit_plan_mode", ToolCallExitPlanMode),
194. 	create_plan: createToolDefinition("create_plan", ToolCallCreatePlan),
195. 	delete: createToolDefinition("delete", ToolCallDelete),
196. 	think: createToolDefinition("think", ToolCallThink),
197. 	todo: createToolDefinition("todo", ToolCallTodo),
198. 	question: createToolDefinition("question", ToolCallQuestion),
199. 	task: createToolDefinition("task", ToolCallTask),
200. 	task_output: createToolDefinition("task_output", ToolCallTaskOutput),
201. 	skill: createToolDefinition("skill", ToolCallSkill),
202. 	tool_search: createToolDefinition("tool_search", ToolCallToolSearch),
203. 	other: createToolDefinition("other", ToolCallFallback),
204. };
205. 
206. const FALLBACK_TOOL_DEFINITION = createToolDefinition("other", ToolCallFallback);
207. 
208. export function getToolDefinition(
209. 	toolCall: ToolCall,
210. 	toolKind?: ToolKind | null
211. ): ToolDefinition {
212. 	const resolvedKind = toolKind ?? toolCall.kind ?? "other";
213. 	const routeKey = resolveToolRouteKey(toolCall, resolvedKind);
214. 	return TOOL_DEFINITIONS[routeKey] ?? FALLBACK_TOOL_DEFINITION;
215. }
216. 
217. export function resolveFullToolEntry(options: ToolDisplayOptions): AgentToolEntry {
218. 	return getToolDefinition(options.toolCall, options.toolKind).buildFullEntry(options);
219. }
220. 
221. export function resolveCompactToolDisplay(options: ToolDisplayOptions): CompactToolDisplay {
222. 	return getToolDefinition(options.toolCall, options.toolKind).buildCompactEntry(options);
223. }
224. 
225. export function compactAgentToolEntry(entry: AgentToolEntry): CompactToolDisplay {
226. 	return compactToolEntry(entry);
227. }
228.
```

```
1. import { err, ok, type Result } from "neverthrow";
2. import { safeJsonParse } from "../../../../logic/json-utils.js";
3. import { EXECUTE_TOOL_ERROR_CODES, ExecuteToolError } from "../errors/execute-tool-error.js";
4. import { ToolResultOutputSchema } from "../schemas/tool-result-output.schema.js";
5. 
6. /**
7.  * Parsed result with stdout, stderr, and exit code (like 1code).
8.  */
9. export interface ParsedToolResult {
10. 	stdout: string | null;
11. 	stderr: string | null;
12. 	exitCode: number | undefined;
13. }
14. 
15. interface NormalizedExecEnvelope {
16. 	output: string;
17. 	exitCode: number | undefined;
18. }
19. 
20. function normalizeExecEnvelope(raw: string): NormalizedExecEnvelope {
21. 	const outputMarker = /(?:^|\n)Output:\r?\n/;
22. 	const markerMatch = outputMarker.exec(raw);
23. 	if (!markerMatch) {
24. 		return { output: raw, exitCode: undefined };
25. 	}
26. 
27. 	const header = raw.slice(0, markerMatch.index + markerMatch[0].length);
28. 	const output = raw.slice(markerMatch.index + markerMatch[0].length);
29. 	const exitCodeMatch = /Process exited with code\s+(-?\d+)/i.exec(header);
30. 	const parsedExitCode =
31. 		exitCodeMatch && Number.isFinite(Number(exitCodeMatch[1]))
32. 			? Number(exitCodeMatch[1])
33. 			: undefined;
34. 
35. 	return { output, exitCode: parsedExitCode };
36. }
37. 
38. /**
39.  * Parses tool result to extract stdout, stderr, and exit code separately.
40.  * Matches 1code's behavior for bash tool output display.
41.  *
42.  * @param result - Unknown tool result to parse
43.  * @returns Parsed result with stdout, stderr, and exitCode
44.  */
45. export function parseToolResultWithExitCode(result: unknown): ParsedToolResult {
46. 	// Handle null/undefined early
47. 	if (result === null || result === undefined) {
48. 		return { stdout: null, stderr: null, exitCode: undefined };
49. 	}
50. 
51. 	// If result is an object, extract fields directly
52. 	if (typeof result === "object" && !Array.isArray(result)) {
53. 		const obj = result as Record<string, unknown>;
54. 
55. 		// Extract stdout (check output first, then stdout)
56. 		let stdout: string | null = null;
57. 		let envelopeExitCode: number | undefined;
58. 		if (typeof obj.output === "string") {
59. 			const normalized = normalizeExecEnvelope(obj.output);
60. 			stdout = normalized.output;
61. 			envelopeExitCode = normalized.exitCode;
62. 		} else if (typeof obj.stdout === "string") {
63. 			const normalized = normalizeExecEnvelope(obj.stdout);
64. 			stdout = normalized.output;
65. 			envelopeExitCode = normalized.exitCode;
66. 		}
67. 
68. 		// Extract stderr
69. 		let stderr: string | null = null;
70. 		if (typeof obj.stderr === "string") {
71. 			stderr = obj.stderr;
72. 		}
73. 
74. 		// Extract exit code (check exitCode and exit_code)
75. 		let exitCode: number | undefined;
76. 		if (typeof obj.exitCode === "number") {
77. 			exitCode = obj.exitCode;
78. 		} else if (typeof obj.exit_code === "number") {
79. 			exitCode = obj.exit_code;
80. 		} else {
81. 			exitCode = envelopeExitCode;
82. 		}
83. 
84. 		return { stdout, stderr, exitCode };
85. 	}
86. 
87. 	// If result is a string, treat it as stdout
88. 	if (typeof result === "string") {
89. 		const normalized = normalizeExecEnvelope(result);
90. 		return {
91. 			stdout: normalized.output,
92. 			stderr: null,
93. 			exitCode: normalized.exitCode,
94. 		};
95. 	}
96. 
97. 	return { stdout: null, stderr: null, exitCode: undefined };
98. }
99. 
100. /**
101.  * Parses tool result output from various formats.
102.  *
103.  * Handles:
104.  * - Plain string results
105.  * - JSON-stringified strings
106.  * - Objects with output/stdout/stderr fields
107.  * - Nested JSON-stringified values
108.  *
109.  * Uses Zod schemas and safeJsonParse to avoid try-catch blocks.
110.  *
111.  * @param result - Unknown tool result to parse
112.  * @returns Result containing parsed string output or null, or an error
113.  */
114. export function parseToolResultOutput(result: unknown): Result<string | null, ExecuteToolError> {
115. 	// Handle null/undefined early
116. 	if (result === null || result === undefined) {
117. 		return ok(null);
118. 	}
119. 
120. 	// Use Zod schema to validate and extract structure
121. 	const schemaResult = ToolResultOutputSchema.safeParse(result);
122. 
123. 	if (!schemaResult.success) {
124. 		return err(
125. 			new ExecuteToolError(
126. 				`Failed to parse tool result: ${schemaResult.error.message}`,
127. 				EXECUTE_TOOL_ERROR_CODES.PARSE_RESULT_FAILED,
128. 				schemaResult.error
129. 			)
130. 		);
131. 	}
132. 
133. 	const parsed = schemaResult.data;
134. 
135. 	// If result is null (from object with no output fields), return null
136. 	if (parsed === null) {
137. 		return ok(null);
138. 	}
139. 
140. 	// If result is a string, try to parse it as JSON
141. 	if (typeof parsed === "string") {
142. 		const normalized = normalizeExecEnvelope(parsed);
143. 		const normalizedOutput = normalized.output;
144. 
145. 		// Try to parse as JSON-stringified string
146. 		const jsonParseResult = safeJsonParse<unknown>(normalizedOutput);
147. 
148. 		if (jsonParseResult.isOk()) {
149. 			const parsedValue = jsonParseResult.value;
150. 			// If successfully parsed and result is a string, return it
151. 			if (typeof parsedValue === "string") {
152. 				return ok(parsedValue);
153. 			}
154. 		}
155. 
156. 		// If JSON parsing failed or result is not a string, return original
157. 		return ok(normalizedOutput);
158. 	}
159. 
160. 	// This should not happen based on schema, but handle it
161. 	return ok(null);
162. }
163.
```

</details>

19. 	/**
20. 	 * The tool call to display.
21. 	 */
22. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
23. 	/**
24. 	 * Turn state for dynamic UI updates.
25. 	 */
26. 	turnState?: TurnState;
27. 	/**
28. 	 * Project path for opening files in panels.
29. 	 */
30. 	projectPath?: string;
31. }
32. 
33. let { toolCall, turnState }: ExecuteToolUIProps = $props();
34. 
35. // Get comprehensive tool status (includes interrupt detection)
36. const toolStatus = $derived(getToolStatus(toolCall, turnState));
37. 
38. const sessionStore = getSessionStore();
39. 
40. // Use state class for UI state management
41. const uiState = new ExecuteToolUIState();
42. 
43. // Extract command: streaming args → typed arguments → backtick title

45. 	resolveExecuteCommand(
46. 		sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id),
47. 		toolCall.arguments,
48. 		toolCall.title
49. 	)
50. );
51. 
52. // Parse result with stdout, stderr, and exit code (like 1code)
53. const parsedResult = $derived.by(() => {
54. 	return parseToolResultWithExitCode(toolCall.result);
55. });
56. 
57. const stdout = $derived(parsedResult.stdout);
58. const stderr = $derived(parsedResult.stderr);
59. const exitCode = $derived(parsedResult.exitCode);
60. 
61. // Determine if we have any output
62. const hasOutput = $derived(stdout !== null || stderr !== null);
63. 
64. // Show content when we have a command to display (even while running) or output (after completion)
65. const hasContent = $derived(extractedCommand !== null || hasOutput);
66. 
67. function handleToggleExpand() {
68. 	uiState.toggleCollapse();
69. }
70. 
71. function handleClickExpand() {
72. 	if (uiState.isCollapsed) {
73. 		uiState.toggleCollapse();
74. 	}
75. }
76. 
77. function handleCloseModal() {
78. 	uiState.closeModal();
79. }
80. 
81. const modalDisplayOutput = $derived.by(() => {
82. 	// Combine stdout and stderr for modal display
83. 	let output = "";
84. 	if (stdout) {
85. 		output += stripAnsiCodes(stdout);
86. 	}
87. 	if (stderr) {
88. 		if (output) output += "\n";
89. 		output += stripAnsiCodes(stderr);
90. 	}
91. 	if (output) return output;
92. 
93. 	const parseResult = parseToolResultOutput(toolCall.result);
94. 	if (parseResult.isOk() && parseResult.value) {
95. 		return stripAnsiCodes(parseResult.value);
96. 	}
97. 
98. 	if (toolCall.result) {
99. 		const stringifyResult = safeJsonStringify(toolCall.result);
100. 		if (stringifyResult.isOk()) {
101. 			return stringifyResult.value;
102. 		}
103. 		return String(toolCall.result);
104. 	}
105. 
106. 	return null;
107. });
108. </script>
109.

137. 				{stderr}
138. 				{exitCode}

140. 				isExpanded={!uiState.isCollapsed}
141. 				onClickExpand={handleClickExpand}
142. 			/>
143. 		{/if}
144. 	</AgentToolCard>
145. {/if}
146. 
147. <ToolContentModal bind:isOpen={uiState.isModalOpen} onClose={handleCloseModal} {toolCall}>
148. 	{#snippet title()}
149. 		<div class="text-sm font-medium">Execute Tool Output</div>
150. 	{/snippet}
151. 
152. 	<div class="flex flex-col gap-4">
153. 		<!-- Command Card -->

157. 			</div>

```
1. /**
2.  * Tool Call Event Flow Tests
3.  *
4.  * TDD tests to verify the complete flow of tool call events from
5.  * ACP session updates through to UI rendering.
6.  *
7.  * The flow is:
8.  * 1. ACP agent sends `tool_call` session update
9.  * 2. Rust backend parses and emits typed SessionUpdate event
10.  * 3. Frontend EventSubscriber receives the event
11.  * 4. SessionEventService.handleSessionUpdate processes it
12.  * 5. SessionStore.createToolCallEntry creates the entry
13.  * 6. UI receives the entry and renders via ToolCallRouter
14.  */
15. 
16. import { describe, expect, it } from "bun:test";
17. 
18. import type { SessionUpdate, ToolCallData } from "../../../services/converted-session-types.js";
19. import type { SessionEntry } from "../../application/dto/session.js";
20. 
21. import { SessionEntryStore } from "../session-entry-store.svelte.js";
22. 
23. function applyStreamingArguments(
24. 	entryStore: SessionEntryStore,
25. 	sessionId: string,
26. 	toolCallId: string,
27. 	streamingArguments: Parameters<SessionEntryStore["updateToolCallEntry"]>[1]["streamingArguments"]
28. ): void {
29. 	entryStore.updateToolCallEntry(sessionId, {
30. 		toolCallId,
31. 		status: null,
32. 		result: null,
33. 		content: null,
34. 		rawOutput: null,
35. 		title: null,
36. 		locations: null,
37. 		normalizedTodos: null,
38. 		normalizedQuestions: null,
39. 		streamingArguments,
40. 	});
41. }
42. 
43. /**
44.  * Create a mock tool_call SessionUpdate matching what Rust sends.
45.  *
46.  * Based on the JSONL data:
47.  * - tool_use blocks have: id, name (mcp__acp__Read), input
48.  * - ACP adapter transforms to: toolCallId, _meta.claudeCode.toolName, rawInput, status, kind
49.  */
50. function createToolCallUpdate(
51. 	sessionId: string,
52. 	toolCallId: string,
53. 	toolName: string,
54. 	kind: "read" | "edit" | "execute" | "search" | "other" = "other",
55. 	filePath?: string
56. ): SessionUpdate {
57. 	const toolCall: ToolCallData = {
58. 		id: toolCallId,
59. 		name: toolName,
60. 		arguments:
61. 			kind === "read"
62. 				? { kind: "read", file_path: filePath ?? null }
63. 				: kind === "edit"
64. 					? {
65. 							kind: "edit",
66. 							edits: [
67. 								{ filePath: filePath ?? null, oldString: null, newString: null, content: null },
68. 							],
69. 						}
70. 					: { kind: "other", raw: {} },
71. 		status: "pending",
72. 		kind: kind,
73. 		title: null,
74. 		locations: null,
75. 		skillMeta: null,
76. 		result: null,
77. 		awaitingPlanApproval: false,
78. 	};
79. 
80. 	return {
81. 		type: "toolCall",
82. 		tool_call: toolCall,
83. 		session_id: sessionId,
84. 	};
85. }
86. 
87. /**
88.  * Create a mock tool_call_update SessionUpdate matching what Rust sends.
89.  */
90. function createToolCallUpdateEvent(
91. 	sessionId: string,
92. 	toolCallId: string,
93. 	status: "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed" | "failed" = "completed"
94. ): SessionUpdate {
95. 	return {
96. 		type: "toolCallUpdate",
97. 		update: {
98. 			toolCallId: toolCallId,
99. 			status: status,
100. 			result: null,
101. 			content: null,
102. 			rawOutput: null,
103. 			title: null,
104. 			locations: null,
105. 		},
106. 		session_id: sessionId,
107. 	};
108. }
109. 
110. /**
111.  * Helper to convert a SessionUpdate tool_call to a SessionEntry.
112.  * This mirrors the logic in session-event-service.svelte.ts
113.  */
114. function convertToolCallToEntry(update: SessionUpdate, entryId: string): SessionEntry | null {
115. 	if (update.type !== "toolCall") {
116. 		return null;
117. 	}
118. 
119. 	const toolCallData = update.tool_call;
120. 	return {
121. 		id: entryId,
122. 		type: "tool_call",
123. 		message: toolCallData,
124. 		timestamp: new Date(),
125. 	};
126. }
127. 
128. describe("Tool Call Event Flow", () => {
129. 	async function appendAssistantChunks(
130. 		entryStore: SessionEntryStore,
131. 		sessionId: string,
132. 		messageId: string,
133. 		textChunks: readonly string[]
134. 	): Promise<void> {
135. 		for (const text of textChunks) {
136. 			const result = await entryStore.aggregateAssistantChunk(
137. 				sessionId,
138. 				{ content: { type: "text", text } },
139. 				messageId,
140. 				false
141. 			);
142. 			expect(result.isOk()).toBe(true);
143. 		}
144. 	}
145. 
146. 	function assistantEntryText(entry: SessionEntry): string {
147. 		if (entry.type !== "assistant") return "";
148. 		return entry.message.chunks
149. 			.map((chunk) => (chunk.block.type === "text" ? chunk.block.text : ""))
150. 			.join("");
151. 	}
152. 
153. 	describe("SessionUpdate parsing", () => {
154. 		it("should correctly identify toolCall update type", () => {
155. 			const update = createToolCallUpdate(
156. 				"sess-123",
157. 				"toolu_016jRdp79JqqfcH22yvZLkF3",
158. 				"mcp__acp__Read",
159. 				"read",
160. 				"/Users/example/Documents/acepe/packages/desktop/src/lib/test-file.ts"
161. 			);
162. 
163. 			expect(update.type).toBe("toolCall");
164. 		});
165. 
166. 		it("should preserve tool call data through the update structure", () => {
167. 			const update = createToolCallUpdate(
168. 				"sess-123",
169. 				"toolu_016jRdp79JqqfcH22yvZLkF3",
170. 				"mcp__acp__Read",
171. 				"read",
172. 				"/path/to/file.ts"
173. 			);
174. 
175. 			if (update.type === "toolCall") {
176. 				const toolCall = update.tool_call;
177. 				expect(toolCall.id).toBe("toolu_016jRdp79JqqfcH22yvZLkF3");
178. 				expect(toolCall.name).toBe("mcp__acp__Read");
179. 				expect(toolCall.kind).toBe("read");
180. 				expect(toolCall.status).toBe("pending");
181. 
182. 				if (toolCall.arguments.kind === "read") {
183. 					expect(toolCall.arguments.file_path).toBe("/path/to/file.ts");
184. 				}
185. 			}
186. 		});
187.

191. 				tool_call: {
192. 					id: "toolu_01SYJ3hktXpiGHpucHrjNj7U",
193. 					name: "mcp__acp__Edit",
194. 					arguments: {

200. 								newString: "export function multiply\n\nexport function subtract",
201. 								content: null,

204. 					},

340. 	/**
341. 	 * Update tool call entry.
342. 	 * Backend handles child-to-parent updates via TaskReconciler.
343. 	 */
344. 	updateEntry(sessionId: string, update: ToolCallUpdate): Result<void, AppError> {
345. 		this.rememberToolCallSession(sessionId, update.toolCallId);
346. 
347. 		// Extract result from update.result, update.rawOutput, or content (as fallback)
348. 		const extractedResult =
349. 			update.result ?? update.rawOutput ?? extractResultFromContent(update.content);
350. 
351. 		const entryRef = this.findToolCallEntryRef(sessionId, update.toolCallId);
352. 
353. 		if (!entryRef) {
354. 			if (isStreamingOnlyToolUpdate(update)) {
355. 				logger.debug("Ignoring streaming-only tool update without canonical entry", {
356. 					sessionId,
357. 					toolCallId: update.toolCallId,
358. 				});
359. 				return ok(undefined);
360. 			}
361. 
362. 			// Entry doesn't exist yet - create it
363. 			logger.debug("Creating tool call entry from update", {
364. 				sessionId,
365. 				toolCallId: update.toolCallId,
366. 			});
367. 
368. 			const createdAtMs = nowMs();
369. 			const newToolCall: ToolCall = {
370. 				id: update.toolCallId,
371. 				name: "Tool",
372. 				arguments: update.arguments ?? { kind: "other", raw: {} },
373. 				status: update.status ?? "pending",
374. 				result: extractedResult,
375. 				title: update.title,
376. 				locations: update.locations,
377. 				normalizedTodos: update.normalizedTodos,
378. 				normalizedQuestions: update.normalizedQuestions,
379. 				awaitingPlanApproval: false,
380. 				progressiveArguments: update.streamingArguments ?? undefined,
381. 				startedAtMs: createdAtMs,
382. 				completedAtMs: isTerminalStatus(update.status) ? createdAtMs : undefined,
383. 			};
384. 
385. 			const newEntry: SessionEntry = {
386. 				id: update.toolCallId,
387. 				type: "tool_call",
388. 				message: newToolCall,
389. 				timestamp: new Date(createdAtMs),
390. 				isStreaming: isToolCallStreaming(update.status),
391. 			};
392. 
393. 			this.entryStore.addEntry(sessionId, newEntry);
394. 			this.operationStore.upsertFromToolCall(sessionId, newEntry.id, newToolCall);
395. 			return ok(undefined);
396. 		}
397. 
398. 		const entry = entryRef.entry;
399. 		if (!isToolCallEntry(entry)) {
400. 			logger.warn("Entry is not a tool call", { sessionId, entryId: entry.id });
401. 			return ok(undefined);
402. 		}
403. 
404. 		const toolCall = entry.message;
405. 
406. 		// Determine the result to use:
407. 		// - If extractedResult is null, keep existing toolCall.result
408. 		// - If toolCall.result is a structured object (e.g., {numFiles: 4}) and extractedResult
409. 		//   is just a string (text from content), preserve the structured result
410. 		// - Otherwise, use extractedResult
411. 		const isStructuredResult =
412. 			toolCall.result !== null &&
413. 			typeof toolCall.result === "object" &&
414. 			!Array.isArray(toolCall.result);
415. 		const isTextExtracted = typeof extractedResult === "string";
416. 		const shouldPreserveStructuredResult = isStructuredResult && isTextExtracted;
417. 		const incomingStatus = update.status ?? toolCall.status;
418. 		const newStatus = resolveNextStatus(toolCall.status, incomingStatus);
419. 		const startedAtMs = toolCall.startedAtMs ?? entry.timestamp?.getTime() ?? nowMs();
420. 		const completedAtMs =
421. 			toolCall.completedAtMs ?? (isTerminalStatus(newStatus) ? nowMs() : undefined);
422. 		const rawNextArguments = update.arguments ?? update.streamingArguments ?? toolCall.arguments;
423. 		const nextArguments =
424. 			rawNextArguments === toolCall.arguments
425. 				? toolCall.arguments
426. 				: mergeToolArguments(toolCall.arguments, rawNextArguments);
427. 		const nextProgressiveArguments = isTerminalStatus(newStatus)
428. 			? undefined
429. 			: (update.arguments ?? null) != null
430. 				? undefined
```

480. 		if (!hasSession) {
481. 			this.bufferPendingEvent(sessionId, update);
482. 			return;
483. 		}
484. 
485. 		switch (update.type) {
486. 			case "agentMessageChunk": {
487. 				const aggregationKey = getAssistantAggregationKey(update);
488. 				// Text chunks are handled by fast path above, this handles non-text (e.g., tool_use)
489. 				handler
490. 					.aggregateAssistantChunk(sessionId, update.chunk, aggregationKey, false)
491. 					.mapErr((error) => logger.error("Failed to aggregate message chunk", { error }));
492. 				handler.ensureStreamingState(sessionId);
493. 				break;
494. 			}
495. 
496. 			case "agentThoughtChunk": {
497. 				const aggregationKey = getAssistantAggregationKey(update);
498. 				// Text chunks are handled by fast path above, this handles non-text
499. 				handler
500. 					.aggregateAssistantChunk(sessionId, update.chunk, aggregationKey, true)
501. 					.mapErr((error) => logger.error("Failed to aggregate thought chunk", { error }));
502. 				handler.ensureStreamingState(sessionId);
503. 				break;
504. 			}
505. 
506. 			case "toolCall":
507. 				logger.debug("Creating tool call entry", {
508. 					sessionId,
509. 					toolCallId: update.tool_call.id,
510. 					toolName: update.tool_call.name,
511. 					toolKind: update.tool_call.kind,
512. 				});
513. 				handler.createToolCallEntry(sessionId, update.tool_call);
514. 				handler.ensureStreamingState(sessionId);
515. 				break;
516. 
517. 			case "toolCallUpdate":
518. 				// Streaming input deltas are handled by fast path above
519. 				// Regular tool call update (status, result, etc.)
520. 				// Apply canonical backend-owned tool updates directly; the frontend no longer
521. 				// reconstructs child mutations from child-only deltas.
522. 				logger.debug("Updating tool call entry (may be child)", {
523. 					sessionId,
524. 					toolCallId: update.update.toolCallId,

476. 	getStreamingArguments(toolCallId: string): ToolArguments | undefined {
477. 		const sessionId = this.toolCallSessionIndex.get(toolCallId);
478. 		if (!sessionId) {

480. 		}
481. 
482. 		const entryRef = this.findToolCallEntryRef(sessionId, toolCallId);
483. 		if (entryRef && isToolCallEntry(entryRef.entry)) {

486.

488. 		if (!parentInfo || parentInfo.sessionId !== sessionId) {

1. /**
2.  * Tool Call Manager - Manages tool call CRUD, child-parent reconciliation,
3.  * and canonical progressive tool state.
4.  *
5.  * Extracted from SessionEntryStore to isolate tool call concerns.
6.  * All dependencies are injected via interfaces for testability.
7.  *
8.  * Note: This file uses native Map/Set for internal indexes that are NOT reactive.
9.  * Progressive tool arguments live on the tool entry itself; indexes only help locate it.
10.  */
11. 
12. import { ok, type Result } from "neverthrow";
13. 
14. import type {
15. 	ContentBlock,
16. 	ToolArguments,
17. 	ToolCallData,
18. 	ToolCallStatus,
19. } from "../../../services/converted-session-types.js";
20. import type { AppError } from "../../errors/app-error.js";
21. import type { ToolCall, ToolCallUpdate } from "../../types/tool-call.js";
22. import { createLogger } from "../../utils/logger.js";
23. import { OperationStore } from "../operation-store.svelte.js";
24. import type { SessionEntry } from "../types.js";
25. import { isToolCallEntry } from "../types.js";
26. import type { IEntryIndex } from "./interfaces/entry-index.js";
27. import type { IEntryStoreInternal } from "./interfaces/entry-store-internal.js";
28. import type { IToolCallManager } from "./interfaces/tool-call-manager-interface.js";
29. 
30. const logger = createLogger({ id: "tool-call-manager", name: "ToolCallManager" });
31. 
32. /**
33.  * Determine if a tool call entry should be marked as streaming based on its status.
34.  * Tool calls are streaming when pending or in progress, not streaming when completed or failed.
35.  */
36. export function isToolCallStreaming(status: ToolCallStatus | null | undefined): boolean {
37. 	return status === "pending" || status === "in_progress";
38. }
39. 
40. function isTerminalStatus(status: ToolCallStatus | null | undefined): boolean {
41. 	return status === "completed" || status === "failed";
42. }
43. 
44. function nowMs(): number {
45. 	return Date.now();
46. }
47. 
48. /**
49.  * Prevent status regressions when replayed historical events arrive out-of-order.
50.  * A terminal tool call should never be downgraded back to pending/in_progress.
51.  */
52. function resolveNextStatus(
53. 	currentStatus: ToolCallStatus | null | undefined,
54. 	nextStatus: ToolCallStatus | null | undefined
55. ): ToolCallStatus | null | undefined {
56. 	if (!nextStatus) {
57. 		return currentStatus;
58. 	}
59. 
60. 	if (isTerminalStatus(currentStatus) && !isTerminalStatus(nextStatus)) {
61. 		return currentStatus;
62. 	}
63. 
64. 	return nextStatus;
65. }
66. 
67. /**
68.  * Extract result text from content blocks.
69.  */
70. export function extractResultFromContent(
71. 	content: ContentBlock[] | null | undefined
72. ): string | null {
73. 	if (!content || !Array.isArray(content) || content.length === 0) {
74. 		return null;
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	const textParts: string[] = [];
78. 	for (const block of content) {
79. 		if (block && typeof block === "object") {
80. 			const anyBlock = block as Record<string, unknown>;
81. 			if (anyBlock.type === "content" && anyBlock.content) {
82. 				const innerContent = anyBlock.content as Record<string, unknown>;
83. 				if (innerContent.type === "text" && typeof innerContent.text === "string") {
84. 					textParts.push(innerContent.text);
85. 				}
86. 			} else if (block.type === "text" && "text" in block) {
87. 				textParts.push(block.text);
88. 			}
89. 		}
90. 	}
91. 
92. 	return textParts.length > 0 ? textParts.join("\n") : null;
93. }
94. 
95. function mergeEditEntries(
96. 	currentEdits: Extract<ToolArguments, { kind: "edit" }>["edits"],
97. 	incomingEdits: Extract<ToolArguments, { kind: "edit" }>["edits"]
98. ): Extract<ToolArguments, { kind: "edit" }>["edits"] {
99. 	const maxLength = Math.max(currentEdits.length, incomingEdits.length);
100. 	const merged: Extract<ToolArguments, { kind: "edit" }>["edits"] = [];
101. 
102. 	for (let index = 0; index < maxLength; index += 1) {
103. 		const currentEdit = currentEdits[index];
104. 		const incomingEdit = incomingEdits[index];
105. 
106. 		if (currentEdit && incomingEdit) {
107. 			merged.push({
108. 				filePath: incomingEdit.filePath ?? currentEdit.filePath,
109. 				moveFrom: incomingEdit.moveFrom ?? currentEdit.moveFrom,
110. 				oldString: incomingEdit.oldString ?? currentEdit.oldString,
111. 				newString: incomingEdit.newString ?? currentEdit.newString,
112. 				content: incomingEdit.content ?? currentEdit.content,
113. 			});
114. 			continue;
115. 		}
116. 
117. 		if (incomingEdit) {
118. 			merged.push(incomingEdit);
119. 			continue;
120. 		}
```

385. fn deserialize_agent_context<E>() -> Result<AgentType, E>
386. where
387.     E: serde::de::Error,
388. {
389.     current_agent().ok_or_else(|| {
390.         serde::de::Error::custom("Missing agent context for ToolCallData deserialization")
391.     })
392. }

394. /// Tool call update data.
395. #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize, Type)]
396. #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
397. pub struct ToolCallUpdateData {
398.     pub tool_call_id: String,
399.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
400.     pub status: Option<ToolCallStatus>,
401.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
402.     pub result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
403.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
404.     pub content: Option<Vec<crate::acp::types::ContentBlock>>,
405.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
406.     pub raw_output: Option<serde_json::Value>,
407.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
408.     pub title: Option<String>,
409.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
410.     pub locations: Option<Vec<ToolCallLocation>>,
411.     /// Streaming input delta - partial JSON string for progressive tool input display.
412.     /// Extracted from _meta.claudeCode.streamingInputDelta.
413.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
414.     pub streaming_input_delta: Option<String>,
415. 
416.     /// Progressive normalized todos from streaming input.
417.     /// Populated by the streaming accumulator for TodoWrite-like tools.
418.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
419.     pub normalized_todos: Option<Vec<TodoItem>>,
420. 
421.     /// Progressive normalized questions from streaming input.
422.     /// Populated by the streaming accumulator for AskUserQuestion-like tools.
423.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
424.     pub normalized_questions: Option<Vec<QuestionItem>>,
425. 
426.     /// Pre-parsed streaming arguments for progressive tool input display.
427.     /// Produced by the streaming accumulator when partial JSON parse succeeds.
428.     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
429.     pub streaming_arguments: Option<ToolArguments>,
430.
```

136. pub(crate) fn parse_tool_call_update(
137.     data: &serde_json::Value,
138.     normalize_tool_kind: fn(&str) -> ToolKind,
139. ) -> Result<RawToolCallUpdateInput, ParseError> {
140.     let id = data

144.         .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::MissingField("toolCallId".to_string()))?;
145. 
146.     let status = data
147.         .get("status")
148.         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
149.         .map(tool_call_status_from_str);
150. 
151.     let result = data
152.         .get("rawOutput")
153.         .cloned()
154.         .or_else(|| {
155.             data.get("_meta")
156.                 .and_then(|m| m.get("claudeCode"))
157.                 .and_then(|c| c.get("toolResponse"))
158.                 .cloned()
159.         })
160.         .or_else(|| data.get("result").cloned());
161. 
162.     let content = data.get("content").cloned();
163.     let title = data
164.         .get("title")
165.         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
166.         .map(|s| s.to_string());
167.     let locations = data.get("locations").cloned();
168.     let streaming_input_delta = data
169.         .get("_meta")
170.         .and_then(|m| m.get("claudeCode"))
171.         .and_then(|c| c.get("streamingInputDelta"))
172.         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
173.         .map(|s| s.to_string());
174.     let tool_name = data
175.         .get("_meta")
176.         .and_then(|m| m.get("claudeCode"))
177.         .and_then(|c| c.get("toolName"))
178.         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
179.         .map(|s| s.to_string());
180.     let raw_input = data.get("rawInput").cloned().filter(|v| !v.is_null());
181.     let kind = data
182.         .get("kind")
183.         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
184.         .map(normalize_tool_kind);
185. 
186.     Ok(RawToolCallUpdateInput {
187.         id,
188.         status,
189.         result,
190.         content,
```

486.     pub(crate) fn parse_tool_call_update_impl(
487.         &self,
488.         data: &serde_json::Value,
489.     ) -> Result<RawToolCallUpdateInput, ParseError> {
490.         let id = data
491.             .get("toolCallId")
492.             .or_else(|| data.get("tool_use_id"))
493.             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
494.             .map(|s| s.to_string())
495.             .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::MissingField("toolCallId or tool_use_id".to_string()))?;
496. 
497.         let result = data
498.             .get("rawOutput")
499.             .or_else(|| data.get("raw_output"))
500.             .or_else(|| data.get("output"))
501.             .or_else(|| data.get("content"))
502.             .cloned();
503. 
504.         // Resolve explicit status directly to ToolCallStatus, skipping the
505.         // intermediate String allocation that normalize_status would produce.
506.         let explicit_status = data.get("status").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).and_then(|s| {
507.             let parsed = tool_call_status_from_str(s);
508.             // tool_call_status_from_str returns Pending for unrecognised input.
509.             // Distinguish genuine "pending" from unknown values.
510.             if parsed != ToolCallStatus::Pending || s.trim().eq_ignore_ascii_case("pending") {
511.                 Some(parsed)
512.             } else {
513.                 None
514.             }
515.         });
516.         let inferred_status = status_utils::infer_status_from_result(result.as_ref());
517.         let status = match (explicit_status, inferred_status.as_deref()) {
518.             (_, Some("failed")) => ToolCallStatus::Failed,
519.             (Some(s), _) => s,
520.             (None, Some(s)) => tool_call_status_from_str(s),
521.             (None, None) => ToolCallStatus::Pending,
522.         };
523. 
524.         let title = data
525.             .get("title")
526.             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
527.             .map(|s| s.to_string());
528. 
529.         let mut raw_input = data
530.             .get("rawInput")
531.             .or_else(|| data.get("raw_input"))
532.             .or_else(|| data.get("input"))
533.             .cloned()
534.             .filter(|v| !v.is_null())
535.             .map(|value| Self::merge_outer_arguments(&value));
536. 
537.         let explicit_name = data.get("name").and_then(|value| value.as_str());
538.         let payload_kind = kind_utils::infer_kind_from_payload(
539.             &id,
540.             title.as_deref(),
```

<sub>⏱️ 228m 50s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I’ve confirmed the execute card itself is capable of rendering output. The stronger hypothesis now is: the “tests” case is coming through a different tool path—likely task/task-child output—not the plain execute path, so the command survives but stdout/stderr lands somewhere that the UI never surfaces.

1. import type { AgentToolEntry } from "@acepe/ui/agent-panel";
2. import type { TurnState } from "../../../../store/types.js";
3. import type { ToolCall } from "../../../../types/tool-call.js";
4. import { resolveFullToolEntry } from "../../tool-definition-registry.js";
5. 
6. /**
7.  * Convert taskChildren (ToolCall[]) into AnyAgentEntry[] for the AgentToolTask UI.
8.  */
9. export function convertTaskChildren(
10. 	children: ToolCall[] | null | undefined,
11. 	turnState?: TurnState,
12. 	parentCompleted: boolean = false
13. ): AgentToolEntry[] {
14. 	if (!children || children.length === 0) return [];
15. 	return children.map((child) =>
16. 		resolveFullToolEntry({
17. 			toolCall: child,
18. 			turnState,
19. 			parentCompleted,
20. 		})
21. 	);
22. }
23.
```

</details>

```
1. <script lang="ts">
2. import { AgentToolTask } from "@acepe/ui/agent-panel";
3. import * as m from "$lib/paraglide/messages.js";
4. import type { TurnState } from "../../store/types.js";
5. import type { ToolCall } from "../../types/tool-call.js";
6. import { getToolStatus } from "../../utils/tool-state-utils.js";
7. 
8. interface Props {
9. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
10. 	turnState?: TurnState;
11. 	projectPath?: string;
12. 	elapsedLabel?: string | null;
13. }
14. 
15. let { toolCall, turnState, elapsedLabel }: Props = $props();
16. 
17. const toolStatus = $derived(getToolStatus(toolCall, turnState));
18. 
19. // Extract task_id from the TaskOutput arguments
20. const taskId = $derived.by(() => {
21. 	if (toolCall.arguments.kind === "taskOutput") {
22. 		return toolCall.arguments.task_id ?? null;
23. 	}
24. 	return null;
25. });
26. 
27. // Build a description from the task_id
28. const description = $derived.by(() => {
29. 	if (taskId) return `Task: ${taskId}`;
30. 	return toolStatus.isPending
31. 		? m.tool_task_output_running()
32. 		: m.tool_task_output_completed();
33. });
34. 
35. // Get the result as a string
36. const resultText = $derived.by(() => {
37. 	if (!toolCall.result) return null;
38. 	if (typeof toolCall.result === "string") return toolCall.result;
39. 	return null;
40. });
41. 
42. // Map tool status to AgentToolStatus
43. const agentStatus = $derived.by(() => {
44. 	if (toolStatus.isPending) return "running" as const;
45. 	if (toolStatus.isError) return "error" as const;
46. 	return "done" as const;
47. });
48. </script>
49. 
50. <AgentToolTask
51. 	{description}
52. 	prompt={null}
53. 	{resultText}
54. 	children={[]}
55. 	status={agentStatus}
56. 	iconBasePath="/svgs/icons"
57. 	durationLabel={elapsedLabel ?? undefined}
58. 	runningFallback={m.tool_task_output_running()}
59. 	doneFallback={m.tool_task_output_completed()}
60. 	resultLabel={m.tool_task_output_completed()}
61. />
62.
```

</details>

```
1. <script lang="ts">
2. import { AgentToolTask } from "@acepe/ui/agent-panel";
3. import * as m from "$lib/paraglide/messages.js";
4. import { getSessionStore } from "../../store/index.js";
5. import type { TurnState } from "../../store/types.js";
6. import type { ToolCall } from "../../types/tool-call.js";
7. import { getToolStatus } from "../../utils/tool-state-utils.js";
8. import { convertTaskChildren } from "./tool-call-task/logic/convert-task-children.js";
9. import { resolveTaskSubagent } from "./tool-call-task/logic/resolve-task-subagent.js";
10. 
11. interface Props {
12. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
13. 	turnState?: TurnState;
14. 	projectPath?: string;
15. 	elapsedLabel?: string | null;
16. }
17. 
18. let { toolCall, turnState, elapsedLabel }: Props = $props();
19. 
20. const sessionStore = getSessionStore();
21. const toolStatus = $derived(getToolStatus(toolCall, turnState));
22. 
23. // Extract subagent data
24. const subagent = $derived.by(() => {
25. 	return resolveTaskSubagent(toolCall, sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id));
26. });
27. 
28. // Get the result as a string
29. const resultText = $derived.by(() => {
30. 	if (!toolCall.result) return null;
31. 	if (typeof toolCall.result === "string") return toolCall.result;
32. 	return null;
33. });
34. 
35. // Map tool status to AgentToolStatus
36. const agentStatus = $derived.by(() => {
37. 	if (toolStatus.isPending) return "running" as const;
38. 	if (toolStatus.isError) return "error" as const;
39. 	return "done" as const;
40. });
41. 
42. // Convert task children to presentational entries
43. const children = $derived(convertTaskChildren(toolCall.taskChildren, turnState, toolStatus.isSuccess));
44. </script>
45. 
46. <AgentToolTask
47. 	description={subagent?.description ?? null}
48. 	prompt={subagent?.prompt ?? null}
49. 	{resultText}
50. 	{children}
51. 	status={agentStatus}
52. 	showDoneIcon={toolStatus.isSuccess}
53. 	iconBasePath="/svgs/icons"
54. 	durationLabel={elapsedLabel ?? undefined}
55. 	runningFallback={m.tool_task_running_fallback()}
56. 	doneFallback={m.tool_task_fallback()}
57. 	resultLabel={m.tool_task_result_label()}
58. />
59.
```

</details>

430. fn apply_update_to_tool_call(tool_call: &mut ToolCallData, update: &ToolCallUpdateData) {
431.     if let Some(status) = &update.status {
432.         // Prevent status regression: terminal states should never downgrade.
433.         // Mirrors frontend's resolveNextStatus() pattern.
434.         if !is_terminal_status(&tool_call.status) || is_terminal_status(status) {
435.             tool_call.status = status.clone();
436.         }
437.     }
438.     if let Some(result) = &update.result {
439.         tool_call.result = Some(result.clone());
440.     }
441.     if let Some(title) = &update.title {
442.         tool_call.title = Some(title.clone());
443.     }
444.     if let Some(locations) = &update.locations {
445.         tool_call.locations = Some(locations.clone());
446.     }
447.     if let Some(arguments) = &update.arguments {
448.         tool_call.arguments = merge_tool_arguments(tool_call.arguments.clone(), arguments.clone());
449.     }
450.     // Note: raw_output and content are not applied to ToolCallData
451.     // as they have different structures
452. }
453. 
454. fn tool_call_to_update(tool_call: &ToolCallData) -> ToolCallUpdateData {
455.     ToolCallUpdateData {
456.         tool_call_id: tool_call.id.clone(),
457.         status: Some(tool_call.status.clone()),
458.         result: tool_call.result.clone(),
459.         content: None,
460.         raw_output: None,
461.         title: tool_call.title.clone(),
462.         locations: tool_call.locations.clone(),
463.         streaming_input_delta: None,
464.         normalized_todos: tool_call.normalized_todos.clone(),
465.         normalized_questions: tool_call.normalized_questions.clone(),
466.         streaming_arguments: None,
467.         streaming_plan: None,
468.         arguments: Some(tool_call.arguments.clone()),
469.         failure_reason: None,
470.     }
471. }
472. 
473. fn merge_tool_call(current: ToolCallData, incoming: ToolCallData) -> ToolCallData {
474.     let next_plan_approval_request_id = if incoming.awaiting_plan_approval {
475.         incoming
476.             .plan_approval_request_id
477.             .or(current.plan_approval_request_id)
478.     } else {
479.         None
480.     };
```

</details>

```
230. 
231.     let resolved_kind = classified_arguments
232.         .as_ref()
233.         .map(|classified| classified.kind)
234.         .or_else(|| {
235.             Some(
236.                 resolve_raw_tool_identity(
237.                     parser,
238.                     tool_call_id,
239.                     raw.raw_input.as_ref(),
240.                     identity_hints,
241.                 )
242.                 .kind,
243.             )
244.         });
245. 
246.     let result = normalize_web_search_result(result, resolved_kind.as_ref());
247. 
248.     ToolCallUpdateData {
249.         tool_call_id: raw.id,
250.         status,
251.         result,
252.         content,
253.         raw_output: None,
254.         title: raw.title,
255.         locations,
256.         streaming_input_delta,
257.         normalized_todos,
258.         normalized_questions,
259.         streaming_arguments,
260.         streaming_plan: final_streaming_plan,
261.         arguments,
262.         failure_reason: None,
263.     }
264. }
265. 
266. fn normalize_count_only_search_result(
267.     result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
268.     _arguments: Option<&ToolArguments>,
269. ) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
270.     let Some(serde_json::Value::Object(mut object)) = result else {
271.         return result;
272.     };
273.     if object.contains_key("mode")
274.         || object.contains_key("content")
275.         || object.contains_key("filenames")
276.     {
277.         return Some(serde_json::Value::Object(object));
278.     }
279. 
280.     let total_files = object.get("totalFiles").and_then(|value| value.as_u64());
281.     let total_matches = object.get("totalMatches").and_then(|value| value.as_u64());
282.     if total_files.is_none() && total_matches.is_none() {
283.         return Some(serde_json::Value::Object(object));
284.     }
285. 
286.     object.insert(
287.         "mode".to_string(),
288.         serde_json::Value::String("count".to_string()),
289.     );
290.     if let Some(value) = total_files {
291.         object.insert(
292.             "numFiles".to_string(),
293.             serde_json::Value::Number(serde_json::Number::from(value)),
294.         );
295.     }
296.     if let Some(value) = total_matches {
297.         object.insert(
298.             "numMatches".to_string(),
299.             serde_json::Value::Number(serde_json::Number::from(value)),
300.         );
301.     }
302. 
303.     Some(serde_json::Value::Object(object))
304. }
305. 
306. // Web search results from Claude Code arrive as an array of {type, url, title,
307. // encrypted_content, page_age} objects. Reshape into {results: [...], summary: ""}
308. // so the frontend's parseWebSearchResult can render link cards directly.
309. fn normalize_web_search_result(
310.     result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
311.     kind: Option<&ToolKind>,
312. ) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {
313.     if kind != Some(&ToolKind::WebSearch) {
314.         return result;
315.     }
316.     let Some(serde_json::Value::Array(items)) = result else {
317.         return result;
318.     };
319. 
320.     let results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = items
321.         .iter()
322.         .filter_map(|item| {
323.             let url = item.get("url").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;
324.             if !(url.starts_with("http://") || url.starts_with("https://")) {
325.                 return None;
326.             }
327.             let title = item.get("title").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(url);
328.             let page_age = item.get("page_age").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
329.             Some(json!({
330.                 "title": title,
331.                 "url": url,
332.                 "page_age": page_age,
333.             }))
334.         })
335.         .collect();
336. 
337.     if results.is_empty() {
338.         return Some(serde_json::Value::Array(items));
339.     }
340.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 229m 11s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Inspecting UI task rendering**

I need to look into the shared AgentToolTask. It appears that it may display children as compact rows, just showing the title and subtitle without the full stdout or stderr. If the user explains, “for instance like tests, we see the command, but never the output,” that might be because the tests are child tools within a task card. I think it's a reasonable assumption! Now I’ll check the `@acepe/ui` AgentToolTask implementation further.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 229m 13s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found a stronger likely root cause: test runs often show up as **task children**, not standalone execute cards. If that’s true, we’re rendering those children through the task UI’s compact row path, which may preserve the command title but drop the child execute output entirely.

<sub>⏱️ 229m 36s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found the main cause: **the missing output is usually happening on task-child execute calls, not standalone execute cards**. Standalone execute cards already support `stdout`/`stderr`, but the task UI does **not** render child tools in full. It only shows:

1. the **last child** as a one-line `AgentToolRow` (`title/subtitle/filePath` only), and  
2. the **ToolTally** strip.

So when a task child runs something like tests, we keep the command label, but we never mount `AgentToolExecute` for that child, so its output is dropped from the visible UI.

The relevant code path is:

- `packages/desktop/src/lib/acp/components/tool-calls/tool-call-task.svelte` converts `taskChildren`
- `packages/ui/src/components/agent-panel/agent-tool-task.svelte` narrows them to `toolCallChildren`
- then it renders only:
  - `lastToolCall` via `AgentToolRow`
  - `ToolTally`
- and **never renders child `stdout` / `stderr`**, even though those fields exist on `AgentToolEntry`

That’s why you “see the command, but never the output” for cases like tests. The output is available in the child entry model, but the task card’s child presentation is intentionally compact and throws that detail away.

---

<sub>⏱️ 229m 43s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Root cause:** test runs are usually showing up as **task children**, and the task card only renders those children as a **compact row + tally**, not as full execute cards. So the command survives, but the child `stdout`/`stderr` never gets rendered.

The path is:

- `tool-call-task.svelte` passes `taskChildren`
- `agent-tool-task.svelte` filters them to `toolCallChildren`
- then renders only:
  - `lastToolCall` via `AgentToolRow`
  - `ToolTally`

`AgentToolRow` only knows `title`, `subtitle`, and `filePath`. It does **not** render `stdout`/`stderr`. By contrast, standalone execute entries do render output through `AgentToolExecute`.

So this is not primarily an execute-parser bug. It’s a **task child presentation bug**: child execute output exists in the entry model, but the task UI collapses child tools to a summary-only surface.

39. function createChildToolCall(overrides: Partial<ToolCall> & Pick<ToolCall, "id" | "kind" | "status">): ToolCall {
40. 	return {

43. 		kind: overrides.kind,

54. 	};

57. function createTaskToolCall(children: ToolCall[]): ToolCall {

59. 		id: "tool-task-1",

61. 		kind: "task",
62. 		status: "completed",
63. 		title: "Task",

69. 		},

71. 		skillMeta: null,
72. 		result: "Completed successfully",

92. 		displayName: fallbackDisplayName ?? agentId,
93. 		displayOrder: 65535,
94. 		supportsModelDefaults: false,
95. 		variantGroup: "plain",
96. 		defaultAlias: undefined,
97. 		reasoningEffortSupport: false,
98. 		autonomousApplyStrategy: "postConnect",
99. 	};
100. }
101. 
102. export function getProviderMetadataFromModelsDisplay(
103. 	modelsDisplay: ModelsForDisplay | null | undefined,
104. ): ProviderMetadataProjection | null {
105. 	return modelsDisplay?.presentation?.provider ?? null;
106. }
107. 
108. export function normalizeModelsForDisplay(
109. 	agentId: string,
110. 	modelsDisplay: ModelsForDisplay | null | undefined,
111. 	fallbackDisplayName?: string,
112. 	providerMetadataOverride?: ProviderMetadataProjection | null,
113. ): ModelsForDisplay | null {
114. 	if (!modelsDisplay) {
115. 		return null;
116. 	}
117. 
118. 	const providerMetadata = resolveProviderMetadataProjection(
119. 		agentId,
120. 		providerMetadataOverride ?? getProviderMetadataFromModelsDisplay(modelsDisplay),
121. 		fallbackDisplayName,
122. 	);
123. 	const presentation = modelsDisplay.presentation;
124. 	const displayFamily =
125. 		presentation?.displayFamily ??
126. 		(providerMetadata.variantGroup === "reasoningEffort"
127. 			? "codexReasoningEffort"
128. 			: "providerGrouped");
129. 
130. 	return {
131. 		groups: modelsDisplay.groups,
132. 		presentation: {
133. 			displayFamily,
134. 			usageMetrics: presentation?.usageMetrics ?? "spendAndContext",
135. 			provider: providerMetadata,
136. 		},
137. 	};
138. }
139.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 235m 24s</sub>

### ✅ `task_complete`

****Fixed:** task cards now render the last child **execute...**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "summary": "**Fixed:** task cards now render the last child **execute** tool as a real execute card, so commands like test runs show their `stdout`/`stderr` instead of collapsing to a one-line summary row.\n\nI changed `packages/ui/src/components/agent-panel/agent-tool-task.svelte` to detect execute children and mount `AgentToolExecute` with the child command, output, and exit code, while non-execute children still use the compact row. I also added a regression contract test for that behavior and updated the provider metadata shim in `packages/desktop/src/lib/services/acp-provider-metadata.ts` to include the new `preconnectionSlashMode` field so desktop typecheck stays green."
}
```

</details>

```
**Fixed:** task cards now render the last child **execute** tool as a real execute card, so commands like test runs show their `stdout`/`stderr` instead of collapsing to a one-line summary row.

I changed `packages/ui/src/components/agent-panel/agent-tool-task.svelte` to detect execute children and mount `AgentToolExecute` with the child command, output, and exit code, while non-execute children still use the compact row. I also added a regression contract test for that behavior and updated the provider metadata shim in `packages/desktop/src/lib/services/acp-provider-metadata.ts` to include the new `preconnectionSlashMode` field so desktop typecheck stays green.
```


---

<sub>⏱️ 235m 24s</sub>

### ✓ Task Complete

**Fixed:** task cards now render the last child **execute** tool as a real execute card, so commands like test runs show their `stdout`/`stderr` instead of collapsing to a one-line summary row.

I changed `packages/ui/src/components/agent-panel/agent-tool-task.svelte` to detect execute children and mount `AgentToolExecute` with the child command, output, and exit code, while non-execute children still use the compact row. I also added a regression contract test for that behavior and updated the provider metadata shim in `packages/desktop/src/lib/services/acp-provider-metadata.ts` to include the new `preconnectionSlashMode` field so desktop typecheck stays green.

---

1. <script lang="ts">
2. import { safeJsonStringify } from "../../../../logic/json-utils.js";
3. import { stripAnsiCodes } from "../../../../utils/ansi-utils.js";
4. import { parseToolResultOutput } from "../logic/parse-tool-result.js";
5. 
6. /**
7.  * Strip the "Exited with code X.Final output:\n\n" or "New output:\n\n" prefix
8.  * that the ACP server adds to bash tool results.
9.  */
10. function stripOutputPrefix(text: string): string {
11. 	// Match patterns like "Exited with code 0.Final output:\n\n" or "New output:\n\n"
12. 	return text.replace(
13. 		/^(Exited with code \d+\.(Signal `[^`]+`\. )?Final output:\n\n|New output:\n\n)/s,
14. 		""
15. 	);
16. }
17. 
18. interface ExecuteToolContentProps {
19. 	/**
20. 	 * The command that was executed.
21. 	 */
22. 	command: string | null;
23. 
24. 	/**
25. 	 * Stdout output from command execution.
26. 	 */
27. 	stdout: string | null;
28. 
29. 	/**
30. 	 * Stderr output from command execution.
31. 	 */
32. 	stderr: string | null;
33. 
34. 	/**
35. 	 * Exit code from command execution.
36. 	 */
37. 	exitCode?: number;
38. 
39. 	/**
40. 	 * Raw result from tool call. Validated at use via parseToolResultOutput (Zod ToolResultOutputSchema).
41. 	 */
42. 	result: unknown;
43. 
44. 	/**
45. 	 * Whether the content is expanded.
46. 	 */
47. 	isExpanded?: boolean;
48. 
49. 	/**
50. 	 * Callback when content is clicked (for click-to-expand).
51. 	 */
52. 	onClickExpand?: () => void;
53. }
54. 
55. let {
56. 	command,
57. 	stdout,
58. 	stderr,
59. 	exitCode,
60. 	result,
61. 	isExpanded = false,
62. 	onClickExpand,
63. }: ExecuteToolContentProps = $props();
64. 
65. // Get the stdout to display
66. const displayStdout = $derived.by(() => {
67. 	if (stdout) {
68. 		return stripOutputPrefix(stripAnsiCodes(stdout));
69. 	}
70. 
71. 	// Try to extract and parse output from result using shared parsing function
72. 	const parseResult = parseToolResultOutput(result);
73. 	if (parseResult.isOk() && parseResult.value) {
74. 		return stripOutputPrefix(stripAnsiCodes(parseResult.value));
75. 	}
76. 
77. 	// Fallback to JSON stringify of entire result
78. 	if (result) {
79. 		const stringifyResult = safeJsonStringify(result);
80. 		if (stringifyResult.isOk()) {
81. 			return stripOutputPrefix(stringifyResult.value);
82. 		}
83. 		return stripOutputPrefix(String(result));
84. 	}
85. 
86. 	return null;
87. });
88. 
89. const displayStderr = $derived(stderr ? stripAnsiCodes(stderr) : null);
90. 
91. const hasOutput = $derived(displayStdout || displayStderr);
92. 
93. // Determine stderr color based on exit code (like 1code)
94. // exitCode 0 = warning (amber), non-zero = error (rose)
95. const stderrColorClass = $derived(
96. 	exitCode === 0 || exitCode === undefined
97. 		? "text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400"
98. 		: "text-rose-500 dark:text-rose-400"
99. );
100. 
101. let contentEl: HTMLDivElement | undefined = $state();
102. 
103. // When collapsed, scroll to bottom so end of output (e.g. test summary) is visible.
104. // overflow: hidden still allows programmatic scrollTop changes.
105. $effect(() => {
106. 	const _stdout = displayStdout;
107. 	const _stderr = displayStderr;
108. 	if (contentEl && !isExpanded) {
109. 		contentEl.scrollTop = contentEl.scrollHeight;
110. 	}
111. });
112. </script>
113. 
114. <!-- Content - always visible, clickable to expand (only when collapsed) (1code style) -->
115. <!-- svelte-ignore a11y_click_events_have_key_events -->
116. <!-- svelte-ignore a11y_no_static_element_interactions -->
117. <div
118. 	onclick={() => {
119. 		if (hasOutput && !isExpanded) onClickExpand?.();
120. 	}}
121. 	data-scrollable={isExpanded ? "" : undefined}
122. 	class="border-t border-border px-2.5 py-1.5 transition-colors duration-150 {isExpanded
123. 		? 'max-h-[200px] overflow-y-auto'
124. 		: 'flex flex-col justify-end max-h-[72px] overflow-hidden'} {hasOutput && !isExpanded
125. 		? 'cursor-pointer hover:bg-muted/50'
126. 		: ''}"
127. >
128. 	<!-- Command -->
129. 	{#if command}
130. 		<div class="font-mono text-xs">
131. 			<span class="text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400">$ </span>
132. 			<span class="whitespace-pre-wrap break-all text-foreground">{command}</span>
133. 		</div>
134. 	{/if}
135. 
136. 	<!-- Stdout -->
137. 	{#if displayStdout}
138. 		<div class="execute-output mt-1.5 font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground">
139. 			<pre class="whitespace-pre-wrap break-all">{displayStdout}</pre>
140. 		</div>
141. 	{/if}
142. 
143. 	<!-- Stderr -->
144. 	{#if displayStderr}
145. 		<div class="execute-output mt-1.5 font-mono text-xs {stderrColorClass}">
146. 			<pre class="whitespace-pre-wrap break-all">{displayStderr}</pre>
147. 		</div>
148. 	{/if}
149. </div>
150. 
151. <style>
152. 	/* Execute output styling - preserve monospace and whitespace for command output */
153. 	.execute-output {
154. 		overflow-wrap: break-word;
155. 		word-break: break-word;
156. 
157. 		& pre {
158. 			margin: 0;
159. 			font-family: inherit;
160. 			font-size: inherit;
161. 		}
162. 	}
163. </style>
164.
```

<details>
<summary>67 lines</summary>

```
1. import { z } from "zod";
2. 
3. /**
4.  * Zod schemas for parsing tool result output.
5.  *
6.  * Handles various formats of tool execution results:
7.  * - Plain string output
8.  * - Objects with output/stdout/stderr fields
9.  * - MCP content block arrays: [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]
10.  *
11.  * Note: JSON-stringified values are handled in the parsing logic function,
12.  * not in the schema transforms, to avoid try-catch blocks.
13.  */
14. 
15. /**
16.  * Schema for MCP content block format.
17.  * Handles arrays like [{"type": "text", "text": "..."}]
18.  */
19. const McpContentBlockSchema = z
20. 	.array(
21. 		z.object({
22. 			type: z.string(),
23. 			text: z.string().optional(),
24. 		})
25. 	)
26. 	.transform((blocks) => {
27. 		// Extract text from all text blocks and join them
28. 		const textParts = blocks
29. 			.filter((block) => block.type === "text" && block.text)
30. 			.map((block) => block.text);
31. 		return textParts.length > 0 ? textParts.join("\n") : null;
32. 	});
33. 
34. /**
35.  * Schema for object results with output fields.
36.  * Extracts output from output/stdout/stderr fields in priority order.
37.  */
38. const ObjectResultSchema = z
39. 	.object({
40. 		output: z.string().optional(),
41. 		stdout: z.string().optional(),
42. 		stderr: z.string().optional(),
43. 	})
44. 	.transform((obj) => {
45. 		return obj.output ?? obj.stdout ?? obj.stderr ?? null;
46. 	});
47. 
48. /**
49.  * Schema for plain string results.
50.  */
51. const StringResultSchema = z.string();
52. 
53. /**
54.  * Union schema that handles all tool result output variations.
55.  * Tries MCP content block array first, then object schema, then string schema.
56.  */
57. export const ToolResultOutputSchema = z.union([
58. 	McpContentBlockSchema,
59. 	ObjectResultSchema,
60. 	StringResultSchema,
61. ]);
62. 
63. /**
64.  * Type inferred from the schema.
65.  */
66. export type ToolResultOutput = z.infer<typeof ToolResultOutputSchema>;
67.
```

</details>

1. import { describe, expect, it } from "bun:test";

5. describe("parseToolResultOutput", () => {
6. 	it("should return plain string result", () => {
7. 		const result = parseToolResultOutput("Hello, world!");
8. 
9. 		expect(result.isOk()).toBe(true);
10. 		if (result.isOk()) {
11. 			expect(result.value).toBe("Hello, world!");
12. 		}
13. 	});
14. 
15. 	it("should parse JSON-stringified string result", () => {
16. 		const jsonString = JSON.stringify("Parsed string");
17. 		const result = parseToolResultOutput(jsonString);
18. 
19. 		expect(result.isOk()).toBe(true);
20. 		if (result.isOk()) {
21. 			expect(result.value).toBe("Parsed string");
22. 		}
23. 	});
24. 
25. 	it("should extract output from object with output field", () => {
26. 		const result = parseToolResultOutput({ output: "Command output" });
27. 
28. 		expect(result.isOk()).toBe(true);

30. 			expect(result.value).toBe("Command output");
31. 		}
32. 	});
33.

35. 		const result = parseToolResultOutput({ stdout: "Standard output" });
36.

39. 			expect(result.value).toBe("Standard output");

### 💭 Reasoning

***Inspecting routing issues**

I think the root cause might be related to routing. I need to check if the tool-call-execute or components like execute-tool-ui.svelte are being used correctly. Let's get this sorted out.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 238m 52s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I found a likely real bug this time: the live execute route is using the **thin shared execute card**, which only gets `parseToolResultWithExitCode(...)`. There’s also a richer execute UI in the codebase that can fall back to `parseToolResultOutput(...)`, but it isn’t the one currently wired into the route. So if the result isn’t a simple `{ stdout/stderr/output }` shape, you get the command and lose the visible output.

---

1. <script lang="ts">
2. import { AgentToolExecute } from "@acepe/ui/agent-panel";
3. import * as m from "$lib/paraglide/messages.js";
4. import { getSessionStore } from "../../store/index.js";
5. import type { TurnState } from "../../store/types.js";
6. import type { ToolCall } from "../../types/tool-call.js";
7. import { stripAnsiCodes } from "../../utils/ansi-utils.js";
8. import { getToolStatus } from "../../utils/tool-state-utils.js";
9. import { parseToolResultWithExitCode } from "./tool-call-execute/logic/parse-tool-result.js";
10. import { resolveExecuteCommand } from "./tool-call-execute/logic/resolve-execute-command.js";
11. 
12. interface ToolCallExecuteProps {
13. 	/**
14. 	 * The tool call to display.
15. 	 */
16. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
17. 	/**
18. 	 * Turn state for dynamic UI updates.
19. 	 */
20. 	turnState?: TurnState;
21. 	/**
22. 	 * Project path for opening files in panels.
23. 	 */
24. 	projectPath?: string;
25. 	elapsedLabel?: string | null;
26. }
27. 
28. let { toolCall, turnState, elapsedLabel }: ToolCallExecuteProps = $props();
29. 
30. const sessionStore = getSessionStore();
31. const toolStatus = $derived(getToolStatus(toolCall, turnState));
32. 
33. // Extract command: streaming args → typed arguments → backtick title
34. const extractedCommand = $derived(
35. 	resolveExecuteCommand(
36. 		sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id),
37. 		toolCall.arguments,
38. 		toolCall.title
39. 	)
40. );
41. 
42. // Parse result with stdout, stderr, and exit code
43. const parsedResult = $derived(parseToolResultWithExitCode(toolCall.result));
44. 
45. // Map tool status to AgentToolStatus
46. const agentStatus = $derived.by(() => {
47. 	if (toolStatus.isPending) return "running" as const;
48. 	if (toolStatus.isError) return "error" as const;
49. 	return "done" as const;
50. });
51. </script>
52. 
53. <AgentToolExecute
54. 	command={extractedCommand}
55. 	stdout={parsedResult.stdout ? stripAnsiCodes(parsedResult.stdout) : null}
56. 	stderr={parsedResult.stderr ? stripAnsiCodes(parsedResult.stderr) : null}
57. 	exitCode={parsedResult.exitCode}
58. 	status={agentStatus}
59. 	durationLabel={elapsedLabel ?? undefined}
60. 	runningNoCmdLabel={m.tool_bash_running_no_cmd()}
61. 	runningLabel={m.tool_bash_running_label()}
62. 	doneLabel={m.tool_bash_completed_label()}
63. 	successLabel={m.tool_bash_success()}
64. 	failedLabel={m.tool_bash_failed()}
65. 	ariaCollapseOutput={m.aria_collapse_output()}
66. 	ariaExpandOutput={m.aria_expand_output()}
67. />
68.
```

11. function createToolCall(overrides?: Partial<ToolCall>): ToolCall {
12. 	const base: ToolCall = {
13. 		id: "tool-1",
14. 		name: "Bash",
15. 		kind: "execute",
16. 		arguments: { kind: "execute", command: "git status --short" },
17. 		status: "in_progress",
18. 		result: null,

30. 		completedAtMs: undefined,
31. 	};

34. 		return base;
35. 	}
36.

12. interface ExecuteToolHeaderProps {
13. 	/** Tool call status */
14. 	status: ToolCallStatus;
15. 	/** Comprehensive tool status result */
16. 	toolStatus?: ToolStatusResult;
17. 	/** The command to display */
18. 	command: string | null;
19. 	/** Whether we have output to show */
20. 	hasOutput?: boolean;
21. 	/** Exit code from command execution */
22. 	exitCode?: number;
23. 	/** Whether the content view is collapsed */
24. 	isExpanded?: boolean;
25. 	/** Callback to toggle collapse state */
26. 	onToggleExpand?: () => void;
27. }
28. 
29. let {
30. 	status,
31. 	toolStatus,
32. 	command,
33. 	hasOutput = false,
34. 	exitCode,
35. 	isExpanded = false,
36. 	onToggleExpand,
37. }: ExecuteToolHeaderProps = $props();
38. 
39. // Create a fallback toolStatus if not provided
40. const effectiveToolStatus = $derived<ToolStatusResult>(
41. 	toolStatus ?? {
42. 		isPending: status === "in_progress" || status === "pending",
43. 		isInputStreaming: false,
44. 		isInterrupted: false,
45. 		isSuccess: status === "completed",
46. 		isError: status === "failed",
47. 	}
48. );
49. 
50. // For bash tools, success/error is determined by exitCode, not by state
51. // exitCode 0 = success, anything else (or undefined if no output yet) = error
52. const isSuccess = $derived(exitCode === 0);
53. const isError = $derived(exitCode !== undefined && exitCode !== 0);
54. 
55. // Extract command summary - first word of each command in a pipeline (like 1code)
56. function extractCommandSummary(cmd: string): string {
57. 	// Normalize line continuations (backslash + newline) into single line
58. 	const normalizedCommand = cmd.replace(/\\\s*\n\s*/g, " ");
59. 	const parts = normalizedCommand.split(/\s*(?:&&|\|\||;|\|)\s*/);
60. 	const firstWords = parts.map((p) => p.trim().split(/\s+/)[0]).filter(Boolean);
61. 	// Limit to first 4 commands to keep it concise
62. 	const limited = firstWords.slice(0, 4);
63. 	if (firstWords.length > 4) {
64. 		return `${limited.join(", ")}...`;
65. 	}
66. 	return limited.join(", ");
67. }
68. 
69. const commandSummary = $derived(command ? extractCommandSummary(command) : "");
70. </script>

7. import type { TurnState } from "../../../../store/types.js";
8. import type { ToolCall } from "../../../../types/tool-call.js";
9. import { stripAnsiCodes } from "../../../../utils/ansi-utils.js";
10. import { getToolStatus } from "../../../../utils/tool-state-utils.js";
11. import ToolContentModal from "../../tool-content-modal.svelte";
12. import { parseToolResultOutput, parseToolResultWithExitCode } from "../logic/parse-tool-result.js";
13. import { resolveExecuteCommand } from "../logic/resolve-execute-command.js";
14. import { ExecuteToolUIState } from "../state/execute-tool-ui-state.svelte.js";

16. import ExecuteToolHeader from "./execute-tool-header.svelte";
17. 
18. interface ExecuteToolUIProps {
19. 	/**
20. 	 * The tool call to display.
21. 	 */
22. 	toolCall: ToolCall;
23. 	/**
24. 	 * Turn state for dynamic UI updates.
25. 	 */
26. 	turnState?: TurnState;
27. 	/**
28. 	 * Project path for opening files in panels.
29. 	 */
30. 	projectPath?: string;
31. }
32. 
33. let { toolCall, turnState }: ExecuteToolUIProps = $props();
34. 
35. // Get comprehensive tool status (includes interrupt detection)
36. const toolStatus = $derived(getToolStatus(toolCall, turnState));
37. 
38. const sessionStore = getSessionStore();
39. 
40. // Use state class for UI state management
41. const uiState = new ExecuteToolUIState();
42. 
43. // Extract command: streaming args → typed arguments → backtick title
44. const extractedCommand = $derived(
45. 	resolveExecuteCommand(
46. 		sessionStore.getStreamingArguments(toolCall.id),
47. 		toolCall.arguments,
48. 		toolCall.title
49. 	)
50. );
51. 
52. // Parse result with stdout, stderr, and exit code (like 1code)
53. const parsedResult = $derived.by(() => {
54. 	return parseToolResultWithExitCode(toolCall.result);
55. });
56. 
57. const stdout = $derived(parsedResult.stdout);
58. const stderr = $derived(parsedResult.stderr);
59. const exitCode = $derived(parsedResult.exitCode);
60. 
61. // Determine if we have any output
62. const hasOutput = $derived(stdout !== null || stderr !== null);
63. 
64. // Show content when we have a command to display (even while running) or output (after completion)
65. const hasContent = $derived(extractedCommand !== null || hasOutput);
66. 
67. function handleToggleExpand() {
68. 	uiState.toggleCollapse();
69. }
70. 
71. function handleClickExpand() {
72. 	if (uiState.isCollapsed) {
73. 		uiState.toggleCollapse();
74. 	}
75. }
76. 
77. function handleCloseModal() {
78. 	uiState.closeModal();
79. }
80. 
81. const modalDisplayOutput = $derived.by(() => {
82. 	// Combine stdout and stderr for modal display
83. 	let output = "";
84. 	if (stdout) {
85. 		output += stripAnsiCodes(stdout);
86. 	}
87. 	if (stderr) {
88. 		if (output) output += "\n";
89. 		output += stripAnsiCodes(stderr);
90. 	}
91. 	if (output) return output;
92. 
93. 	const parseResult = parseToolResultOutput(toolCall.result);
94. 	if (parseResult.isOk() && parseResult.value) {
95. 		return stripAnsiCodes(parseResult.value);
96. 	}
97. 
98. 	if (toolCall.result) {
99. 		const stringifyResult = safeJsonStringify(toolCall.result);
100. 		if (stringifyResult.isOk()) {
101. 			return stringifyResult.value;
102. 		}
103. 		return String(toolCall.result);
104. 	}
105. 
106. 	return null;
107. });
108. </script>

149. 		<div class="text-sm font-medium">Execute Tool Output</div>
150. 	{/snippet}
151. 
152. 	<div class="flex flex-col gap-4">
153. 		<!-- Command Card -->
154. 		<div class="modal-command-card">
155. 			<div class="flex items-center gap-2 border-b border-border bg-muted/30 px-3 py-2">
156. 				<span class="text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground">Command</span>
157. 			</div>

162. 						>{extractedCommand || m.common_command_fallback()}</span
163. 					>

<sub>⏱️ 242m 16s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating tool support options**

I’m thinking about adding optional elapsedLabel support to the header in ExecuteToolUI. If the contract expects it, we might need to update the test instead of simply integrating it. It’s also important to preserve the existing duration label if we implement changes. I may pass elapsedLabel through as a prop to both ExecuteToolUI and ExecuteToolHeader, with explicit props and no spread. I’m also considering how to maintain localization without losing functionality.**Exploring tool execution issues**

I’m looking into the user issue where the actual execute tool isn’t functioning like a subagent, and I suspect the root cause is that the route only uses a thin parser. Instead of swapping the entire UI, I could enhance the parseToolResultWithExitCode or tool-call-execute.svelte to utilize parseToolResultOutput as a fallback when both parsedResult.stdout and stderr are null. This approach could maintain visibility while keeping the existing shared AgentToolExecute. I'll need to investigate how parseToolResultOutput handles specific result shapes that the other function does not.**Considering output handling**

I’m thinking that if the result is a nested JSON string, it might cause the issue where the user sees no command output. This is likely linked to the absence of a route fallback. Enhancing the route or parser seems like the right approach. One idea is to make parseToolResultWithExitCode handle arrays for unified logic, but since parseToolResultOutput already manages that, perhaps I could extend parseToolResultWithExitCode to detect arrays and nested objects effectively.**Searching for bash in Rust tests**

I’m considering searching for "bash" in Rust tests which could involve using `grep` on raw output arrays. I think it might be beneficial to make my searches more targeted. That way, I can find specific content related to bash more effectively without going down unnecessary rabbit holes. I'll focus on refining my approach as I proceed to optimize the search results for the task at hand.*

---

<sub>⏱️ 242m 26s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

I can fix this without swapping the whole component: the shared execute card is fine, but `parseToolResultWithExitCode` currently returns **no stdout at all for array/content-block results**. If the provider sends execute output as MCP-style text blocks, the card gets a command and `null` output. I’m confirming that shape and then I’ll extend the parser so the existing execute UI can display it.

### 💭 Reasoning

***Considering UI component localization**

This seems like a straightforward task about localizing UI components, but there are hardcoded labels in the tool-specific desktop UI. Changing this might affect the text slightly, which could be okay, but I wonder about the reasoning behind using shared components for localization. It's essential to avoid regressions, so perhaps enhancing the current route with a fallback from parseToolResultOutput would be a better option. I'll need to think about this more!**Exploring schema extensions**

I'm considering extending the `parseToolResultOutput` schema to include a `content` array within the object, along with modifications to `parseToolResultWithExitCode`. I wonder if these structures actually occur. It's essential to thoroughly check or search to see if these shapes are present in the current implementation. This could help in making the schema more robust and accommodating for future needs, so I'll look into it further.*

2755.             // encrypted_content stripped

### 💭 Reasoning

***Evaluating tool output handling**

I’m considering changes to improve how we handle tool outputs. A simple fix could be to implement a fallback for parsing results if standard output is missing. I’d want to inspect the tool-definition-registration to see how I can update it similarly. Also, ensuring main session threads reflect accurate outputs while maintaining consistency with the agent panel is key. If needed, I might create tests to validate our parser. Even if the array isn't the main issue, refining it could help resolve the user's problem.**Investigating tool result handling**

I need to confirm if the result from the provider is an object with a `content` array or a raw object without output. It could be useful to check session logs to see if there are entries with execute tool results. I might use tools to search through persisted session data, but I'm cautious about avoiding unnecessary access to the working directory. Since the user wants help with debugging, tauri logs might be helpful, but they could also indicate a bug from the code itself. Adding a test for the parsing fallback might clarify the situation, though it could fail.**Improving parser functionality**

I’m considering adding a test for the build-agent entry to address a bug. First, I need to check how the current parser handles arrays since it seems to return null. This could mean my test will likely fail, which is actually fine because it highlights what needs fixing. I might also implement a parser fallback for arrays. Additionally, I could extend the ToolResultOutputSchema to include nested content objects, enhancing robustness. However, expanding it might not be strictly necessary.*
